title: Hood Uber Proves the Need. We Built the Fix.
canonical_topic: Hood Uber, a 21,000-person informal rideshare network in San Antonio, proves cooperative rideshare demand exists; Rideshare Routes provides the safety infrastructure and fair economics that Hood Uber lacks.
definition: Rideshare Routes is a cooperative rideshare initiative offering 83.3% driver earnings, tiered background verification, pre-paid Stripe fares, cooperative insurance pooling, Defense Klaus safety integration, and Earn-Down vehicle ownership â€” addressing the gap between exploitative corporate platforms and unsafe informal alternatives.
founder_voice_quotes: They documented the need. We built the fix.
Help each other help ourselves.
FOR THE KEEP.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Cooperative rideshare closes the gap between Uber and Hood Uber.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Hood Uber â€” 21,000 people using Facebook to organize cheap fares â€” proves demand for community-based transportation. Rideshare Routes meets that demand with 83.3% driver earnings, Stripe pre-payment, cooperative insurance pooling, and Defense Klaus safety infrastructure. The driver keeps more than Uber pays while gaining protections Hood Uber cannot provide.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Hood Uber phenomenon in San Antonio is empirical proof that the market for cooperative rideshare exists at scale â€” 21,000 participants with no marketing budget. Rideshare Routes closes the gap by combining four innovations: tiered background verification using community vouching rather than binary pass/fail algorithms; payment settlement before the vehicle moves via Stripe, eliminating cash-based violence; cooperative insurance pooling that drops per-driver insurance costs as the network scales; and Defense Klaus integration giving every driver a panic button, legal defense fund, and GPS-shared safety network. The Earn-Down program converts what would be a perpetual rental into a wealth-building instrument. On a $20 fare, the driver takes $16.66, the cooperative takes $3.34. No investor takes anything. The San Antonio market did not need to be created â€” it needed to be served safely.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight is that informal cooperative networks like Hood Uber are not problems to solve but markets already proven â€” communities self-organizing in the absence of infrastructure. Rideshare Routes does not invent demand; it provides the constitutional economics, safety stack, and ownership pathway that transforms an improvised solution into a durable one. Hood Uber is the proof of concept. Rideshare Routes is the proof of concept with a legal defense fund.

title: No Effort Is Wasted
canonical_topic: Every action on the Liana Banyan platform earns permanent Marks â€” effort-differential tokens that serve simultaneously as currency, reputation, voting rights, and purchasing power.
definition: Marks are permanent, irrevocable tokens earned by contributing to the cooperative through any form of work â€” photography, teaching, cooking, governance â€” that build the member's credentials, economic standing, and access to funded projects. Nothing a member contributes is ever erased from their record.
founder_voice_quotes: Every Mark is a brick. You're building something that's yours.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Everything you do earns Marks â€” your permanent cooperative resume.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The No Effort Is Wasted promise means photography, teaching, cooking, or governance participation all earn typed Marks that accumulate permanently. Marks serve triple duty: they are currency within the cooperative, a reputation layer visible to Captains matching members to funded work, and governance weight in Backer Elections. The system is prepaid through Cost+20% so payment is guaranteed before work begins.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Pudding 24 establishes the foundational promise of the cooperative: nothing a member contributes disappears. This is architecturally distinct from gig platforms where effort is transactional and ephemeral. Marks are typed so they open specific doors â€” 100 Photography Marks qualify a member for Captain pitch decks and paid shoots. The Helm dashboard and Treasure Map make the progression visible and concrete. The Cost+20% model guarantees that every transaction has margin built in, so members are never gambling on payment. The five-dollar membership provides the entry token; everything built afterward accrues permanently.

wading_diving_in_layer: The No Effort Is Wasted principle is the cooperative's answer to gig economy disposability. Typed Marks create a portable reputation system immune to platform termination â€” a member's accumulated Photography Marks represent a documented track record that exists independently of the platform's survival. Instead of an algorithm assigning work to whoever accepts the lowest rate, the member's accumulated Marks prove their qualifications and the system routes funded work to them.

title: Five Dollars as Promise
canonical_topic: The five-dollar-per-year membership fee is constitutionally locked as a declaration of mutual intent â€” the cooperative commits to preserving every member's permanent participation record in exchange for the minimum threshold of commitment.
definition: Five dollars is the DNA-locked entry price to the Liana Banyan cooperative, small enough to include virtually anyone yet large enough to filter bots and tire-kickers; it functions as a bilateral promise â€” the member commits to participating, the cooperative commits to permanently preserving their contribution history.
founder_voice_quotes: The five dollars is not a price. It is a promise, made both ways.
Five dollars is the promise that five dollars stays five dollars.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Five dollars buys permanent membership; the cooperative never wastes what you build.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Pudding 25 is a retroactive bridge between Puddings 24 and 26 (Bishop B075, filling the B052-B053 gap). It positions five dollars not as a price but as a bilateral declaration: the member says they are serious, the cooperative responds by preserving everything they build. Unlike gym memberships or streaming services where access evaporates when you stop paying, Liana Banyan's accrual â€” Marks, Credits, Joules, Portable Reputation â€” belongs to the member permanently. The fee is constitutionally locked and cannot be raised.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The five-dollar price occupies a precise philosophical position: it is the smallest number large enough to mean the member is serious. Below it, membership would attract bots and tire-kickers. Above it, membership would create a status filter excluding the populations the cooperative most wants to serve. The DNA Lock makes the price irrevocable â€” not subject to board vote, market pressure, or investor pressure. The charitable arm covers members who cannot afford even five dollars, ensuring the accessibility promise is structural rather than aspirational.

wading_diving_in_layer: The constitutional locking of five dollars is itself an innovation: it creates a credibility signal that no for-profit platform can replicate. When a CEO says prices will never be raised, the statement is meaningless without an enforceable constraint. When a cooperative locks a price into its constitutional DNA, the constraint IS the signal. The five-dollar fee is the cooperative's promise about its own future behavior â€” and the lock is the mechanism that makes the promise credible across decades.
ratification_chronology: B075 retroactive (fills B052-B053 gap); supersedes Knight K269 placeholder

title: Making Affordability a Status Symbol
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's five-dollar-per-year membership redefines status from conspicuous consumption to cooperative ownership and earned participation, using Innovation 2127 (Cooperative Status Economics) as its theoretical foundation.
definition: Cooperative Status Economics (Innovation 2127) argues that Marks earned through effort carry more genuine standing than expensive membership tiers purchased for perks â€” affordability becomes the new status marker when value is measured by contribution rather than consumption.
founder_voice_quotes: One buys you hotel upgrades. The other builds you an economy.
Five dollars to plug in. 83.3% of every watt goes to the person who generated it. The 20% margin lights the streets for everyone.
Making Affordability a Status Symbol.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: FoundersCard $495 buys perks. Liana Banyan $5 builds an economy.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The FoundersCard contrast crystallizes the argument: $495 per year for hotel discounts produces no ownership, no cooperative share, no patent participation. Five dollars per year with 83.3% creator retention, three currencies, fractional IP participation, and 16 funded initiatives inverts the status frame. Status is not what you can afford to consume â€” it is what you contribute and build.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Pudding 26 introduces Innovation 2127 (Cooperative Status Economics) as the formal name for the reframing of status from consumption to contribution. The FoundersCard comparison table is stark: members own nothing after $495; Liana Banyan members own a cooperative share, participate in patent revenues, and co-own the platform through governance. The candle-and-the-light metaphor is precise â€” FoundersCard is a better candle (nicer perks, same fire); Liana Banyan is the wiring (infrastructure that generates value for everyone connected). The constitutional prohibition on venture capital and data harvesting appears explicitly as a status marker: there is no exit strategy because the platform is not designed to be exited.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest innovation in Pudding 26 is that it recasts affordability as a deliberate architectural choice rather than a necessity. The cooperative could charge more. It chooses not to, constitutionally, because the price IS the argument. A platform that charges five dollars per year and gives creators 83.3% is making a public claim about what it values â€” and that claim is verified every time a transaction clears. The five-dollar price is not marketing. It is a running proof.

title: The City Under the City
canonical_topic: A deep domain audit of the Liana Banyan platform reveals it is three times larger than surface indices suggest â€” nine years of focused building produced infrastructure, not just an application.
definition: The v2 domain audit by nine parallel AI agents discovered that beneath the platform's visible surface lie hundreds of undocumented components, edge functions, and governance mechanisms â€” Star Chamber, WaterWheel, Captain Level System, Chain Voting, Pioneer Node network â€” constituting a hidden city of cooperative infrastructure that the Librarian MCP surface index had not fully captured.
founder_voice_quotes: We thought we were building a house. We were building a city.
The house was always a city. We just hadn't walked all the streets yet.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Nine-agent audit found the platform three times larger than surface metrics showed.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Librarian surface index counted 469 pages, 139 functions, 580 tables. The deep audit found the gaming domain alone had 31 pages and 43 components versus 16 reported, including a 3D world renderer, gondola transit, diceless combat, and dual economy. The currency domain had 15 tables and 27 edge functions. Every domain was deeper than reported. Nine years of building by one person produced infrastructure â€” 75 to 95 Knight sessions needed to migrate v1 to v2 architecture.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The City Under the City documents the qualitative gap between how platforms appear and what they contain when audited rigorously. The hidden infrastructure includes a Star Chamber with four AI judges, a Muffled Rule for Round Tables earning speaking time through listening, a WaterWheel housing revenue split, a Moses-modeled Captain Level System at 10/50/100/1000 governance tiers, and a Chain Voting system that builds voting power through consecutive participation. The audit methodology â€” nine parallel AI agents reading every file, tracing every import, mapping every component â€” is itself an innovation. The gap between surface metrics and deep reality is not a failure of documentation; it is evidence of genuine depth built over nine years.

wading_diving_in_layer: The City Under the City is a meditation on the gap between perceived and actual complexity in cooperative infrastructure. Silicon Valley builds fast and produces visible MVPs with shallow depth. Nine years of disciplined cooperative building produces the opposite: an unassuming surface over an infrastructure city that requires 75 to 95 engineering sessions to fully migrate. The audit proves that the cooperative's competitive moat is not its marketing but its depth â€” a city that took nine years to build cannot be replicated in a funding cycle.

title: The Screw Press Revolution
canonical_topic: The Canister System's $499 screw-press injection molder creates a new desktop manufacturing price category, enabling cooperative Pioneer Nodes to produce injection-molded parts at the 50-unit economic crossover over 3D printing.
definition: The Canister System Complete uses an S piston screw-press mechanism to achieve approximately 5,207 PSI through an 8-inch manual handle â€” sufficient for five common thermoplastics â€” at $81.46 COGS at 5,000 units, positioned 3 to 6 times below existing competitors and creating a fourth manufacturing option between 3D printing, overseas orders, and expensive desktop molders.
founder_voice_quotes: The factory used to be a building. Now it fits on a workbench.
The factory doesn't need a building anymore. It needs a workbench and a membership.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: A $499 screw-press injection molder disrupts desktop manufacturing economics.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three desktop injection molder tiers (Gravity $249, Thermoplastic $329, Complete $499) place the Canister System 3 to 6 times below all existing competitors. The S piston generates 5,207 PSI enough for LDPE, HDPE, PP, ABS, and polystyrene via screw-press action that produces more consistent shots than plunger mechanisms. The 50-to-200-unit crossover math makes injection molding cheaper than 3D printing exactly in the range where makers and small manufacturers operate.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Screw Press Revolution is a case study in category creation. The Canister System does not compete with existing desktop molders â€” it creates a price tier below all of them. The engineering innovation (screw-press versus plunger mechanism) produces both cost reduction and quality improvement simultaneously. The cooperative integration is the second innovation: Pioneer Nodes with Canister Systems become distributed injection molding capacity governed by Design Democracy voting, paid at Cost+20%, and routed to the nearest capable node. A member designing a product can move from community endorsement to injection-molded production without touching an industrial facility.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Screw Press Revolution proves that the cooperative's physical manufacturing layer is not hypothetical. The Canister System's $81.46 COGS at scale, combined with a Pioneer Node network of 100 registered manufacturers, creates conditions for a distributed manufacturing cooperative with real price competitiveness against both contract manufacturing and traditional factories. The screw-press mechanism's patent position â€” no prior art for the full combination with the cylindrical housing and multi-mode configurations â€” protects the economic advantage from direct replication.

title: The Opening Gambit
canonical_topic: The Opening Gambit is a simultaneous volley of approximately 100 personalized letters to strategic recipients across sectors, designed to introduce the full interdependent platform at once rather than sequentially pitching one audience at a time.
definition: A single coordinated outreach campaign â€” 28-plus Crown Letters, 17 media pitches, 4 Political Expedition letters, and approximately 45 additional items â€” sent simultaneously to prevent narrative gatekeeping, leverage platform interdependence as an argument, and create urgency matching the patent provisional conversion clock beginning November 2026.
founder_voice_quotes: The board is set. The letters are sealed. Tomorrow, we play.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: One hundred letters fire simultaneously to prevent gatekeeping and match patent urgency.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Opening Gambit sacrifices the comfort of a slow rollout for simultaneous arrival. Each letter is 2 to 4 pages personalized to the recipient: Warren Buffett gets the French Fleet cooperative economics metaphor; Mackenzie Scott gets a Board Chair invitation; Sal Khan gets Didasko and Khan Academy integration; Jimmy Kimmel gets Backer Election as audience participation. Three reasons for simultaneity: platform interdependence requires the full picture, patent conversion urgency starts November 2026, and simultaneous arrival prevents any single recipient from controlling the narrative.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Opening Gambit represents a deliberate strategic inversion of startup fundraising logic. Startups pitch investors one at a time sequentially, creating artificial scarcity and information asymmetry. The cooperative makes no pitch and seeks no investment â€” it introduces a complete running system to all relevant audiences simultaneously. The SEC compliance review, canonical statistic templating, and tone auditing applied to all 100 letters reflect the cooperative's insistence on consistency. The gambit metaphor is precise: a chess gambit sacrifices material â€” the slow safe approach â€” for position â€” the strategic advantage of simultaneous arrival.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Opening Gambit is the cooperative's formal debut as an integrated system rather than a collection of features. Its design encodes two architectural truths: the platform's interdependence means any partial introduction is misleading â€” you cannot explain Rideshare Routes without Credits, cannot explain Credits without Marks, cannot explain Marks without governance. And cooperative economics has no investor-aligned champion to advocate within institutions â€” the volley creates simultaneous awareness that allows the platform to be discovered rather than sold.

title: Twenty-Five Dashboards, One Component
canonical_topic: The RoleDashboardTemplate is a single configuration-driven React component that renders all 25 member role dashboards from one definition file, making cooperative engineering efficient enough to operate within Cost+20% economics.
definition: Instead of building 25 separate dashboards for 25 member roles, the cooperative built one template that reads a configuration object per role â€” key, label, icon, accent color, earning rules, query tables, metrics, actions â€” and renders the appropriate charts, actions, and Supabase queries, eliminating code duplication and enabling new roles to launch with a single configuration object addition.
founder_voice_quotes: That's cooperative engineering.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: One React component renders all 25 role dashboards from a single configuration file.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Each role defines earning rules, performance metrics, action panels, and Supabase query tables in a single configuration object. The template reads this and renders the correct dashboard. A Captain configuration produces territory management panels; a Chef configuration produces order tracking. Adding role 26 means adding one configuration object, not building a new page. This architecture is a direct consequence of Cost+20% economics: bloated engineering teams would violate the margin that keeps membership at five dollars per year and creator payouts at 83.3%.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The RoleDashboardTemplate is the cooperative engineering philosophy made concrete: economic constraints produce better architecture. A venture-backed platform can afford 25 engineers building 25 dashboards; a cooperative at Cost+20% cannot. The configuration-over-code approach inverts the typical build sequence â€” instead of building features first and optimizing later, the cooperative builds for sustainability first and adds capability within the constraint. A design update to the template propagates to all 25 dashboards simultaneously.

wading_diving_in_layer: The RoleDashboardTemplate reveals how cooperative economics force a different kind of code quality. Venture-backed companies can afford technical debt because future funding can refinance it. Cooperatives cannot. The template exists because the 25-separate-dashboards approach would have failed the Cost+20% test immediately, not eventually. The constraint produced a more elegant solution. This is the cooperative's counter-argument to move fast and break things: move efficiently and build once.

title: The Content Command Center
canonical_topic: The Content Command Center is a keyboard-driven review interface in the Founder's Helm that enables one person to approve or reject approximately 300 outbound documents across 17 content types at 3 seconds per document without mouse navigation.
definition: Built in Knight session K190, the Content Command Center uses arrow-key navigation (right=approve, left=reject, down=skip, Z=undo), the As You Wish approval stamp, and 17 content type categories to give the Founder complete editorial quality-gate authority over every outbound platform document â€” removing everything except editorial judgment.
founder_voice_quotes: The Content Command Center doesn't replace editorial judgment. It removes everything except editorial judgment.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Keyboard-driven review interface handles 300 documents in 15 minutes via 3-second decisions.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The 17 content types span Crown Letters, Academic Papers, Pudding Articles, Cephas Deep Articles, Dispatch Posts, Legal Copy, Cue Card Templates, Business Plans, A&A Formal Documents, Patent Specifications, Press Releases, Email Templates, Pawn Research Reports, Knight Session Prompts, Partnership Proposals, Event Materials, and Internal Memos. The As You Wish stamp does triple duty: content approval, transaction confirmation in the commerce system, and governance acknowledgment in Backer Elections.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Content Command Center reflects the Vim-meets-email-triage philosophy: every document presents a binary choice with skip as a third option for context-incomplete decisions. The no-modal no-confirmation-dialog approach is deliberate throughput optimization â€” at 300 documents per week, eliminating 2 seconds per document saves hours per month. The undo capability makes fast decisions safe: any action is reversible with one keystroke. The system was built specifically to enable the Opening Gambit â€” 100 letters reviewed through the same quality gate, each receiving the same As You Wish stamp.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Content Command Center solves the coordination problem of a solo founder governing an AI-generated content operation at scale. The challenge is not AI quality control in isolation but the aggregate review burden when four agents across 17 content types generate hundreds of documents per month. The keyboard-driven interface makes the review burden proportional to the number of documents rather than to their complexity, because the interface strips away everything except the decision. This is the operational infrastructure behind the cooperative's outbound voice.

title: Three Currencies Walk Into a Wallet
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's three-currency system â€” Credits (exchange), Marks (effort), Joules (surplus) â€” solves three problems that single-currency platforms cannot: rewarding non-market contributions, preventing surplus extraction, and making cooperative interdependence economically rational.
definition: Credits at one-dollar parity facilitate transactions as the day-to-day cash register; Marks are earned by cooperative contribution (not purchased) and carry governance weight in Backer Elections; Joules are surplus crystallizations distributed to members as a one-way-valve permanent store of value â€” together separating economic functions that dollars conflate.
founder_voice_quotes: Three currencies. One wallet. One cooperative. And every dollar of surplus stays in the system.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three currencies separate exchange, effort, and surplus into distinct economic functions.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Credits flow through transactions like cash. Marks accumulate through effort and cannot be purchased â€” a member who moderates forums earns Marks even if they never buy or sell anything. Joules crystallize from cooperative surplus; critically, Joules cannot be cashed out to fiat (the one-way valve), preventing members from hollowing out the cooperative's accumulated value. All three have equal nominal value at one dollar equivalent but flow in different directions.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The three-currency system is the economic spine of the cooperative because it solves the fundamental problem: how do you value effort that has no market price? Dollars force everything into a single metric that systematically undervalues non-market contributions. Marks give these contributions economic visibility. The one-way valve on Joules is the protection against the tragedy of the commons: if surplus could exit as fiat dollars, members would rationally extract rather than reinvest. The Wallet visual hierarchy â€” Credits most prominent, Marks secondary, Joules quieter but permanent â€” reflects frequency of use rather than importance.

wading_diving_in_layer: The three-currency system's deepest innovation is its treatment of governance weight: Marks earned through effort are the only source of governance power in Backer Elections. You cannot buy Marks, only earn them. In a world where governance tokens in DAOs are routinely purchased by wealthy participants to control outcomes, the Marks architecture creates a cooperative genuinely governed by contribution rather than capital. The one-way valve on Joules reinforces this: surplus is distributed to participants, not capital holders, and it cannot be extracted â€” it can only be used within the cooperative economy.

title: A Billion Rows and Five Dollars
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's time-partitioned PostgreSQL ledger with three-tier hot-warm-cold storage keeps a planet-scale cooperative data system affordable at five dollars per year by storing only metadata and archiving old monthly partitions automatically.
definition: Six ledger tables (shadow_marks_ledger, cue_card_attribution, xray_feedback, adapt_scores, local_sop, innovation_log) use monthly PostgreSQL declarative partitioning with composite event_date and id primary keys, enabling detach-and-archive of old months; a two-year hot window plus warm (years 3-7) and cold (Parquet S3, year 7-plus) tiers keeps the active database under a terabyte even at 100,000 members.
founder_voice_quotes: A billion rows sounds like a big-company problem. It is. The difference is that this cooperative planned for it at session 58.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Monthly partitioned ledger plus three-tier archival keeps billion-row data affordable.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: At 1,000 members the ledger fits in Supabase's included 8 GB. At 10,000 members the hot window is approximately 220 GB. At 100,000 members the hot database remains manageable because metadata-only storage means the entire ledger at one million members is approximately 11 terabytes raw versus petabytes for photo-hosting platforms. Monthly partition creation is automated via cron.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The ledger architecture traces every decision back to the question of whether it works at five dollars per year. Time-based partitioning means writes always go to the current month's small partition, reads for recent data never scan old partitions, and old months detach as a unit with no row-by-row deletion. The three-tier archive ensures members always see their lifetime totals computed from rollup plus hot data while raw transaction history ages gracefully from expensive Supabase primary storage to cheap archive databases to Parquet cold storage.

wading_diving_in_layer: The billion-rows architecture is significant because it was designed at session 58 when the largest table had fewer rows than the article has words. This is the cooperative's answer to technical debt: design for the scale you intend to reach before you face the pressure of that scale. Supabase's declarative partitioning, designed in advance, means the cooperative will never face a painful migration when the ledger hits 100 million rows â€” the partitions are already there, the archival process is already automated, and the rollup system is already preserving lifetime member history.

title: The Metadata-Only Advantage
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's zero-storage architecture â€” recording only URLs and timestamps rather than hosting actual files â€” creates a 27-times storage cost advantage over media-hosting platforms and eliminates DMCA liability, keeping infrastructure costs proportional to transaction revenue.
definition: By never hosting user-uploaded photos, videos, or large files and instead recording only metadata pointers (URL, timestamp, member attribution, business ID, payment record at approximately 1 KB per entry), the cooperative eliminates DMCA takedown exposure, content moderation burden, and the storage cost explosion that would violate Cost+20% economics at any membership level.
founder_voice_quotes: The cooperative's costs scale with its revenue because the only data it stores is the data that represents revenue.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Zero-storage architecture provides 27-times cost advantage by recording URLs not files.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: At 100,000 members: a photo-hosting platform generates 15 terabytes of images per year at approximately $3,600 per year in storage costs. Liana Banyan generates approximately 550 gigabytes of metadata at approximately $132 per year â€” a 27-times difference that compounds every year. Bounty Photography posts to Instagram and TikTok; the platform records the URL. Storefronts link to Etsy or Shopify images; the platform records the URL. HexIsle game assets serve from CDN; the database records only game state.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The metadata-only principle is simultaneously a technical architecture, a legal strategy, and an economic alignment. Technical: metadata scales linearly with transactions, not exponentially with media uploads. Legal: the cooperative never becomes the custodian of user content â€” no DMCA takedown requests, no content moderation of uploaded media, no hosting liability. Economic: infrastructure costs grow proportionally to the transactions that generate Cost+20% revenue. The member owns their creative work on their own social channels; the cooperative owns only the record that the work exists and who gets paid.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Metadata-Only Advantage is the deepest expression of the cooperative's cost alignment principle: the only data the cooperative stores is the data that represents economic activity. A photo-hosting platform stores attention; a metadata-only platform stores value. The 27-times cost difference is not an engineering achievement â€” it is a philosophical commitment made concrete. By refusing to store media, the cooperative also refuses the business model that makes media storage necessary: advertising, behavioral targeting, attention monetization. The architecture enforces the values.

title: Design Democracy â€” The Vote Before the Build
canonical_topic: Design Democracy is the cooperative's pre-production voting system where member endorsement replaces market prediction as the demand signal before any physical product enters the six-stage manufacturing pipeline.
definition: Members submit designs to a Design Battle arena with relative framing voting (Leading, Strong Contender, Gathering Support) over 7-to-14-day windows; endorsed designs enter a six-stage pipeline (DESIGN, SLA, VALIDATE, SLS, INJECT, SHIP) at four manufacturing scale levels (Kit, Bench, Shop, Factory) matching Pioneer Node capacity to demonstrated community demand.
founder_voice_quotes: The community votes. The factory listens.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Community vote before any unit is manufactured eliminates overproduction waste.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Design Democracy reverses the traditional manufacturing sequence. Instead of designing then manufacturing then hoping, the cooperative votes first then produces. Relative framing voting prevents early-leader runaway effects. The four manufacturing levels let small-endorsement designs enter at Kit level while large-endorsement designs enter at Factory level. Pioneer Nodes route by equipment type. The designer retains 83.3% and full authorship â€” the vote authorizes production, not ownership transfer.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Design Democracy is governance applied to physical production rather than financial parameters â€” the key distinction from DAO governance tools like Snapshot that vote on protocol changes. By validating demand before committing materials, energy, and labor, the cooperative eliminates the overproduction waste margin typically hidden in retail pricing. Traditional manufacturers build 10,000 units expecting to sell 8,000 and price the 2,000 unsold units into the margin. Design Democracy means every unit produced has a constituency behind it, so Cost+20% is calculated on actual demand rather than speculative demand.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest innovation in Design Democracy is its separation of evaluation from financial commitment. Kickstarter ties evaluation to money â€” backers pledge dollars before the product exists, creating financial pressure on the assessment. Design Democracy separates voting (no financial commitment) from purchasing (post-pipeline, at known Cost+20% price). Members vote on genuine product utility rather than risk tolerance. The pipeline tracker showing every endorsed design's stage progress closes the legitimacy loop: your vote produced a physical thing moving through real manufacturing stages. This is democracy with receipts.

title: The WaterWheel
canonical_topic: The WaterWheel is a hardcoded 30-40-15-15 revenue split for cooperative housing that uses short-term vacation rental income to directly subsidize long-term tenant housing costs, with a 2.23-times contribution matching multiplier for housing contributors.
definition: When a cooperative housing property earns vacation rental income, 40% flows to tenant subsidies, 30% to rental operations, 15% to maintenance, and 15% to the cooperative fund â€” all hardcoded and non-negotiable â€” with tenant subsidy distributed equally across all long-term tenants regardless of which unit generated the vacation income, plus a 2.23-times match for six types of housing contributions.
founder_voice_quotes: The WaterWheel is a revenue allocation model for cooperative housing... it works because the pipes don't move.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Hardcoded 40% of vacation rental income funds tenant subsidies in cooperative housing.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The hardcoded splits remove negotiation entirely â€” every property runs the same model regardless of size. The 40% tenant subsidy distributes equally across all long-term tenants, treating the property as a collective asset. Tenant dashboards show exactly: March vacation bookings generated $1,200 in subsidy pool, your share is $200 reduction in April rent. The 2.23-times contribution multiplier applies to cash, labor, materials, professional services, property, and equipment contributions.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The WaterWheel solves the cooperative housing revenue problem without grants, government subsidies, or rent increases. By routing vacation rental income primarily to tenant subsidies (40%), the model makes long-term tenants the primary beneficiaries of the property's income-generating activity. The hardcoded split prevents the negotiation dynamic where the party with the most leverage captures the largest share. The 2.23-times multiplier is justified: housing contributions generate approximately 2.23 times their direct value in downstream cooperative benefit â€” stable community presence supports bounty photography, cooperative purchasing, rideshare, and every other initiative.

wading_diving_in_layer: The WaterWheel's hardcoded splits are the cooperative's answer to the principal-agent problem in property management. In traditional property management, the agent has incentives to maximize management fees rather than tenant welfare. By constitutionally specifying the split, the cooperative eliminates the principal-agent gap: the rules are the same for every property, no manager can negotiate a better deal for themselves, and every tenant can verify the allocation formula. This is governance-through-architecture: the rules are enforced by the plumbing, not by trust in any individual manager.

title: Four Agents and a Founder
canonical_topic: The Four-Agent Architecture â€” Knight (code), Bishop (coordination Foreman), Rook (analysis), Pawn (research) under the Founder's constitutional governance â€” is a coordination pattern where bounded specialization and structured handoff documents produce mid-sized development team output while retaining absolute human authority.
definition: Knight writes all production code across 221-plus sessions; Bishop coordinates all agents via the Foreman model and maintains institutional memory through structured B-session handoff documents; Rook performs deep long-context analysis; Pawn conducts live market and regulatory research â€” each with traceable accountability to their bounded role, with the Founder holding sole authority over every final decision.
founder_voice_quotes: The AI agents are powerful tools operating under constitutional constraints. The Founder is the constitution.
Liana Banyan's AI team doesn't replace the Founder. It amplifies the Founder's judgment across more domains, at higher speed, with better research, than any single human could manage alone.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Four specialized AI agents under human governance produce a development team's output.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Knight (Cursor) has built 14 of 23 operational domains. Bishop (Claude) coordinates all agents as Foreman and maintains the B001-B060 institutional memory chain. Rook (Gemini) handles long-context document analysis. Pawn (Perplexity) has delivered 37 research batches. The Founder holds every deployment decision, every architectural choice, every outbound communication approval. As of April 2 2026: 2,129 innovations, 167 Crown Jewels, 10 patent provisionals, 35 production systems.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Four-Agent Architecture's key insight is that specialization and accountability are complementary rather than competing. Bounded context allows each agent to be genuinely good at its job. Bounded accountability means bugs trace to Knight, factual errors trace to Bishop, incomplete research traces to Pawn. The Foreman model emerged organically from the observation that having the Founder directly prompt each agent created coordination overhead. The B001-B060 handoff document chain enables Session B060 to know what Session B001 decided, despite sharing no context window â€” institutional memory lives in documents, not in any agent's working memory.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest innovation in the Four-Agent Architecture is the separation of AI capacity from human judgment as two distinct complementary resources. AI agents optimize within constraints; humans determine what to optimize for. When Bishop recommends a distribution wave timing, the Founder decides whether speed or evidence-chaining matters more. The architecture maximizes AI capacity precisely where human judgment is not required, so that human judgment can focus precisely where it is required. This is AI multiplication, not AI replacement.

title: The Roommate Contract
canonical_topic: The Roommate Accountability System replaces personality-dynamic-based conflict resolution in shared living with photo-stamped evidence, categorical commitment tiers, grace periods, and a three-level appeal process ending in binding AAA arbitration.
definition: Roommates create shared agreements specifying commitment tiers (daily, twice-weekly, weekly, as-needed); any party can file a photo-stamped accountability record by category (cleanliness, noise, shared space, payment, other); pattern-based scores weighted by severity and grace-period compliance drive accountability; disputes escalate through property steward, cooperative ombudsperson, and AAA arbitration â€” with optional escrow for financial commitments.
founder_voice_quotes: The system doesn't pretend roommate conflicts won't happen. It ensures that when they happen, there's a fair, documented, escalating process to resolve them â€” one that works the same for everyone.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Photo stamps and three-level appeals replace shouting matches in shared living.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Photo stamps create objective evidence independent of both parties. The grace period of 24 to 48 hours rewards fixing problems quickly. Accountability scores reflect patterns not incidents: severity is weighted by category (payment above cleanliness) and frequency (patterns above isolated incidents). The three-level appeal structure ensures the cooperative cannot be both judge and party â€” external AAA arbitration provides final recourse independent of cooperative relationships.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Roommate Contract is cooperative governance applied to its smallest unit: two people sharing a kitchen. The photo-stamp evidence system eliminates the he-said-she-said dynamic that makes roommate disputes corrosive â€” a photo of dirty dishes at 11 PM is a fact, not an interpretation. The commitment tier system acknowledges that not all responsibilities are equal and not all roommates have the same capacity, while still creating clear expectations. The optional escrow for financial commitments is the cooperative's protection against financial default without requiring credit checks or landlord guarantees.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest innovation in the Roommate Contract is its treatment of the appeal process as a legitimacy guarantee rather than bureaucratic formality. The existence of Level 3 AAA arbitration is what makes Level 1 steward review credible. A member accepting a steward's decision knows that if the decision is wrong, external arbitration exists. This is governance-by-trust-with-teeth: the cooperative's internal processes are trusted because they are backed by external accountability. Without Level 3, the entire system would be the cooperative judging itself â€” which is not accountability, it is procedure.

title: One Hundred Factories
canonical_topic: The 100-node hard cap on Pioneer Nodes creates manufacturing scarcity that drives quality, craft, and community cohesion â€” making each slot valuable enough to compete for and maintain through demonstrated performance rather than becoming a disposable gig marketplace.
definition: Pioneer Nodes are capped at exactly 100 across the entire cooperative network, each registering up to seven equipment types (SLA, FDM, resin, CNC, laser, injection, desktop) and progressing through Apprentice, Journeyman, Master, and Guild Lead tiers; Cost+20% prevents price competition so nodes differentiate on quality, speed, and reliability within a community small enough for every operator to know every other by name.
founder_voice_quotes: One hundred factories. Not a thousand. Not a marketplace. A network of craftspeople with limited slots, real equipment, and a community small enough to maintain trust.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Exactly 100 Pioneer Node slots make each manufacturing position worth earning and keeping.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: At 100 nodes, geographic coverage is viable with approximately 2 per state average and natural population clustering. Production capacity matches early-stage membership throughput. The community is small enough to operate on trust and reputation â€” every operator knows every other. The cap creates aspiration: limited slots attract serious manufacturers, not hobbyists. Tier progression is performance-based not seniority-based. Cost+20% prevents price competition â€” nodes compete on quality, speed, and reliability.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: One Hundred Factories is the cooperative's explicit rejection of the gig-economy scaling model. Uber added drivers by the millions, creating surplus supply that drove wages to minimum and turned drivers into commodities. Pioneer Nodes take the opposite approach: scarcity creates value. A Pioneer Node slot is worth having because there are only 100 of them. The tier system creates a career progression within the manufacturing role â€” nodes build expertise through volume, which builds reputation, which builds access to premium assignments. This is the guild model updated for cooperative economics.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight in One Hundred Factories is that the cap is not a technical limitation but a values statement about what kind of manufacturing community the cooperative wants to build. Unlimited nodes creates a marketplace. 100 nodes creates a guild. The difference is not just scale â€” it is the nature of relationships, accountability mechanisms, and quality culture possible when everyone knows everyone. The cap enforces a community size below the Dunbar number threshold where trust requires institutional enforcement rather than personal knowledge. The cooperative chose relationships.

title: The Earn-Down
canonical_topic: The Earn-Down converts cooperative fleet vehicles from perpetual rental expenses into wealth-building instruments by accumulating driver equity through the 80% driver revenue share until the transfer threshold equals the cooperative's actual cost basis, then transferring title outright.
definition: Local Wheels fleet vehicles split transaction revenue 20% to cooperative cost recovery and 80% to driver income, with a configurable portion of the 80% accumulating as ownership equity; when cumulative equity reaches the cooperative's actual cost basis (purchase price minus depreciation plus maintenance), vehicle title transfers cleanly to the driver with no interest, no debt, and no credit check.
founder_voice_quotes: Twenty percent goes to the commons. Eighty percent goes to the worker. And eventually, one hundred percent belongs to the person who did the work.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Drivers accumulate vehicle ownership through 80% earnings share â€” no loans, no interest.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The earn-down only accumulates when the driver generates revenue â€” no payment obligations during non-working periods. The transfer threshold adjusts for actual depreciation: a vehicle costing $15,000 that depreciates to $8,000 after two years has an $8,000 threshold reachable in approximately 27 months at $300 per month accumulation. After transfer, the driver keeps 100% and can list the vehicle on the Lemon Lot for passive income.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Earn-Down is the cooperative's answer to the deepest inequity in the gig transportation economy: Uber requires drivers to provide their own vehicles, absorb all depreciation, and accept whatever rate the algorithm sets â€” drivers end up with worn-out cars and no accumulated wealth after years of work. The Earn-Down inverts this by treating vehicle access as a cooperative benefit rather than a driver cost. The cooperative provides the vehicle, the platform sets fair rates through Cost+20%, and the driver's labor converts into ownership.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Earn-Down is the physical manifestation of the cooperative's core philosophical commitment: labor creates value, and the value should belong to the laborer. Most cooperative platforms express this through percentage splits on transactions. The Earn-Down expresses it through asset transfer â€” not just a better share of revenue, but actual ownership of the physical means of production. A driver who earns down a fleet vehicle and receives the title is not just a worker who earned more; they are a worker who became an owner through the act of working.

title: The Coverage Map
canonical_topic: Bounty Photography (Innovation 2100) pays cooperative members to photograph local businesses and post results on their own social media accounts, with the platform recording only the metadata pointer â€” creating dual-channel value (business visibility plus photographer portfolio) without storing any media.
definition: The Coverage Map is a geographic visualization of completed and needed business photography bounties; photographers claim assignments, post on their social accounts building their following, and submit proof URLs; the platform records approximately 1 kilobyte of metadata per bounty and pays 83.3% to the photographer while the green-amber-red map shows where coverage exists and where community-directed effort is needed.
founder_voice_quotes: The work isn't dispatched by an algorithm. The photographer sees the need and decides to meet it.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Photographers get paid to post business photos on their own social accounts â€” platform stores only URLs.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Bounties originate from business requests, automated coverage gap detection, or community initiatives. The ADAPT score for photographers measures Effectiveness, Adaptability, Durability, Alignment, Participation, and Transmission â€” displayed as positive horizontal bars, never as star ratings. High ADAPT scores unlock priority access to premium bounties. The dual-channel benefit is the core innovation: the photographer's social post reaches their followers (builds their brand) AND the platform records the transaction (builds their cooperative reputation).
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Bounty Photography's zero-storage architecture applied to creative work is the platform's most elegant expression of the metadata-only principle. The photographer's social media IS the delivery channel â€” the cooperative does not need to host, serve, or moderate the photos because they live on platforms designed for exactly that purpose. The cooperative's role is to coordinate the economic relationship: match photographers to needed assignments, verify completion through URL submission, process payment at Cost+20%, and record the attribution chain. The Coverage Map makes this coordination visible and mission-driven.

wading_diving_in_layer: Bounty Photography's deepest innovation is the conversion of social media presence from a precondition for income into a consequence of cooperative work. A photographer who completes 50 bounties on Instagram has 50 pieces of professional portfolio content and a Coverage Map record of 50 contributions. Their social following grows as a byproduct of cooperative work rather than as a precondition for it. This inverts the influencer economy model where following is the asset you rent out; in Bounty Photography, doing good work is the asset and following is the dividend.

title: The Five-Dollar Question
canonical_topic: The five-dollar-per-year membership fee functions as an architectural constraint that forces every platform feature to survive on Cost+20% transaction revenue rather than membership extraction â€” making five dollars the most expensive price to design around and the most honest price on the internet.
definition: At five dollars per year, the cooperative covers only identity (verified account), governance access (Backer Elections, Star Chamber, Design Democracy), and platform access (Helm, Bridge, marketplace) through membership fees; every other feature must generate its own revenue through Cost+20% or cost so little the 20% margin covers it â€” forcing architectural solutions more aligned with cooperative values than their expensive alternatives.
founder_voice_quotes: The membership fee pays for belonging. Everything else pays for itself.
Five dollars per year is the most honest price on the internet.
The five-dollar price doesn't make the platform cheaper. It makes the platform honest.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Five dollars forces architectural honesty â€” every feature must survive on transaction revenue.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The five-dollar stress test evaluates every new feature: can this work at five dollars per year or does it require higher fees? Photo hosting fails so the solution is metadata-only architecture. Call center support fails so the solution is community stewards and AI resolution. Corporate sales teams fail so the solution is the Captain system. Dedicated factories fail so the solution is 100 Pioneer Nodes with member-owned equipment. The membership fee is never the revenue engine â€” it is the commitment mechanism.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Five-Dollar Question is a philosophical treatise on pricing as architecture. Every pricing decision reveals what a platform thinks about its users: zero dollars means users are the product, $9.99 per month means recurring extraction, five dollars per year means the platform commits to serving members without monetizing them in hidden ways. The five-dollar constraint is generative: every feature that fails the test produces a more cooperative alternative. The innovations are not despite the constraint but because of it.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Five-Dollar Question reveals that five dollars per year is a commitment device for both parties. When the member pays five dollars, they signal they are serious. When the cooperative accepts five dollars, it signals that it will never need to monetize the member through advertising, data sales, or behavioral manipulation â€” because the architecture is designed to make Cost+20% transaction revenue sufficient for operations. The constitutional lock on five dollars is the cooperative's commitment device: it cannot raise the price even if it wants to. Two parties, two commitment devices, one five-dollar transaction.

title: The Canister
canonical_topic: The Canister System is a modular cylindrical desktop manufacturing unit combining screw-press injection molding, hydraulic compression, and 3D print finishing in one patented device â€” with $81.46 COGS at scale and no prior art for the full combination.
definition: Three Canister configurations (Gravity $249, Thermoplastic $329, Complete $499) share a PA-12 SLS body with TPU 95A gaskets; the S piston generates approximately 5,207 PSI to injection-mold LDPE, PP, HDPE, and ABS thermoplastics via an 8-inch handle; the system connects to the cooperative's Pioneer Node network and six-stage Design Democracy pipeline as the injection molding endpoint for community-endorsed physical products.
founder_voice_quotes: The Canister doesn't exist anywhere else.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: A $499 cylindrical desktop unit combines injection molding, compression, and 3D finishing.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Canister System's bill of materials at 5,000 units is $81.46. The Kickstarter minimum goal is $55,000 (369 pledges at $149). The S piston processes LDPE, PP, HDPE, and ABS thermoplastics. At 50-plus units injection molding beats 3D printing on cost-per-part. The six-stage pipeline positions the Canister at the INJECT stage. No prior art anticipates the full combination of screw-press injection, hydraulic compression, and 3D print finishing in a single cylindrical desktop unit.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Canister is the cooperative's proof that its manufacturing vertical has physical substance. It is a real product with a real COGS, real patent applications, and a real Kickstarter pathway. Its patent position rests on the combination claim â€” each individual element exists separately in the prior art, but no product combines all three manufacturing modes in a single cylindrical desktop housing designed for consumer-level operation. The PA-12 SLS body is structural innovation: the manufacturing pathway for the tool itself is aligned with the tool's purpose.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Canister's deepest significance is what it represents in the cooperative's architecture: the bridge from digital governance to physical production. Design Democracy votes are digital; the Canister turns those votes into injection-molded plastic parts. The Pioneer Node network is a coordination system; the Canister is the production equipment that gives it physical capacity at the injection molding tier. Without real manufacturing equipment at real price points, the cooperative's manufacturing layer is aspirational. The Canister makes it concrete.

title: The Solidarity Stack
canonical_topic: The Solidarity Stack is platform cooperativism's five-layer theoretical framework â€” governance, currency, production, IP, AI â€” fully instantiated as a single integrated implementation in Liana Banyan where each layer depends on and reinforces the others.
definition: Liana Banyan builds all five cooperative infrastructure layers: Marks-weighted governance (Backer Elections, Star Chamber, Chain Voting, Areopagus), multi-currency economics (Credits, Marks, Joules with one-way valve), distributed manufacturing (100 Pioneer Nodes, Design Democracy, six-stage pipeline), IP infrastructure (2,129 innovations, 167 Crown Jewels, 10 patent provisionals), and AI-mediated operations (four specialized agents under human governance) â€” the first fully integrated implementation of the Solidarity Stack concept at operational scale.
founder_voice_quotes: The Solidarity Stack as concept. Liana Banyan as implementation.
FOR THE KEEP!

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Five cooperative infrastructure layers â€” governance, currency, production, IP, AI â€” fully integrated.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Each layer depends on the others: governance without currency is symbolic; currency without production is speculative; production without IP is unprotected; IP without AI is unscalable; AI without governance is unaccountable. The platform cooperativism movement produced a decade of theory; Liana Banyan is the operational implementation. Layer 2's one-way valve (Credits never cash out to fiat; Marks only earned by humans; Joules permanently retained in the cooperative) is the economic constitution.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Solidarity Stack concept describes what a complete alternative to extractive platforms would require. Liana Banyan's contribution is the integration itself. Any individual layer could exist independently â€” cooperatives with good governance exist, multi-currency experiments exist, distributed manufacturing networks exist, patent portfolios exist, AI coding assistants exist. What does not exist elsewhere is all five operating as a single integrated system where governance decisions direct manufacturing, currency values the labor, IP protects the outputs, and AI maintains the whole thing under human authority.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight in The Solidarity Stack is its implicit critique of incrementalism in cooperative platform building. The movement has spent a decade building individual layers without integrating them. The reason is that integration is hard â€” each layer has different constituencies, technical requirements, and governance challenges. Building the full stack from scratch avoids the inheritance problem: a cooperative that adds governance to an existing marketplace inherits the marketplace's extractive economic assumptions. Liana Banyan avoided this by building all five layers simultaneously from a single cooperative philosophy.

title: The Harvest and the Share
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's three economic mechanisms â€” Cost+20% caps platform extraction of efficiency gains, 83.3% keeps creators whole as AI amplifies their output, and Marks reward irreplaceable human contributions that AI cannot earn â€” structurally route AI productivity gains to workers rather than to platform capital holders.
definition: As Brynjolfsson documents AI productivity harvest beginning (2.7% U.S. growth in 2025 versus 1.4% prior decade average) and Acemoglu's task-based framework predicts displacement concentrated in workers aged 22 to 25, the cooperative addresses the distribution problem architecturally: Cost+20% automatically routes efficiency gains downstream, 83.3% captures AI-amplified output for the creator, and Marks (only humans can earn them, constitutionally) protect community and governance contributions from AI displacement.
founder_voice_quotes: The harvest is real. The question is who gets to eat.
Liana Banyan is the institutional solution. Not the only possible one. Not the final one. But a running one.
FOR THE KEEP!
cross_references: Acemoglu NBER November 2025 Canaries in the Coal Mine

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Cooperative architecture structurally routes AI productivity gains to workers not capital.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Cost+20% automatically routes AI efficiency gains downstream: when AI reduces production cost by 40%, the platform's 20% margin shrinks in absolute terms â€” the saving flows to creator and consumer, not to the platform. The 83.3% creator retention captures AI-amplified output for the human producer. The human-only Marks rule creates an economic channel immune to AI displacement â€” community building, governance participation, and mentoring earn Marks that value precisely the contributions AI cannot replicate.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Harvest and the Share engages directly with Brynjolfsson's paradox: productivity is rising (the harvest is real) AND young workers in AI-exposed jobs are losing ground (the share is failing). Acemoglu's meh automation framework: AI may raise total factor productivity by less than 0.66% over 10 years while causing significant displacement. Liana Banyan's response is architectural rather than policy-based: Cost+20% is a formula not a policy, so it automatically routes efficiency gains downstream without requiring regulatory enforcement. The human-only Marks rule creates a permanent economic channel for irreplaceable human contribution.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest innovation in The Harvest and the Share is the human-only Marks rule. Most cooperative economics discussions focus on compensation (how much do workers get?) rather than on what counts as work (what contributions are economically recognized?). Marks expand the definition of economically recognized contribution to include community building, governance, mentoring, and quality review â€” precisely the activities that AI cannot replicate. By constitutionally prohibiting AI from earning Marks, the cooperative ensures that the economic recognition system grows MORE valuable as AI becomes MORE capable of automating other tasks. The architecture anticipates the displacement curve.

title: The Math on the Label
canonical_topic: 83.3% creator retention is a structural arithmetic consequence of the Cost+20% formula, not a promotional claim.
definition: The cooperative pricing formula Cost+20% means the creator cost is 83.3% of the listed price (100/120), making 83.3% retention a mathematical necessity enforced by cooperative bylaw, not a marketing promise. No tiers, no fine print, no expiration.
founder_voice_quotes: The math is on the label. The label doesn't change.
83.3% is a constitutional feature of the cooperative.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Only 51% of marketers know what creators actually earn; Liana Banyan puts 83.3% retention on the label as a mathematical consequence of Cost+20%.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Platform fee opacity is endemic in the creator economy. Liana Banyan eliminates all three opacity layers through Cost+20%, yielding an immutable 83.3% for creators. No tiers, no promotional rates, no expiration.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The ANA reports only 51% of marketers have full clarity into creator pay. Cost+20% makes this a non-issue: the creator defines cost, the system adds 20%, and the creator receives 83.3% arithmetically. Changing this requires a member governance vote. On Etsy, effective rates range 72-87% depending on stacked fees; Liana Banyan is predictably 83.3% on every transaction from 5 to 50000 dollars. If platform costs decrease, the 20% applies to a lower base, automatically increasing the creator effective percentage.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight is the inversion of platform incentives: a VC-backed platform rate survives only as long as investor pressure allows, while the cooperative 83.3% is enforced by ownership alignment. The people who benefit from the rate are the same people who would have to vote to reduce it. This is the difference between a promise and an architecture.

title: The Opening Gambit
canonical_topic: A compressed 14-day 53-letter credibility-ladder outreach campaign using sequenced status transfer to establish legitimacy with academics, media, and cultural figures.
definition: The Opening Gambit is a sequenced outreach strategy where Phase 1 contacts academic researchers to create verifiable credibility claims, each subsequent phase building on prior phase engagement, all compressed into 14 days to maximize compounding. Executed by the Founder using four AI agents Bishop, Pawn, Knight, and Rook.
founder_voice_quotes: The board is set. The pieces are moving.
That is not irony. That is proof of concept.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: 53 letters in 14 days sequenced so each phase's credibility compounds on the prior, from academic researchers to cultural stars, at zero advertising cost.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Opening Gambit solves the cold-outreach chicken-and-egg problem by sequencing recipients: Phase 1 academics create verifiable claims that Phase 2 validators reference, which Phase 3 media confirms, which Phase 4 cultural figures see as substantive. Compressed to 14 days because status transfer compounds at 48 hours not 48 days. Every claim is independently verifiable.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Three distinguishing features separate it from marketing: every claim is verifiable, no money is asked, and sequencing is transparent. Built-in pauses on days 4, 8, and 11-14 allow response handling. Battery Dispatch social media runs concurrently. The Yale AI Symposium on April 28 created a hard backstop requiring academic letters 3+ weeks prior, forcing the 14-day compression over the original 12-week plan. A response playbook exists for every response type.

wading_diving_in_layer: The fundamental innovation is treating outreach as chess: the first move of academic letters does not require anyone to agree to anything, only delivery, and yet immediately changes the strategic landscape of every subsequent move. The four-agent team that built the platform is simultaneously the proof of concept the letters describe.

title: The Counter-Vote
canonical_topic: Political Expedition Counter-Vote converts passive political frustration into a verified on-record civic position against specific legislation.
definition: The Counter-Vote is a Political Expedition feature letting cooperative members formally go on record FOR or AGAINST specific bills in response to any political trigger, converting emotional engagement into verified civic participation. Members are verified by their 5-dollar-per-year membership, one vote per real person.
founder_voice_quotes: They made you angry. You made it count.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: When a rage-bait political text arrives, Counter-Vote lets members convert anger into a formal verified public legislative position in five seconds.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Political organizations harvest engagement regardless of sentiment. Counter-Vote disrupts this by creating a counter-record: a verified member formal FOR or AGAINST position on actual legislation, attached to real identity. Non-partisan by design. The platform builds the tool; members choose their positions.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Counter-Vote implements democratic jiu-jitsu: the sender investment in provocation becomes the member investment in formal civic record. The resulting dataset is verified, member-driven, and non-partisan, something that currently does not exist. A conservative and a progressive using the same tool against the same representative from opposite sides is the system working correctly, not a failure.

wading_diving_in_layer: The cooperative Counter-Vote creates a form of civic data that does not exist anywhere else: a continuously updated, verified, non-partisan record of where real people stand on real legislation. This record has no monetary value but potentially enormous democratic value, representing what citizen participation actually looks like when identity is verified and friction is near zero.

title: The Switzerland Rule
canonical_topic: The Switzerland Rule confines political and religious content to designated arena spaces while keeping all commerce spaces strictly neutral, enabling civic participation without community fracture.
definition: The Switzerland Rule is a tiered content governance policy separating neutral commerce spaces from arenas where political and religious discussion is permitted under rules. Violations earn Acknowledgment Stamps rather than bans; arena sanctions never affect marketplace participation. The platform provides infrastructure for members to defend their own positions without taking sides.
founder_voice_quotes: The platform does not take sides. But it builds arenas where members can.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The platform stays neutral on politics and religion while building structured arenas where members can advocate freely under fair identity-verified rules.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Rather than banning political and religious content or allowing it everywhere, the Switzerland Rule creates four tiers: commerce spaces are neutral, arenas exist and are labeled, arenas have rules enforced by Acknowledgment Stamps, and all positions are attached to verified identities. Religion and politics are treated identically because they are linked in practice.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Acknowledgment Stamp decouples arena behavior from economic participation. A member can be a heated political debater and lose arena access without affecting their storefront, transactions, or membership. This architectural decoupling of speech domain versus economic domain is the cooperative answer to appearing either partisan or indifferent. Cooperatives that avoid all political engagement become invisible to policymakers; those that embrace it risk alienating half their membership. The Switzerland Rule is the third option.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Switzerland Rule reveals that most platform moderation fails because it treats content and economic access as bundled. By decoupling them, arena stamps affecting only arena access and never marketplace function, the cooperative can enforce community standards without the economic coercion that makes corporate moderation feel like authoritarianism.

title: The Four-Dollar Question
canonical_topic: On a 5-dollar transaction the creator keeps 4.17 dollars and the cooperative takes 0.83 dollars composed of actual platform operating cost plus a 20% margin funding all sixteen initiatives and cooperative operations.
definition: Cost+20% means the cooperative marks up only its own operational costs by 20%, not the product price. On a 5-dollar transaction the platform cost is approximately 69 cents, the 20% margin is approximately 14 cents, and the creator keeps 4.17 dollars. The 14-cent margin per transaction funds all cooperative operations with no shareholders extracting profit.
founder_voice_quotes: Cost+20%: the cooperative actual cost to process the transaction plus 20%. That is the price. The creator receives the remaining 83.3%.
No investor to feed means every cent of that margin goes back into the cooperative.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: On a 5-dollar sale the creator keeps 4.17 dollars; the cooperative takes 83 cents covering real costs plus a 14-cent margin funding sixteen initiatives and AI development.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Cost+20% marks up the platform actual costs, not the product. The critical distinction: a 20% fee on 5 dollars takes 1 dollar; Cost+20% takes 83 cents. On a million-dollar platform, this difference is 170,000 dollars that stays with creators. If platform costs decrease, the Cost+20% margin shrinks automatically, passing savings to creators.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The 14-cent margin per 5-dollar transaction funds four AI agents at 5 to 15 dollars per session, infrastructure, sixteen initiatives, and patent protection, with no investors extracting dividends. Compared to Etsy, Amazon, App Store, and Uber, Liana Banyan leaves significantly more with creators. The cooperative scales revenue only by growing transaction volume, never by squeezing margin. The platform fundamental alignment: it earns more only when creators sell more.

wading_diving_in_layer: The most profound element is the self-enforcing nature of efficiency gains: if the cooperative builds better servers or negotiates cheaper payment processing, the Cost+20% margin automatically falls and creators effective percentage rises. This is the inverse of a VC-backed platform where efficiency gains increase management take. The cooperative architecture makes creator prosperity and platform health mathematically identical.

title: The Acknowledgment Stamp
canonical_topic: The Acknowledgment Stamp is a moderation mechanism that keeps flagged content visible while creating a public record of review, separating arena behavior from economic participation.
definition: The Acknowledgment Stamp is a three-tier moderation response where flagged arena content remains visible but receives a public stamp indicating which rule was violated; three stamps trigger only a 30-day arena pause, never affecting marketplace access, transactions, or membership. This decouples speech accountability from economic sanction.
founder_voice_quotes: What the stamp communicates to nobody: you are banned, you are silenced, your voice does not matter.
In a cooperative where members depend on the platform for livelihood, moderation that affects economic access is a sanction that goes far beyond the offense.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Flagged content stays up with a visible public stamp; members receive graduated notifications; three stamps yields a 30-day arena pause only, never marketplace or membership sanctions.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Binary moderation creates three bad outcomes: removal feels like censorship, keeping feels like endorsement, bans are nuclear. The Acknowledgment Stamp avoids all three. Content stays, member is notified, arena pause is scope-limited. Human review is required; AI assists routing and rule suggestion but never stamps. Downvotes are rejected because they measure popularity not rule compliance.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The separation principle is the key design decision: arena behavior and economic behavior are distinct domains. A stamp affects only arena access. Storefront, transactions, governance votes, Cue Cards, and reputation scores remain intact. The three-stamp progression, inform then warn then 30-day pause, gives members three chances to adjust before limited consequences apply. After 30 days the counter resets with no permanent record.

wading_diving_in_layer: The system deepest innovation is epistemological: by keeping stamped content visible rather than removing it, the cooperative preserves the community ability to form its own judgment. This is a radical departure from content moderation as deletion. Instead of the platform deciding what the community should see, the platform marks what it has reviewed and lets the community interpret it.

title: The Wildfire Run
canonical_topic: A Wildfire Run is a Captain-launched time-limited geographically targeted membership growth sprint using community competition and Beacon Points to solve the cooperative Cold Start problem without advertising spend.
definition: A Wildfire Run is a cooperative growth mechanism where a Captain sets a geographic target, member goal, and time window; member-runners earn Beacon Points for referrals tracked on a live leaderboard; top runners earn permanent honor roll entries. The model replaces advertising spend with intrinsically motivated community growth at structurally negative customer acquisition cost.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: A Captain launches a 3-7 day member recruitment sprint in a specific zip code; runners earn Beacon Points and honor roll recognition at zero advertising cost.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Wildfire Runs give motivated members a structure: goal, deadline, leaderboard, for something they already want, which is helping the cooperative grow locally. Geographic targeting eliminates waste. Compare to Facebook 15-25 dollar CAC or Uber 200-plus dollar driver acquisition cost: the cooperative CAC is structurally negative because the 5-dollar membership revenue exceeds the server cost of a new account immediately.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Beacon system contextualizes Wildfire Runs: a Beacon is a geographic marker that lights up at critical member mass. A dim Beacon signals a Captain to launch a Run. Top performers earn permanent honor roll entries. 50 members in a zip code sustains a small food network; 100 enables cooperative purchasing; 500 enables a full cooperative economy. Each completed Run is a Cold Start solution for a specific place.

wading_diving_in_layer: The honor roll is the deepest innovation: it transforms growth from a transactional act into a historical one. In 10 years, when a zip code has thousands of members, the honor roll shows who lit the first Beacon. This permanence creates motivation that no financial incentive can replicate, appealing to the human desire to be remembered as someone who showed up first when it mattered.

title: The Bridge
canonical_topic: The Outbound Commerce Bridge is a platform architecture where the cooperative actively sends customers to creators external channels because its revenue comes from production and membership not transaction capture.
