skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Guided Tour is a deliberate rejection of activation-metric-optimized onboarding. Conventional platforms design onboarding to maximize completion of tasks that benefit the platform (fill in data fields, connect payment methods, invite friends). The Guided Tour orients members so they can participate in a cooperativeâ€”which requires understanding, not task completion. The WildFire Tour (demonstration mode for onboarding/demo purposes) is distinct from the Guided Tour (real user orientation), and the default state for new users is empty/zeroed data, not mock balance.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Guided Tour's design philosophy reveals a deeper cooperative principle: the platform exists to serve members, and that service begins by respecting their time and trusting their judgment. A gamified onboarding with badges and completion rewards treats members as activation metrics. A six-stop docent tour that ends with 'go build something' treats members as adults who joined to participate, not to collect rewards. The design choice is a statement about the relationship between platform and member.

title: The Coalition
canonical_topic: Voluntary purchasing alliances formed by independent storefronts to achieve wholesale volume discounts with built-in cross-promotion and Cost+20% transparent economics leaving $416.67 of every $500 purchase with the creator.
definition: A Coalition is a voluntary, non-franchise association of independent member storefronts that pool specific purchasing needs to achieve tiered volume discounts (e.g., 12% at 500 units/month, 18% at 1,000/month)â€”displayed via storefront cards and plain-language discount tiers, with built-in cross-promotion across coalition members and a transparent purchasing pipelineâ€”at Cost+20% on all flows, leaving $416.67 of every $500 coalition purchase with the creator.
founder_voice_quotes: A cooperative is not a platform that connects buyers to sellers. A cooperative is a structure where the participants own the mechanism that connects them.
Twelve shops, one purchase order, shared savings, no extraction.
The savings go back to the shops, not to a middleman.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Twelve coffee shops buy 600 pounds of beans instead of twelve times 50â€”the volume discount stays in the neighborhood.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Coalitions are not franchises: no central authority, no mandatory branding, no operational control ceded. They are voluntary associations pooling specific purchasing needs. Discount tiers appear in plain language (not spreadsheets). Cross-promotion is structuralâ€”coalition members' customers see the other members. The purchasing pipeline shows pending confirmations and bottlenecks in one stream. Cost+20% applies: on $500, creator gets $416.67.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Coalition makes the Group Purchasing Organization (GPO) modelâ€”proven in healthcare, hospitality, and food service for decadesâ€”available to any collection of small businesses on the cooperative for $5/year per member. The interface design (storefront cards, plain-language tiers, single-stream pipeline) makes the mechanism practical for businesses that cannot afford a purchasing department. The cross-promotion built into coalition membership means joining for purchasing power also creates marketing exposureâ€”one cooperative action creates multiple cooperative benefits.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Coalition is the cooperative principle made tangible at the local business level: twelve independent shops retain full operational autonomy while gaining scale advantages previously available only to chains. The key distinction from a franchise is structuralâ€”no entity owns or controls the other members. The cooperative owns the mechanism, the members use it, and the savings flow entirely to the members at Cost+20%. This is what 'participants own the mechanism' means in concrete economic terms.

title: The Lemon Lot
canonical_topic: A military-inspired neighbor-to-neighbor vehicle sharing system with a $15/day database-enforced floor rate, mandatory insurance verification, and safety ledger trip logging.
definition: The Lemon Lot digitizes the military installation peer vehicle sharing practice with a database-enforced $15/day minimum (CHECK constraint preventing underpricing), mandatory insurance verification before listing (verified/pending badge system), and a safety ledger for trip start/end logging creating a dispute-proof recordâ€”without owning vehicles, employing drivers, or setting surge pricing, treating vehicle sharing as community trust with digital infrastructure.
founder_voice_quotes: The military figured out decades ago that people who trust each other will share resources without a corporation in the middle.
The platform does not own vehicles, employ drivers, or set surge pricing.
$15/day floor. Insurance verified. The Lemon Lot is open.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: $15/day minimum, insurance verified, trip loggedâ€”vehicle sharing between neighbors the way military installations always did it.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The $15/day floor is database-enforced via a CHECK constraint: you cannot list your car for $10/day even if you want to. This protects listers from race-to-bottom dynamics that destroyed driver economics on gig platforms. Insurance verification is required before listing goes liveâ€”no badge, no rental. The safety ledger logs every trip start and end with timestamp and rental ID for dispute resolution.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The military Lemon Lot analogy is precise: neighbors on an installation share vehicles through a parking lot with phone numbers on signs, enabled by shared trust and shared accountability. The cooperative provides the digital infrastructureâ€”listing, floor rate, insurance check, safety ledger, Cost+20% economicsâ€”and everything else is between two members who live in the same community. The cooperative does not become a rental company (no vehicle ownership, no driver employment, no surge pricing) but provides the structure that makes peer trust scalable.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Lemon Lot's most significant design decision is the database-enforced floor rate rather than a policy-recommended minimum. A suggested $15/day minimum would be undercut immediately by competitive pressure. A CHECK constraint cannot be undercut. This is the same philosophy as Cost+20% in the pricing engine: rules that exist in code are guaranteed, rules that exist in policy documents are intentions. The cooperative encodes protection in architecture.

title: The Rideshare Route
canonical_topic: A transparent cost-based rideshare system where drivers enter actual gas costs, riders see the complete math, drivers keep 83.3%, and no surge pricing exists because the structural constants are the same on Tuesday afternoon as on New Year's Eve.
definition: Rideshare Routes are priced on driver-entered actual gas cost multiplied by Cost+20%â€”rider sees the full calculation before the trip, driver keeps 83.3%, platform takes Cost+20% on processing, no surge pricing, no algorithmic fare manipulationâ€”with a safety ledger logging every match to build the driver's portable ADAPT score as a personal asset the driver owns and controls.
founder_voice_quotes: Driver enters gas cost. Rider sees the math. Nobody gets surprised.
They are structural constants â€” the same percentage on a Tuesday afternoon as on New Year's Eve.
A driver who can predict their earnings and a rider who can see the math will tolerate a slightly higher fare in exchange for knowing that the system is not designed to extract from either of them.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Driver enters gas cost, rider sees the full math, driver keeps 83.3%â€”no mystery fares, no surge pricing, no quarterly surprises.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: On a route where gas costs $8, the rider pays $9.60, the driver keeps $8, the platform takes $1.60. The math is on the label before the trip starts. No surge pricingâ€”the percentage is the same at 2 PM Tuesday as on New Year's Eve. The safety ledger logs every match, building the driver's portable ADAPT score. The competitive advantage is trust, not priceâ€”a driver who can predict earnings and a rider who can see the math will pay a fair price for transparency.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Rideshare Routes does not attempt to compete with Uber on price (Uber subsidizes with venture capital to acquire market share). It competes on trust: a conventional rideshare driver on a $25 fare receives $15-19 with no explanation of the deduction; a Rideshare Route driver on a $9.60 fare receives $8 and both parties understand exactly why. The structural constants (83.3%/16.7%) cannot be changed by a quarterly business decisionâ€”they are the same mechanism that governs every other transaction on the platform.

wading_diving_in_layer: Rideshare Routes demonstrates that the cooperative's economic model (Cost+20%, structural constants, transparent math) can directly replace extractive gig economy mechanics in a specific vertical. The driver's ADAPT score as a portable reputation the driver ownsâ€”rather than a platform metric the platform controlsâ€”is the key differentiator: a driver who leaves the cooperative takes their reputation with them, which is the structural opposite of the Uber 4.9-stars-stay-behind model.

title: The Subscription Channel
canonical_topic: A creator recurring revenue system where creators keep 83.3% structurally and subscribers pay in any of four currenciesâ€”Credits, Marks, Joules, or dollarsâ€”with no platform-driven revenue share changes.
definition: Subscription Channels are creator-controlled recurring revenue streams with a structural 83.3% creator share (Cost+20%), four accepted payment currencies (Credits at 1:1 to dollar, Marks from governance contributions, Joules from surplus value, dollars), creator-controlled preview/locked content mix, and in-place subscription unlockâ€”competing on transparent math (on $10/month with 100 subscribers, the difference is $996-$1,596 annually versus conventional platforms).
founder_voice_quotes: Four currencies. One creator share: 83.3%. The math does not change.
The 83.3% is structural, not promotional. It does not change when the platform decides it needs more revenue.
The subscribers are the creator's.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Creators keep 83.3%, subscribers pay in four currencies, and the math never changesâ€”because it's structural, not promotional.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: On a $10/month subscription, creator keeps $8.33 here versus $7.00-$9.50 on conventional platforms. Over a year with 100 subscribers, that difference is $996-$1,596â€”rent for many creators. The platform handles currency routing; the creator sees one revenue number regardless of which currency subscribers use. Preview content appears on the same page as locked content; subscribing unlocks it in-place without a redirect.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The four-currency model reflects the cooperative's three-currency economic system: Credits (1:1 to dollar), Marks (earned through governance participation and contribution), and Joules (surplus currency earned when the cooperative generates more value than it consumes), plus dollars. The economics block appears near the subscription optionsâ€”visible as the answer to 'why subscribe here?' The 83.3% is the same structural constant that governs every transaction on the platform; there is no 'creator economy' tier with different rules.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Subscription Channel's most significant feature is the structural permanence of 83.3%: it cannot be changed by a business decision, only by a governance vote. When Patreon adjusted its fee structure in 2017, creators who had built businesses around Patreon's terms had no recourse. On the cooperative, the creator's 83.3% is in the operating agreement alongside the $5 membership feeâ€”both require a member vote to change. This transforms the creator-platform relationship from contractual to constitutional.

title: The Tribe
canonical_topic: Personal community groupings organized around identity, place, and shared lifeâ€”structurally separated from professional Guildsâ€”with map-first UX, no activity leaderboards, and Family Table integration.
definition: Tribes are opt-in personal community groupings (neighborhoods, veterans' circles, gardening communities, faith fellowships, chosen families) with a map-first directory defaulting to proximity as primary filter, warmer interface DNA than Guilds, no activity or size leaderboards, and Family Table integration for household coordinationâ€”structurally separated from professional Guild identity so personal belonging is never optimized for engagement metrics or monetized through behavioral targeting.
founder_voice_quotes: Guilds are what you do. Tribes are who you are. Both matter equally.
The platform does not rank Tribes by size, activity, or growth rate.
A Tribe of six neighbors who share a lawnmower is as valid as a Tribe of six hundred veterans.
Your neighborhood group should not be optimized for engagement metrics.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Guilds are what you do. Tribes are who you are. Both cost $5, both matter equally, neither is ranked.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Tribes default to map view because they are about proximity and shared placeâ€”distinct from Guild directories that default to skill-first. Tribe cards are warmer and softer than Guild cards. No leaderboard: a six-person lawnmower-sharing Tribe is structurally equal to a six-hundred-person veterans' Tribe. Family-type Tribes connect to the Family Table for household coordination without becoming a surveillance network.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The structural separation of Tribes and Guilds prevents the perverse incentive created when personal and professional identity collapse into one profile: neighborhood groups should not be engagement-metric-optimized, veterans' circles should not be monetized through targeted advertising, faith communities should not be ranked by activity scores. The cooperative's design recognizes that personal belonging and professional reputation are different human needs requiring different architectural DNAâ€”and that conflating them serves the platform's engagement metrics at the expense of authentic community.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Tribe's deepest insight is that 'no leaderboard' is a design choice that costs the platform something (engagement metrics, growth data, optimization handles) in order to protect members from something (having their personal communities turned into platforms for extraction). The cooperative explicitly chooses not to measure, rank, or monetize Tribe activityâ€”because the cooperative's mission is to serve human life, and human belonging cannot be measured without being distorted.

title: The Guild
canonical_topic: Professional skill-based communities with search-first directories, multi-Guild membership, portable ADAPT score reputation, 83.3% creator share on Guild-sourced work, and no premium tiers or promoted listings.
definition: Guilds are professional communities organized around skills and trades with search-first, category-organized directories, skill verification, project boards, and multi-Guild membership per memberâ€”where the ADAPT score provides portable reputation the member owns (not platform-locked), 83.3% applies to all Guild-sourced work at Cost+20%, no promoted listings exist, and the Guild is an organizing structure rather than a toll booth.
founder_voice_quotes: Medieval model. Modern infrastructure. Five dollars. Your reputation is yours.
The guild vouched for the craftsperson. The craftsperson's reputation traveled with the guild's seal.
The Guild does not take a cut â€” it is an organizing structure, not a toll booth.
Structurally incapable of extracting from their members.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Professional communities where $5/year gets you in, no premium tier gets you seen, and your reputation is yours to take anywhere.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Guilds default to search-first, category-organized directories because they are about function (you need a plumber) not proximity (you need your neighbor). Multi-Guild membership means a carpenter who also teaches and does photography is a full member of three Guilds simultaneously. The ADAPT score is unified across all Guildsâ€”the whole person's reputation, not siloed versions. The Guild does not take a cut on any work it sources; Cost+20% goes to the cooperative, 83.3% to the creator.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The medieval Guild analogy is structurally precise: medieval guilds set standards, trained apprentices, and provided portable reputation through their seal. Modern professional networks monetize the mechanism (LinkedIn's recruiter access fees, Upwork's 10-20% cut). The cooperative Guild is an organizing structure: it provides community, skill verification, project boards, and directory visibilityâ€”and takes nothing from the work it helps match. A plumber with ten years' experience and a 95th-percentile ADAPT score appears in the directory on the same terms as a plumber who joined yesterday because the difference is in the ADAPT score, visible to anyone, not in the advertising budget.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Guild's most significant innovation is the combination of portable reputation and no toll: the ADAPT score follows the member out of the cooperative if they leave, and the Guild takes nothing from the work it matches. These two features together make the Guild structurally incapable of the extraction dynamic that defines modern professional platformsâ€”the platform cannot hold the reputation hostage (portable) or extract from the work (no cut). The cooperative platform can only grow by providing genuine value, not by accumulating lock-in leverage.

title: The Storefront
canonical_topic: A five-step wizard creating a cooperative marketplace listing in under ten minutes with live preview, Cost+20% transparent economics at the pricing step, and no promoted listings or pay-to-play visibility.
definition: The Storefront Builder is a five-step wizard (what/describe/price/photos/review) with a live preview pane that publishes marketplace listings without theme selection, payment integration, or per-listing feesâ€”showing Cost+20% economics at step three (on $50, creator keeps $41.67), ranking listings by relevance and recency rather than advertising spend, and connecting to Coalition, Captain dashboard, Cue Card one-hop attribution, and Red Carpet creator recruitment.
founder_voice_quotes: Five steps. Ten minutes. Your shop is open.
The platform takes Cost+20% and nothing else.
There are no promoted listings. There is no pay-to-play visibility.
A new seller with one product has the same visibility mechanics as a seller with fifty products.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Five steps, under ten minutes, your shop is openâ€”with Cost+20% explained at the pricing step and no advertising budget required.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Compare: Etsy takes 45 minutes, Shopify takes longer (domain, themes, payment integration), Amazon Seller Central takes days (bank verification, tax interviews, category approvals). The Storefront Builder is five steps with a live preview pane. The pricing step shows Cost+20% in descriptive formâ€”not buried in a FAQ. Rankings are by relevance and recency, not advertising spend. New sellers and established sellers compete on the same terms.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Storefront connects to every other cooperative system: Coalition membership pools purchasing power, Captain dashboard provides analytics without surveillance, Cue Card one-hop attribution lets members recommend the storefront to their networks, Red Carpet brings high-value creators whose audiences discover products. There is no per-listing fee, no monthly fee beyond the $5 annual membership, no promoted listing option. The cooperative's revenue comes from Cost+20% on transactionsâ€”which means the platform only benefits when members succeed.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Storefront's no-promoted-listings rule is the cooperative's most direct statement about the relationship between visibility and payment: every platform that sells promoted visibility creates a two-tier marketplace where advertising budget determines reach. The cooperative cannot sell promoted listings because it is structurally committed to equal visibility mechanics for all members. This is not a business choiceâ€”it is a cooperative principle encoded as a feature absence.

title: The Dispatch
canonical_topic: The cooperative's outbound messaging system (MoneyPenny) requiring a Steward Stamp on each message to structurally prevent mass-blast campaigns and enforce deliberate, quality-over-volume communication.
definition: The Dispatch (MoneyPenny) is a multi-channel outbound communication system handling transactional emails, operational notifications, Cue Card outreach, and Battery Dispatch sequencesâ€”with a Steward Stamp required per message (structurally preventing mass-blast), code-enforced per-platform daily rate limits, a visible dispatch queue (scheduled/sent/failed/retry), and inefficiency-by-design that treats recipient attention as a non-renewable resource.
founder_voice_quotes: Every outbound message costs the sender something. Not money. Attention.
Attention is not free.
Fewer messages, each one deliberate, each one worth the recipient's time.
The guardrails are not suggestions. They are enforced in code.
Every message stamped. Every send deliberate.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every message requires a Steward Stampâ€”deliberately inefficient, structurally preventing spam, treating attention as non-renewable.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Dispatch handles four message types with different guardrails: transactional emails (immediate), operational notifications (batched), Cue Card outreach (rate-limited per platform), and Battery Dispatch sequences (timed drops). The Steward Stamp makes mass-blast campaigns structurally impossibleâ€”each message must be individually stamped, which means individually considered. The dispatch queue shows everything in pipeline with per-platform rate limits visible.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The system is inefficient by design. A conventional platform optimized for message volume would remove the stamp requirement, increase rate limits, and add bulk-send tools. The cooperative optimized for message quality. The result: no unsubscribe rate problem, because no spam problem. When every message costs the sender a moment of deliberate attention, the messages that get sent are the ones that matter. The MoneyPenny internal name reflects the system's role as the platform's outreach coordinatorâ€”professional, deliberate, and mindful of the recipient's attention.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Dispatch's deliberate inefficiency is the cooperative's most counterintuitive design choice: in a world where communication platforms optimize for volume, the cooperative's outbound system is architecturally constrained to produce fewer, better messages. The Steward Stamp is not a UX featureâ€”it is a structural cost imposed on the sender to protect the recipient. This reflects the cooperative's core premise: the platform serves members, and members are on both sides of every message.

title: The Beacon
canonical_topic: Location-based discovery system that connects cooperative members to local businesses through gamified walking routes without behavioral surveillance.
definition: A Beacon is a location-tagged cooperative signal placed by a member at a real business or service, discoverable by nearby members via phone — not an advertisement or coupon. Beacon Runs, Wildfire Runs, and Treasure Maps gamify local economic discovery, generating foot traffic and reputation without collecting surveillance data.
founder_voice_quotes: Drop a Beacon. Walk a route. Discover your neighborhood. Nobody's watching.
Discovery should benefit the discoverer and the discovered.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Location-based discovery turns a neighborhood walk into an economic act for local businesses.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: A Beacon is a cooperative signal — not an ad — tied to a real business at a real location. Beacon Runs curate gamified routes; Wildfire Runs let members chain discoveries organically; Treasure Maps layer Cephas education onto physical geography. No behavioral data is collected or sold.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Beacon system applies the geocaching and Pokemon Go principle — attach a reward to a location and people will go there — to cooperative local economic discovery. It generates foot traffic for businesses that cannot afford digital advertising and delivers Cephas educational content through Treasure Map quiz nodes embedded in physical locations. Only a member ID and a discovery timestamp are recorded, never location history or movement patterns, making the system architecturally opposed to surveillance commerce.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Beacon system is the cooperative's proof that economic signal does not require surveillance: value flows to local businesses and participating members through voluntary discovery, not algorithmic behavioral targeting, and the member's walking patterns remain their own property.

title: The HexIsle
canonical_topic: A cooperative game world built on hexagonal geography where in-game economic mechanics mirror the platform's real three-currency system.
definition: HexIsle is a gaming domain where Credits, Marks, and Joules operate identically to the rest of the cooperative, ensuring game participation generates genuine cooperative value. The hex grid's equal-adjacency geometry enforces structural fairness, and a Spanish-language parallel surface (Hexislo.com) serves Spanish speakers natively rather than through translation.
founder_voice_quotes: Hexagons. Real economics. Two languages. The game where the money is yours.
economic systems can be designed so that participation generates value for the participant, not just for the system operator.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: HexIsle is the cooperative's game domain — hexagonal geography, real economics, two languages.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Unlike extractive games that convert player engagement into platform revenue via loot boxes, HexIsle uses the cooperative's real Credits, Marks, and Joules. FocusShell removes all navigation chrome during play for full immersion. Hexislo.com is a parallel Spanish surface, not a translation layer.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: HexIsle demonstrates the platform's core design philosophy in playable form: economic systems built so participation generates value for participants. The hex grid enforces this geometrically (no positional advantage by shape), the currency linkage enforces it economically (gaming time is cooperative time), and the bilingual architecture enforces it linguistically (Spanish speakers are first-class participants). HexIsle is one of 23 domains but its design logic is the cooperative's design logic made experiential.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight of HexIsle is that a game's economic architecture is the most honest proof-of-values a platform can offer: if game currencies extract from players, so does the platform; if they circulate value back to participants, so does the platform. HexIsle's real-economy currency linkage is architectural proof, not a game feature.

title: The IP Ledger
canonical_topic: A member-facing intellectual property documentation system that tracks innovations from creation to patent filing with full creator attribution and equal access for all members.
definition: The IP Ledger records every cooperative member's innovations with timestamps, A&A formal documents, and Crown Jewel classifications, making  provisional patent filings accessible to any member. The system uses relational tables with JSONB metadata, full-text search via GIN-indexed tsvector columns, and recursive CTEs, scaling to 1M documents in Postgres.
founder_voice_quotes: 2,130 innovations. 168 Crown Jewels. Every one documented. Every creator credited.
The ledger is not a filing cabinet. It is the infrastructure that makes individual IP ownership practical at cooperative scale.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The IP Ledger documents every member's innovations with the same quality as the platform's own.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Members are not employees — they own what they create. The IP Ledger enforces this with timestamps, A&A formal documents (description, prior art, claims, Crown Jewel classification), and patent bag groupings for provisional filing. As of B063, the ledger contains 2,130 innovations, 168 Crown Jewels, and 2,103 formal claims across 11 USPTO provisionals filed for  total.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The IP Ledger's architectural significance is that it makes individual IP documentation at cooperative scale practical without reserving those tools for institutional actors. Any member with one innovation gets the same template quality, AI assistance, and formal process as the platform itself. The Postgres architecture (JSONB, GIN full-text, recursive CTEs) scales to 100,000-1,000,000 innovations before requiring a graph database migration, giving the cooperative decades of runway before architectural change is needed.

wading_diving_in_layer: The IP Ledger is the infrastructure layer that transforms cooperative membership from participation-without-ownership into documented-ownership-with-legal-standing: every member's contribution is timestamped, classified, and filing-ready, making the cooperative's IP protection architecture as member-facing as its commerce architecture.
empirical_receipts: 2,130 innovations documented; 168 Crown Jewels; 11 provisionals filed at  total as of B063

title: The Twenty-Three Domains
canonical_topic: A five-month architectural migration from monolithic React app to 23 self-contained domain modules, executed by one Founder and four AI agents with zero employees.
definition: Between November 2025 and April 2026, Knight, Bishop, Rook, and Pawn — coordinated by the AI Tuner — reorganized the platform into 23 distinct domains each with pages, components, hooks, libraries, routes, and types. The result is 658 v2 files backed by 580 database tables, 139 edge functions, 470 pages, and 455 Cephas content entries.
founder_voice_quotes: 23 domains. 658 files. 5 months. One founder. Four agents. Zero employees.
The founder — who calls himself the AI Tuner — does not write code. He writes prompts.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: 23 self-contained domains built by one Founder and four AI agents in five months.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: In November 2025 the platform was a monolithic React app. By April 2, 2026 it was 23 distinct domains. Knight executed 230 sessions. Bishop wrote prompts and maintained the Librarian knowledge base. Rook analyzed. Pawn researched and designed pages. The Founder wrote prompts — never code.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The 23-domain architecture is significant not as a technical achievement but as proof that cooperative-quality platforms can be built without venture capital, without a payroll, and without compromising architectural standards — by treating AI agents as genuine collaborators rather than autocomplete tools. Each domain (Commerce, Housing, Governance, Currency, Beacon, Calendar, Manufacturing, Defense, and 15 more) is independently understandable and testable, enabling the cooperative to evolve any domain without destabilizing others.

wading_diving_in_layer: The v2 migration's deepest proof is systemic: a single human writing prompts and four AI agents with complementary specializations can produce institutional-quality software architecture with 580 tables, 139 edge functions, and 35 production systems in five months — not because AI replaces engineers but because it amplifies a careful Founder's architectural vision into executable reality.
empirical_receipts: 230 Knight sessions; 658 v2 files; 580 tables; 139 edge functions; 35 production systems

title: The Librarian
canonical_topic: A self-indexing Model Context Protocol server that delivers task-scoped knowledge packages to AI agents in ~600 words, solving the context-window memory problem at platform scale.
definition: The Librarian MCP is a TypeScript application exposing 23 specialized tools and 15 index files rebuilt in 24 seconds, enabling AI agents to call brief_me and receive relevant context rather than reading every file. It catalogs 580 tables, 679 components, 71 hooks, 147 libraries, and all platform content without itself reasoning or making decisions.
founder_voice_quotes: 23 tools. 15 indexes. 24 seconds to rebuild. The memory the agents need.
A librarian's job is not to read every book. It is to know where every book is and to hand you the right one when you need it.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The Librarian is the memory system that lets AI agents operate across a platform too large for any single context window.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Librarian MCP solves AI context memory limitations with 23 specialized tools across 15 index files rebuilt in 24 seconds. brief_me returns task-scoped context in ~600 words. moneypenny_checklist validates tasks pre-implementation. check_consistency catches architectural violations before they reach production. The Librarian retrieves; it does not reason.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Librarian's architecture separates retrieval from reasoning: it indexes 580 tables, 679 components, 71 hooks, 147 libraries, 15 content categories and makes them queryable by purpose-built tools. This allows any AI agent entering a session cold to reach architectural competence in a single tool call rather than spending half its context window on orientation. The 24-second rebuild time is both a performance specification and an architectural constraint — the system must be light enough to be rebuilt on-demand.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Librarian is the infrastructure layer that makes the AI Tuner's human-AI collaboration model structurally sound: without it, every session would require exhaustive re-orientation that consumes agent context; with it, agents arrive knowing exactly what they need to know for the task at hand, making each session's effective work capacity orders of magnitude greater.
empirical_receipts: 24-second full rebuild; 15 index files; 23 tools; 580 tables catalogued

title: The Cost Plus Twenty
canonical_topic: The canonical pricing rule — Cost+20% — that mathematically enforces cooperative margin and structurally prevents platform extraction as membership scales.
definition: Cost+20% means the platform calculates its actual facilitation cost and adds exactly 20% margin, yielding 83.3% to creators on every transaction regardless of platform size or market position. This margin is hardcoded in the codebase, database, and legal structure — there is no mechanism anywhere in the system to adjust it.
founder_voice_quotes: Cost+20%. The margin that cannot change. The math on every label.
The math is on the label because the math is the product.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Cost+20%: the margin that cannot change, enforced in code, database, and law.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Every platform has a take rate. Uber: 25-40%. Etsy: 6.5% plus fees. Amazon: 8-45%. Liana Banyan: Cost+20%, applied universally to every transaction. The 20% is a sustainability number, not a profit-maximizing number. It cannot be adjusted because extraction requires the ability to increase the take rate — and the code prevents that.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Cost+20% is architecturally significant because extraction is not a policy failure but a structural possibility: any platform that can raise its margin will, eventually, once it gains market power. By encoding Cost+20% as a database trigger and legal constraint rather than a policy, Liana Banyan eliminates the structural possibility of extraction. Growth benefits members (more customers, more purchasing power) without costing them a higher percentage. The conventional counterargument — fixed margins limit growth capital — is accepted as a feature, not a bug.

wading_diving_in_layer: Cost+20% is the cooperative's most fundamental structural proof: a platform cannot be both extractive and constitutionally margin-capped, and by making the cap immutable, the cooperative has permanently resolved the structural tension between platform economics and member welfare in favor of members.
empirical_receipts: 83.3% creator share on every transaction;  transaction yields .67 to creator

title: The One-Way Valve
canonical_topic: The architectural constraint making Credits, Marks, and Joules one-directional — dollars convert in but cooperative currencies never convert back to fiat, preventing speculation and money-transmission regulation.
definition: The one-way valve eliminates cash-out from cooperative currencies, making Credits internal service tokens (not money-transmission instruments), Marks effort records, and Joules surplus certificates. This both insulates the internal economy from external financial markets and avoids state-by-state money-transmission licensing that would be fatal to a 5-dollar-per-year cooperative.
founder_voice_quotes: Dollars go in. Credits, Marks, and Joules stay in. The valve does not reverse.
The valve is irrevocable. It is not a policy that can be changed by a board vote. It is a structural constraint baked into the system's architecture.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Credits go in, value stays in — the valve that makes the internal economy speculation-proof.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Every platform with an internal currency has a cash-out button — Twitch, Roblox, airline miles. Liana Banyan does not. Dollars convert to Credits 1:1. Credits never convert back. This eliminates the speculative layer that destroyed every crypto utility token, insulates the cooperative economy from stock market crashes, and legally classifies Credits as arcade tokens rather than money-transmission instruments.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The one-way valve's regulatory significance is as important as its economic significance: a currency convertible to dollars triggers state-by-state money-transmission licensing requirements and federal oversight that would cost more than the cooperative earns at any realistic early membership size. By making the valve irrevocable and structural (not a policy), the cooperative permanently avoids this regulatory exposure while preserving the economic utility of internal currencies for member-to-member commerce.

wading_diving_in_layer: The one-way valve is the cooperative's answer to the fundamental question of internal currency design: should cooperative currencies be instruments of member value exchange or instruments of external financial speculation? By making the answer structural and irrevocable, the cooperative ensures that Credits, Marks, and Joules can only ever serve the purpose for which they were designed — facilitating cooperative activity.

title: The Vacation Network
canonical_topic: A cooperative housing exchange where members share homes using Credits, Marks, or dollars at a fixed Cost+20% rate, enabling travel through earned effort-currency rather than cash.
definition: The Vacation Network enables member-to-member home sharing with Cost+20% as the immutable platform margin and the ADAPT score as the trust layer, eliminating hidden fees and dynamic pricing. Members with accumulated Marks can travel without spending dollars, making access to cooperative housing proportional to cooperative participation.
founder_voice_quotes: Share your home. Spend your Marks. Travel the network. The platform takes Cost+20% and nothing more.
The housing exists. The members exist. The currencies exist. The network connects them without extracting from the connection.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Cooperative home sharing at Cost+20% where participation earns you the ability to travel.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Airbnb started cooperative and became extractive — service fees climbed to 14-20%, cleaning fees appeared at checkout, dynamic pricing raised rates at peak demand. The Vacation Network returns to the original premise: Cost+20% always, .33 per  to the host always. The ADAPT score replaces five-star gaming with demonstrated behavioral reliability. Marks earned through cooperative participation spend on stays without cash.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Vacation Network's deepest innovation is currency portability across initiatives: Marks earned by completing Beacon runs, moderating the Round Table, or teaching a class can be spent on a weekend in another member's home. This creates a genuine cooperative economy where diverse forms of contribution translate into diverse forms of consumption, and the platform's margin is fixed regardless of how desirable the destination or how peak the demand period.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Vacation Network proves that cooperative economics scales to housing — one of the largest individual expense categories — without requiring venture capital, platform-controlled pricing algorithms, or extraction of member value: Cost+20% and the ADAPT score are sufficient infrastructure to run a cooperative short-term rental market.

title: The Hundred
canonical_topic: The hundredth Pudding article, reflecting on the corpus of cooperative documentation and the AI-human partnership that produced 100 accessible articles covering the full platform architecture.
definition: Pudding #100 marks the completion of a hundred accessible articles about the Liana Banyan platform, written by Bishop and covering cooperative economics, three-currency systems, location-based discovery, manufacturing, dispute resolution, and the AI architecture underlying the build. The name Pudding derives from 'the proof of the pudding is in the eating,' framing articles as tasting evidence of a working system, not a recipe.
founder_voice_quotes: per year. 83.3% to the creator. Cost+20%. The math on the label.
For the Keep.
One hundred articles. One platform. One founder. Four agents.
The proof of the pudding is in the eating. Here are one hundred reasons to take a bite.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: One hundred proof-of-pudding articles about a cooperative platform built by one Founder and four AI agents.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Pudding name derives from the phrase 'the proof is in the pudding' — you cannot know if a pudding is good by reading the recipe, you taste it. Each of the hundred articles is a tasting note for one piece of a platform with 23 domains, 580 tables, 2,130 innovations, 168 Crown Jewels, 11 provisionals, and 35 production systems at /year membership with 83.3% creator share and Cost+20% margin.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Pudding #100 is architecturally significant because it demonstrates the AI Tuner model at documentary scale: the same human-AI collaboration that built 23 domains and 580 tables also produced 100 accessible explanations of those domains and tables. The Founder did not write the articles any more than he wrote the code — he wrote prompts specifying what needed to exist, and Bishop produced the artifacts. The documentation of the cooperative is itself a product of the cooperative's AI architecture.

wading_diving_in_layer: The hundredth Pudding is a milestone that proves the documentation architecture is as real as the technical architecture: 100 articles covering the full breadth of a cooperative platform built from scratch over 37 years of thinking and 5 months of building, with the articles themselves as evidence that the platform's ideas are expressible, testable, and ready to be eaten.
empirical_receipts: 100 Pudding articles completed; 580 tables; 2,130 innovations; 168 Crown Jewels; 11 provisionals; 35 production systems as of B063

title: The Fingertips System
canonical_topic: A self-scaling knowledge infrastructure where Librarian instances automatically reproduce at the Half Rule threshold, ensuring the AI team always has instant domain-specialist access regardless of archive size.
definition: The Fingertips System introduces a five-rank hierarchy — Red Queen (adversarial AI validator), Master Librarian (orchestrator), Staff Librarian (domain specialist), Corps Script (scheduled worker), Invokable Script (atomic task) — where each rank spawns a new instance at 50% capacity load, guaranteeing the system always operates below half its threshold. The Red Queen must always be a different AI architecture than the system she verifies, structurally preventing blind-spot propagation.
founder_voice_quotes: Everything the AI team needs is always at the fingertips. Always.
The Fingertips System is named for its promise: everything the AI team needs is always at the fingertips. Not in a drawer. Not behind a 24-second rebuild.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The knowledge infrastructure that grows automatically as the archive grows — never overloaded, never blacked out.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The current Librarian works at 10,000 files but will not at 100,000. The Fingertips System solves this through military-style hierarchy: Red Queen validates, Master Librarian routes, Staff Librarians own domains, Corps Scripts run schedules, Invokable Scripts handle atomic tasks. New instances spawn at the Half Rule threshold — 50% of capacity — so the system always has headroom. One failure means one domain degraded, not total blackout.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Fingertips System borrows the logic of congressional apportionment — more constituents, more representatives — to solve AI knowledge infrastructure scaling. Each data unit is a constituent; each Librarian instance is a representative with bounded capacity to actually serve its constituency. The Red Queen's architectural requirement (always a different AI model than the system she verifies) is the most sophisticated element: it ensures adversarial verification retains independent judgment rather than sharing the same blind spots as the system it audits.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Fingertips System's deepest architectural insight is that knowledge infrastructure must be treated as a first-class scaling problem with the same rigor applied to database sharding or microservice decomposition: bounded capacity, automatic replication at threshold, adversarial verification, and single-point-of-failure elimination are not nice-to-haves but structural requirements for a platform that intends to outlive its initial builders.
empirical_receipts: 49:1 Cathedral vs Grep hit ratio observed at B134; Half Rule guarantees sub-50% operating load

title: The Blizzard
canonical_topic: A failure taxonomy for silent platform failures — defined as the absence of expected signal rather than the presence of error — which AI agents cannot detect without expected-state models.
definition: A Blizzard is when a platform appears operational (200 OK, successful build, no error messages) yet produces no visible output, because the failure lives in what should have happened but did not: the auth callback that never fired, the lazy-loaded component that silently failed to mount. The diagnostic doctrine — BOTG, layer-by-layer verification, absence detection — was developed from three documented Blizzard incidents in November 2025.
founder_voice_quotes: The most dangerous failure is the one that looks like nothing.

A blizzard does not announce itself with a single thunderclap. It arrives as a gradual whiteout — visibility drops, landmarks disappear, and you realize you are lost only after you have been walking in the wrong direction for an hour.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Silent failures that look like nothing: the Blizzard taxonomy and how to diagnose what AI agents cannot detect.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: A Blizzard is not a crash (loud, has a stack trace) or a Conk (has a location). It is silence where there should be signal. AI agents can detect presence (errors) but not absence (the function that never ran). Three documented Blizzards: a Netlify _headers file that stopped component rendering without error, an auth callback that never fired behind a legitimate loading screen, and lazy-loaded components that received raw .tsx source instead of compiled JS.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Blizzard taxonomy is architecturally significant because it identifies a fundamental asymmetry in AI agent diagnostic capability: agents reason from evidence and cannot reason from its absence. Teaching absence detection requires expected-state models ('the auth callback should fire within 3 seconds'), timeout-based absence signals, and cross-layer correlation ('successful deploy + blank screen = Blizzard signal'). The AI Nanny protocol (Innovation #2129) is the first step toward this; Blizzard detection would require real-time monitoring of expected-state assertions.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Blizzard's deepest implication is that member-facing platform failures will often be Blizzards — a storefront where products silently fail to load, a listing where content fails to render — and the support methodology must match: not 'what error do you see?' but 'what should be here that is not?' The Blizzard diagnostic doctrine is as much a member support methodology as a developer debugging protocol.
ratification_chronology: B070 (April 3, 2026); Journal 001-003; November 2025 development sprint; Innovation #2129
empirical_receipts: Three documented Blizzard incidents resolved via BOTG + absence detection methodology

title: Blood, Sweat, and Tears
canonical_topic: A vote-gated serial publishing system (Crewman #6 / Innovation #2133) that converts the platform's 260-document archive into hourly micro-posts, with audience social engagement triggering full paper publication on Cephas.
definition: Innovation #2133 (Crewman #6) extracts ~280-character micro-posts from academic papers and posts them hourly via Battery Dispatch, unlocking the full source paper when a chapter's social engagement crosses a threshold. The full archive yields an estimated 3,000-5,000 episodes — 4-6 months of continuous content — with zero new writing required, and the publication model makes audience engagement the publication decision rather than an editorial one.
founder_voice_quotes: The proof is in the Pudding. The Pudding is now being served — one bite at a time, voted into existence by anyone who cares enough to engage.
FOR THE KEEP!
Distribution is not separate from content — it IS the content.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The archive becomes hourly episodes; audience engagement votes papers into publication on Cephas.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: 260 publications sat in a vault. Innovation #2133 (Crewman #6 — after the Galaxy Quest character who survives by refusing the script) extracts ~280-char episodes posted hourly via Battery Dispatch. When a chapter's engagement threshold is crossed, the source paper publishes on Cephas. Chapter 1: 52 episodes from StarScreaming. Chapter 2: 42 episodes from The Blizzard. Full archive: 3,000-5,000 episodes. Timeline: 4-6 months of continuous content.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Blood, Sweat, and Tears is architecturally significant because it inverts traditional publishing: readers live through the story in fragments before the paper appears, and their engagement is the publication decision rather than an editorial one. The system self-documents — every episode generates engagement data, every vote-gate crossing generates timing data, and the meta-paper describing the engine will itself become a future chapter voted into existence by the audience it describes. The audience governance of publication is structurally identical to member governance of commerce.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest structural insight of Crewman #6 is that a cooperative's distribution system can itself embody cooperative governance: when audience engagement determines what gets published (not an editor or algorithm), the audience is exercising the same participatory power over content that members exercise over commerce, making the publication system a proof-of-cooperative-principle rather than merely a content strategy.
empirical_receipts: 94 episodes produced in B071 from 2 papers; estimated 3,000-5,000 total from full archive

title: Skipping Stones
canonical_topic: A section-level entry-point system for academic papers that turns each paper section into a shareable Deck Card offering progressive depth from one-sentence hook through Pudding article to full academic paper.
definition: Skipping Stones creates 240-360 independent entry points into 30 academic papers, each as a physical Deck Card with QR code (or digital Cue Card link) landing on a choice between the accessible Pudding or the full paper at the exact section of origin. The Reading Beacon tracks engagement depth (skip, wade, dive) and feeds vote-gating and tier progression.
founder_voice_quotes: You're standing at the edge of a lake. You pick up a flat stone. You don't throw it straight — you throw it sideways, low, and watch it touch the surface once, twice, three times before it decides whether to skip again or sink.
The name isn't accidental. Skipping stones is what you do when you're not sure if the water is worth jumping into.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every paper section becomes its own shareable entry point — skip, wade, or dive at your depth.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Every first-level section of every paper becomes a Deck Card: a physical card with a QR code (or digital Cue Card), one-sentence hook, and a landing page offering two paths: the accessible Pudding (wade in ankle-deep) or the full academic paper deep-linked to the exact section (dive to the bottom). The Reading Beacon records which depth you reached, feeding cooperative engagement systems. 30 papers, 8-12 sections each = 240-360 Skipping Stones.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Skipping Stones and Blood, Sweat, and Tears together create two complete paper discovery systems for the same corpus: Skipping Stones is spatial (enter any paper at any section), BST is temporal (follow the narrative in order). Together they transform a 30-paper academic archive into 1,000+ distributable engagement points without new writing, and the Reading Beacon system turns engagement depth into cooperative currency and governance weight through vote-gating and tier progression.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight of Skipping Stones is that academic papers fail to reach their intended audience not because the ideas are inaccessible but because the format demands full commitment before offering any value: the Deck Card system eliminates that barrier by letting readers touch the surface with zero commitment, making the decision to go deeper the reader's organic choice rather than the paper's institutional demand.
empirical_receipts: 30 papers x 8-12 sections = 240-360 Skipping Stones; 142 BST episodes at time of writing

title: The Tooth
canonical_topic: A reflection on productive discomfort in cooperative business formation, distinguishing hard (necessary) from unnecessarily hard (friction the platform can remove) through the metaphor of a child's loose tooth.
definition: Using the metaphor of a child's loose tooth, Pudding #105 argues that the cooperative's tools — Cue Card, Treasure Map, Cold Start Hub, Captains — remove logistical friction (format, design, distribution, orientation) while preserving the productive discomfort of pricing, pitching, and publishing one's own work that makes the business real. Making it easier, not easy, is the design principle.
founder_voice_quotes: We try to make it easier. Not easy. Easier.

The cooperative is the toothbrush. The Treasure Map is the knowledge that something better is growing underneath. The Cue Card is the gentle wiggle. The Captain is the parent who says: I know. Keep going. It's worth it.
Because the difference between giving up and pushing through is usually not courage. It's having the right tool at the right moment.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The cooperative removes unnecessary friction but preserves the productive discomfort that makes a business real.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Starting a business involves necessary discomfort: describing your work in 15 seconds, pricing honestly, posting publicly. The cooperative does not eliminate this. It removes the unnecessarily hard parts: formatting, designing, distributing, figuring out where to start, being alone in it. The Cue Card gives a template (not your words). The Treasure Map gives steps (not your decisions). The Captain says 'keep going' (not 'I'll do it for you').
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Tooth's philosophical contribution to cooperative design is the distinction between friction that protects members (the productive discomfort of ownership, pricing, public commitment) and friction that merely discourages them (logistical complexity that the platform is better positioned to absorb than the member). A cooperative that removes all friction removes the member's stake in the outcome; one that removes none loses members before they discover their stake. The platform's tools are calibrated to the boundary between these categories.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Tooth is the cooperative's most honest articulation of what membership means: not a service that removes all difficulty but a structure that absorbs the unnecessary difficulty so members can face the necessary difficulty with the right tools at the right moment, understanding that the sting of pricing your work is the sting of calling it yours — which is the whole point of cooperative membership.

title: The Loop
canonical_topic: A self-referencing content distribution system where engagement data from three concurrent series across six channels becomes the content of the next distribution cycle, written by the cooperative's own distribution engine.
definition: The Loop runs Blood Sweat and Tears, Spoonfuls, and Skipping Stones across six social channels with ~24 posts/day tagged by the ten-spice Spice Rack taxonomy, generating ~720 monthly engagement records that determine the next chapter's content. Over 1,000 distributable pieces exist from existing material, providing ~50 days before any content repeats, with zero new writing required.
founder_voice_quotes: The Loop writes its own next chapter. Distribution generates data. Data shapes content. Content generates distribution.
The audience watches a cooperative learn how to talk to them — in real time — and the learning is part of the story.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Distribution data writes the next chapter: a content system that learns from its own audience in real time.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three concurrent series (BST: hourly narrative micro-posts; Spoonfuls: bite-sized insights; Skipping Stones: paper section entry points) run across six channels with no overlap, tagged by ten-spice taxonomy. 30 days of posting generates 720 engagement records — enough to answer whether finance content performs better on LinkedIn or Twitter, whether innovation lands at 8am or 6pm. The answers become Chapter 4 of BST. The distribution system writes its own next chapter.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Loop's architectural achievement is closing the feedback loop between distribution and content: a typical content calendar generates vanity metrics; the Loop generates parameterized intelligence (channel x time x topic x audience) that directly informs the next content decision. Combined with 1,000+ existing distributable pieces, the system provides ~50 days of non-repeating multi-channel content from existing material while simultaneously generating the data needed to optimize its own future distribution — a cooperative marketing system that improves through use rather than requiring continuous new creative production.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Loop's deepest proof-of-cooperative-principle is that a cooperative's marketing system can be structurally honest: the engagement data that writes the next chapter is the audience's authentic response, not A/B-tested manipulation; the spice taxonomy routes content to interested audiences through declared taxonomy rather than behavioral surveillance; and the system's improvement compounds through genuine use rather than through optimization against extracted behavioral data.
empirical_receipts: ~24 posts/day; 6 channels; 10 spices; 1,000+ distributable pieces; ~720 monthly engagement records; ~50 days before content repeats

title: The Five Dollar Career
canonical_topic: Six new economic roles — Bounty Photographer, Pearl Diver, Home Teacher, Captain, Freezer Node, Rideshare — that run on shared cooperative infrastructure at 5 dollars per year with unlimited attempts.
definition: At /year membership, the cooperative enables six new economic roles where shared infrastructure (Commerce Engine, three-currency system, scheduling, reputation) reduces marginal role cost near zero. The Pioneer Program breaks the cold-start deadlock by rewarding early adopters inversely proportional to practitioner count — first ten receive 50 Marks/month for a year and a numbered brass medallion — with the subsidy ending when the role recruits itself at 1,000 practitioners.
founder_voice_quotes: Five dollars. Unlimited attempts. The cooperative absorbs the infrastructure. You absorb the coffee money. That is the point.
The cooperative does not create demand — it makes existing demand safe.
On every extractive platform, the cost of trying is the cost of failing — and it is never five dollars.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Six new careers for /year: the cooperative absorbs infrastructure, you absorb the coffee money.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The extractive platform calculus: Uber needs -300/month commercial insurance; Etsy raised fees with no member recourse; Udemy takes 63%. The cooperative calculus: /year, unlimited role attempts, shared Commerce Engine and three-currency system absorbs the marginal infrastructure cost of each new role. The Pioneer Program solves cold-start with inverse-proportional rewards — first-mover risk is compensated, late-mover free-riding is not subsidized. 21,000 San Antonio residents already organize informal ridesharing through Facebook groups; the cooperative does not create demand, it makes existing demand safe.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The  Career paper's most significant contribution is the mathematical demonstration that shared infrastructure at cooperative scale makes marginal role cost approach zero: each new role costs time to write a Cue Card and map income flows, but the payment processing, reputation, scheduling, and three-currency system are already paid for by the  membership base. This inverts the gig platform model where each new service category requires platform investment in supply-side subsidy; the cooperative's members are already the supply side, already paying for their own infrastructure.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight of the  Career is that the economic barrier to trying a new career is not skill (most informal workers already have the skills) or demand (Hood Uber proves organic demand exists) but the cost of failure on an extractive platform where trying costs hundreds in infrastructure and fees. At /year with shared infrastructure, the cost of failure is five dollars and a few hours — survivable enough that rational actors try rather than wait, which is how cooperative economic participation self-compounds.
ratification_chronology: B074 (April 4, 2026); Innovations #1936, #1975-1978, #2100-2105; Paper submitted to Stanford Social Innovation Review
empirical_receipts: 21,000+ observed Hood Uber participants in San Antonio; 6 new roles documented; break-even ~500 active members per locale; self-sustaining at 1,000 practitioners per role

title: The Spice Must Flow
canonical_topic: A ten-spice content taxonomy (Salt through Oregano) that simultaneously tags platform content for audience filtering, declares member skills for project matching, and operationalizes the Stone Soup fable as cooperative project coordination.
definition: The Spice Rack (Innovation #2142) assigns ten business skill domains to culinary spices, enabling members to follow content by declared interest rather than algorithm and projects to declare the recipe of skills they need. The Recipe Pot (Innovation #2143) operationalizes this as cooperative project matching — members bring their spice, the project fills, the colony produces — completing a seven-link food metaphor chain from Stone Soup to Popcorn.
founder_voice_quotes: The Spice Must Flow.
Bring your Garlic to our Pot.
The ants carry their spice. The colony feeds itself.
Not as a slogan. As architecture.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Ten spices tag content, declare skills, and power cooperative project-matching — architecture, not slogan.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Spice Rack maps Salt (operations), Garlic (finance), Sugar (marketing), Cinnamon (design), Pepper (legal), Ginger (innovation), Cumin (engineering), Paprika (leadership), Basil (education), and Oregano (coordination) to content tags, skill declarations, and project requirements simultaneously. The Recipe Pot operationalizes Stone Soup: a project declares 'we need Garlic, Cumin, and a pinch of Pepper'; members bring their spice; the pot fills. 744+ content pieces are spice-tagged at 100% coverage of Spoonfuls.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Spice Must Flow's architectural significance is that a single taxonomy simultaneously solves three distinct coordination problems: content discovery (follow your spice, not an algorithm), skill self-declaration (your spice is what you offer), and project matching (the recipe tells you what the project needs). A sentence that says 'bring your Garlic to our Pot' is simultaneously a content filter, a skill declaration, a project matching mechanism, and a cooperative invitation — the spice taxonomy makes this multi-valent communication possible without requiring separate systems for each function.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest proof of the Spice Rack architecture is that it closes the loop between content consumption and cooperative production: a member who follows the Garlic content becomes more financially literate, declares themselves a Garlic contributor, joins a Recipe Pot project that needs Garlic, and produces value through that project — creating a seamless pathway from content discovery to cooperative contribution that requires no separate on-ramp at any stage.
empirical_receipts: 744+ content pieces spice-tagged; 4 of 4 innovations in the chain are Crown Jewels; 7-link food metaphor chain (Stone to Soup to Bread to Pudding to Spoonfuls to Spices to Popcorn)

title: The Lighthouse Ladder
canonical_topic: A five-level hard-capped mentorship hierarchy that enables one Harbormaster to maintain meaningful connection with 100,000 members through bounded ten-mentee relationships at each level.
definition: The Lighthouse Ladder (Torch Bearer to Lamplighter to Beacon Keeper to Warden to Harbormaster) enforces a maximum of ten mentees per mentor in the data model, with advancement gated by outcomes (retention, engagement, satisfaction scores) rather than recruitment numbers. One Harbormaster reaches 100,000 members geometrically (10^5) with no single mentor responsible for more than ten human beings at any level.
founder_voice_quotes: How do you personally welcome 100,000 people? You do not. That is the honest answer.
One to one hundred thousand. Not through automation. Through ten honest relationships, stacked five levels deep.
The membership still costs five dollars. The onboarding is still human.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Five levels, ten mentees each, hard-capped in the database: 1 Harbormaster reaches 100,000 members.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Dunbar's research caps meaningful cognitive relationships at 5-15 innermost. The Lighthouse Ladder solves the 100,000-member onboarding problem geometrically: 10 Wardens x 10 Beacon Keepers x 10 Lamplighters x 10 Torch Bearers x 10 new members = 100,000, with no human ever responsible for more than ten. Advancement requires outcome metrics (retention, engagement depth, satisfaction scores), not recruitment — explicitly distinguishing the structure from MLM override commissions.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Lighthouse Ladder's key architectural distinction from MLM is that economic participation runs entirely through Cost+20% (separate from mentoring), and advancement is gated by the quality of mentoring outcomes rather than the volume of recruitment. The ten-mentee cap is enforced in the data model (not the employee handbook), making it a structural constraint rather than a policy. The formalized interaction-load model in the companion paper demonstrates bounded mentor workload across all five levels — Torch Bearers handle ~250 interactions per cycle, Harbormasters handle ~20.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Lighthouse Ladder's deepest insight is that human-scale relationships (five to fifteen meaningful connections) are the cooperative's competitive advantage over algorithmic matching platforms: the Torch Bearer who knows their ten mentees deeply, who tracks their Contingency Operator scenarios and temperament hints and guild progress, provides onboarding support that no recommendation engine can replicate, and the Lighthouse Ladder structure makes this level of attention economically viable at 100,000-member scale by bounding each mentor's responsibility to a human-manageable ten.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); companion paper ~10,000 words, 30+ APA references; Kram, Eby, Ragins, Dunbar, Rochet-Tirole, Lave-Wenger
empirical_receipts: Geometric reach: 10^5 from 1 Harbormaster; Torch Bearer load: ~250 interactions/cycle; Harbormaster load: ~20 interactions/cycle

title: The Invisible Temperament
canonical_topic: A four-factor implicit temperament detection system embedded in cooperative onboarding that adapts mentoring and interface presentation without labeling or restricting members.
definition: During onboarding, preference questions invisibly shift a four-dimensional temperament_hint vector (Organizer, Improviser, Connector, Synthesizer) disclosed after onboarding with full member override and disable controls. The hint feeds the Lighthouse Ladder's mentor-matching engine and adapts Contingency Operator presentation without ever restricting access or creating permanent categorization.
founder_voice_quotes: Liana Banyan does not give you a test.
The Invisible Temperament is not about categorizing people. It is about building a platform that pays attention — honestly, transparently, and with the off switch always within reach.
Privacy without deception. Transparency without compulsion.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Invisible detection, visible disclosure, full member control: temperament that adapts without labeling.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: LinkedIn asks for your job title. Most personality platforms give you a test and a label. Liana Banyan embeds preference questions in natural onboarding context, shifts a four-dimensional vector, and discloses after onboarding: 'here is what we noticed, here is how we use it, here is the off switch.' The column name is temperament_hint — deliberately provisional. It feeds mentor matching and Contingency Operator presentation adaptation. Access gates use zero temperament data.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Invisible Temperament's ethical framework distinguishes itself from dark patterns through transparency timing: the hint is collected unobtrusively (not to enable gaming) but disclosed explicitly after onboarding (not to enable deception). Three user controls (inspect, override, disable) are always accessible. The four-factor model (Organizer/Improviser/Connector/Synthesizer) is inspired by Keirsey but stripped of Myers-Briggs letter codes and contested psychometrics — stored as a hint, not a result, because the system's design philosophy is that four factors and five questions cannot capture the full complexity of a human being.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest proof of the Invisible Temperament's design philosophy is the column name itself: temperament_hint instead of temperament_type. The name encodes the epistemological position that the system's knowledge of any member is always provisional, always the member's to correct, and never the platform's to weaponize — which is the cooperative's fundamental position on all member data.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); companion paper ~8,000 words; Keirsey, Big Five, Netflix implicit feedback methodology, Eby negative mentoring experiences
empirical_receipts: 5-8 embedded preference items sufficient for four-factor hint; 0 access gates use temperament; 3 user control mechanisms always accessible

title: Self-Funding Economics
canonical_topic: The cooperative platform's economic architecture — Cost+20%, dollar-5-per-year membership, three separated currencies, and a DNA Lock database trigger — that achieves positive unit economics from transaction one without venture capital.
definition: Unlike venture-subsidized platforms (Uber accumulated  in losses before profitability), Liana Banyan achieves positive unit economics on transaction one through Cost+20%, with the 83.3% creator share protected by a DNA Lock database trigger that intercepts and rolls back any modification attempt. Three currencies (Credits, Marks, Joules) each perform one function, preventing speculation — and the /year membership establishes legal cooperative ownership rather than user status.
founder_voice_quotes: Liana Banyan made money on its first transaction.
Five dollars. Positive unit economics from transaction one. No venture capital. Constitutional protection against extraction. 83.3% to creators. The experiment is live.
The 83.3% creator share is not a policy. It is a database trigger.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Positive unit economics from transaction one: no venture capital, constitutional protection, DNA Lock.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Uber's playbook: raise , subsidize below cost, dominate market, extract. Liana Banyan's playbook: Cost+20% always, positive unit economics from transaction one, no debt to repay, no investors demanding ten-fold returns. The DNA Lock is a database trigger that intercepts and logs any modification attempt to constitutional parameters and rolls back the transaction — the board cannot change it, a referendum cannot change it, an acquisition cannot change it. Three currencies each do one thing: Credits spend, Marks govern, Joules save — one token doing everything produces speculation (Bitcoin proved this).
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Self-Funding Economics' architectural significance is that it resolves the venture capital dependency trap structurally rather than through willpower: by making Cost+20% and /year DNA-locked and making three-currency separation one-way-valved, the cooperative has closed every architectural pathway through which extraction could enter — not as policy positions but as database constraints and legal structures that require the kind of deliberate effort that would be visible to every member before any change could take effect.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest proof of Self-Funding Economics is its formalized solvency analysis at five scale scenarios (300 to 5M members): the cooperative does not need to reach unicorn scale to be financially viable — at 50,000 members it generates ~.95M/year in revenue with Cost+20% margin, sufficient to fund platform operations, member services, and ongoing development without external capital dependency at any point in the growth curve.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); companion paper ~12,000 words; Coase, Williamson, Rochet-Tirole, Buchanan-Tullock, Milgrom, Zuboff, Doctorow, Scholz, Schneider, Mondragon literature
empirical_receipts: Uber:  cumulative pre-profit losses; LB: positive unit economics transaction 1; Apple 30% vs LB 16.7%; Revenue at 50K members: ~.95M/year

title: The Portable Reputation
canonical_topic: A five-category member-owned reputation portfolio that is fully portable on exit, verified against real interactions, and cryptographically anchored for high-stakes use outside the cooperative.
definition: The Portable Reputation Portfolio accumulates blocks (compressed 5:1 to stars, 5:1 to suns) across Quality, Timeliness, Professionalism, Collaboration, and Standards — all anchored to verified real interactions, never self-reported. Members export via signed JSON and blockchain-hash provenance medallion on exit, retaining 100% of their reputation history compared to 0% on extractive platforms.
founder_voice_quotes: An Uber driver completes 10,000 trips. Five stars. A decade of excellent work. Then they leave. Their reputation? Zero.
The cooperative issued your credentials. You own them. Present them anywhere. The signature is on it. The provenance is on-chain.
Privacy without deception. Transparency without compulsion.
cross_references: guild system (Forge Scale Engine Room War Table Ledger Crow's Nest Quarterdeck)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Your cooperative reputation is yours — verified, portable, blockchain-provenance-anchored, never lost on exit.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Platform reputation lock-in is a business model: Uber drivers' 10,000-trip histories disappear on exit because that history is the platform's asset. The Portable Reputation Portfolio inverts this: five verified categories (not a single gamed star rating), blocks-to-stars-to-suns compression that cannot be faked or purchased, guild credentials from seven cooperative guilds, signed JSON export, and blockchain-anchored provenance medallions for high-stakes verification. LB member data retained on exit: 100%. Privacy is member-controlled at the category level — hide or show, but not cherry-pick within categories.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Portable Reputation Portfolio's most architecturally significant element is the provenance medallion: a cryptographic hash on the blockchain proving the portfolio existed in its claimed state at a specific time, with no personal data on-chain. This enables a member to present their portfolio to an employer, a housing provider, or another platform with the same evidentiary weight as a notarized document — cooperative-issued, cryptographically verifiable, and entirely owned by the member regardless of their continued relationship with the cooperative.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight of the Portable Reputation is that reputation portability is a cooperative principle, not a technical feature: an extractive platform keeps your reputation data because your dependency on it is a revenue asset; a cooperative releases your reputation data because your ownership of it is a membership right. The blockchain provenance medallion makes this principle operationally real — you can leave the cooperative and carry its seal of verification with you forever.
