ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); companion paper ~12,000 words, 25+ refs; Resnick, Dellarocas, Bolton, Shapiro-Varian, Scholz, Allen SSI, Deterding gamification
empirical_receipts: Uber exit: 0% reputation retained; LB exit: 100% retained; 5 scoring categories; 25 interactions per sun; 7 guilds with portable credentials

title: The Patent Bags
canonical_topic: Twelve numbered patent bags covering mechanical systems, cooperative commerce, platform simulation, and AI innovations, filed across 11 provisional USPTO applications at micro-entity cost.
definition: Patent Bags 5-10 cover physical innovations (hydraulic oscillation water table, diceless physics-based combat, personal protection jewelry with evidence collection, three cooperative businesses, incubation without equity, Ghost World platform simulation) and Bags 21-26 cover AI and platform systems innovations compiled by Rook — totaling approximately 90 innovations and 300+ claims across 11 USPTO provisionals at  each.
founder_voice_quotes: Somewhere in a vault, filed under a system invented by four AI agents and one Founder, there are twelve numbered bags.
Twelve bags. Ninety innovations between them. Over three hundred claims. Filed across eleven provisional patent applications. Built to last.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Twelve bags, ninety innovations, 300+ claims: physical and digital inventions filed at  each.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Bag 5: a gravity-driven water table operating without electronics, with Golden Lotus pistons and Rooster teeth air pistons. Bag 6: diceless physics-based combat where pushing IS the attack. Bag 7: wearable personal protection jewelry with integrated evidence collection and distributed legal defense fund. Bags 8-9: cooperative commerce businesses. Bag 10: Ghost World — a full-fidelity platform simulation for onboarding where one toggle moves you to real. Bags 21-26: Rook's AI/platform systems innovations covering decentralized QA, engagement, efficiency, and 3D UX.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Patent Bags' strategic architecture is incremental portfolio building through provisional filings: at /filing (micro-entity), the cooperative can file provisional applications across diverse innovation categories annually, gaining 12 months to determine which innovations merit full utility applications while establishing prior art dates. The portfolio spanning physical mechanics (hydraulic, pneumatic, mechanical-only systems) to platform software to cooperative economics demonstrates the breadth of innovation generated by a single AI Tuner with four specialized agents over five months of focused development.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Patent Bags' deepest architectural proof is Ghost World (Bag 10): a full-fidelity platform simulation where every feature exists in both simulation and reality, and new members start in the simulation with full access and no real money at risk until they toggle to real. Ghost World is simultaneously an onboarding tool, a risk mitigation mechanism, a patent-worthy innovation, and a proof-of-cooperative-principle — joining the cooperative is safe enough to try without cost, which is the cooperative's membership promise made experiential.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); Provisional applications 63/925672 through 64/025635; Rook (Gemini) compiled Bags 21-26
empirical_receipts: 12 bags compiled B075; ~90 innovations; ~300+ claims; 11 provisionals filed at  each; Bag 5 operates entirely without electronics

title: Play With These Numbers
canonical_topic: The Contingency Operator — an interactive what-if financial sandbox that replaces aspirational earnings claims with conservative range-bounded scenarios and visible Cost+20% in every model.
definition: A Contingency Operator is a role-specific slider interface where defaults are conservative (below-typical early-stage), outputs are ranges with the low end in larger font (asymmetric framing against optimism bias), and Cost+20% / 83.3% are visible and non-negotiable in every scenario. The CO replaces MLM calculators, integrates with Portable Reputation portfolios as a mini business plan, and is architected to be FTC-safe and SEC-language-compliant.
founder_voice_quotes: PLAY WITH THESE NUMBERS.
What will I earn? Every platform gets asked. And every platform lies.
The model is not behind the interface. The model IS the interface.
Not play as in games. Play as in learning.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Play with the sliders, see the real economics: conservative defaults, ranges, visible Cost+20% in every scenario.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: MLM calculators show diamond-level fantasies. Uber quotes peak-hour Manhattan earnings. DoorDash forgets insurance, fuel, and depreciation. The Contingency Operator does the opposite: conservative defaults (below-typical early-stage), range outputs with the low end in larger font, effective hourly rate compared to local minimum wage, and Cost+20% visible and non-negotiable in every scenario. Your mentor uses the same tool. You save the scenario to your Portable Reputation portfolio as a mini business plan.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Contingency Operator's behavioral economics architecture is sophisticated: the nudge (Thaler-Sunstein) is delivered through asymmetric visual framing (low end larger) rather than disclaimers; the conservative default calibration means members must actively push sliders upward to reach optimistic numbers, experiencing the departure from baseline as a felt choice rather than a passive assumption; and time modeling (prep, drive, actual service hours) surfaces effective hourly rate as a truth that gross revenue figures obscure on every gig platform.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest proof of the Contingency Operator is that it makes Cost+20% and 83.3% experiential rather than declarative: a member who has pushed every slider on every scenario has viscerally understood that the platform takes 16.7% always and they keep 83.3% always, making the constitutional economics of the cooperative as real as the projected earnings — not a policy they were told about, but a constraint they have played with and proved.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); companion paper ~12,000 words, 25+ refs; Lusardi-Mitchell financial literacy, Thaler-Sunstein, de Jong simulation pedagogy, FTC (Vemma), Rosenblat-Dubal-Prassl gig critique, Taylor MLM analysis
empirical_receipts: 16 platform roles with dedicated CO templates; 10 MLM comparison dimensions (COs win all 10); 6 SEC-safe language patterns; 5 papers of 5 in series complete

title: What the Attic Knows
canonical_topic: The Liana Banyan content archive acts as an institutional witness, enabling reconciliation of patent discrepancies and preservation of strategic pivots across 10,039 files.
definition: The Attic is treated as a living witness to organizational iteration â€” six compilation families in B075 revealed a 112-line patent discrepancy, two strategic campaign versions, and 352 variant families. Zero content loss is the standard.
founder_voice_quotes: Liana Banyan treats its Attic as a witness.
Zero content loss. The Attic keeps its promises.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The archive is not clutter â€” it is a witness that caught a 112-line patent gap.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Six document families were compiled from the Attic in B075: Patent Bags, Kickstarter Campaigns, Pitch Templates, and Crown Letters. Each compilation identified canonical versions from variants and flagged discrepancies. Only Bag 8 had a critical difference.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The B075 compilation grind processed 10,039 files across 352 variant families, compiling six major document groups. Patent Bag 8 was the only one with a critical discrepancy â€” 112 VSL lending claims in Emperor directory but not Archive copy. Kickstarter Campaign 1 preserved two strategic theories. The LOCKED prefix system provides IP protection and audit trails across all high-stakes letters.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Attic functions as a distributed institutional memory system â€” every preserved variant is both IP evidence and decision archaeology. The compilation methodology transforms raw file accumulation into a reconciliation engine, catching divergence before it becomes permanent loss.

title: The Missing Claims
canonical_topic: A 112-line discrepancy between the Emperor and Archive copies of Patent Bag 8 nearly cost the cooperative its VSL micro-lending patent claims for innovations #88-#101.
definition: Patent Bag 8 exists in two divergent copies: the Emperor version (795 lines, canonical) contains 52 claims including the full VSL lending mechanism, while the Archive copy (683 lines) is frozen at a pre-VSL draft. The compilation caught the 112-line gap before filing.
founder_voice_quotes: One hundred twelve lines of Very Short Lending claims â€” the micro-duration lending mechanism where cooperative members lend Credits to each other for hours or days, collateralized by Joules, at zero interest.
The compilation caught it.
These claims describe how the cooperative replaces payday lenders.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Two copies of the same patent file had 112 missing lines â€” the compilation caught the gap before filing.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Patent Bag 8 covers 14 innovations (#88-#101) across Let's Make Dinner, JukeBox, and VSL. The Emperor directory held the complete 795-line version with 52 claims; the Archive copy was frozen at 683 lines, missing the VSL lending mechanism. Had the lawyer filed from the Archive copy, those claims would have been lost.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The VSL claims describe a zero-interest micro-lending primitive: members borrow Credits collateralized by Joule deposits, replacing payday lenders who charge 400%+ APR. The discrepancy arose because the Archive copy predated the VSL addition; both files had identical names, making detection dependent on line-count comparison. Every other Patent Bag (5,6,7,9,10) passed the variant check cleanly.

wading_diving_in_layer: The VSL lending mechanism â€” zero-interest micro-loans between cooperative members, collateralized by Joule deposits â€” represents a structural alternative to predatory lending. Its near-loss to a silent archive divergence illustrates why compilation is not housekeeping but IP risk management.

title: The Locked Folders
canonical_topic: The LOCKED prefix file system in the Crown Letter archive preserves IP-protected revision history, with iteration count proportional to the stakes of the ask.
definition: The Liana Banyan Crown Letter archive uses LOCKED-prefixed files to create frozen, numbered snapshots of letter iterations. Craig Newmark's Infrastructure Chancellor letter has 7 variants with 4 LOCKED snapshots; Michael Seibel's CEO offer has 5 variants with 2 LOCKED snapshots.
founder_voice_quotes: This is institutional memory as file system discipline.
The cooperative's iteration history is not a story told after the fact. It is a directory listing.
Every version is preserved. Every iteration is timestamped.
cross_references: Crown Letters archive: Craig Newmark, Michael Seibel, Casey Newton, Tim Ingham

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Seven variants of a single letter â€” each iteration preserved, each frozen version LOCKED â€” because the ask has to be right.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The LOCKED prefix system timestamps and freezes letter drafts at stable review points. LOCKED01 through LOCKED04 for Craig Newmark mean four distinct moments when the draft was stable enough to archive. The pattern: highest-stakes asks (CEO, CFO, Infrastructure Chancellor) have the most preserved revisions.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Five Crown-tier letter families audited in B075: Craig Newmark (7+ variants, 4 LOCKED), Michael Seibel CEO (5+ variants, 2 LOCKED), Casey Newton Platformer (5 variants), Tim Ingham MBW (3 variants). The LOCKED prefix convention serves three functions simultaneously: IP protection, legal audit trail, and institutional memory. Most organizations keep only the final version.

wading_diving_in_layer: The LOCKED prefix convention turns filesystem metadata into legal archaeology. Each LOCKED snapshot is a timestamped claim about what the cooperative believed about a role at a specific moment â€” evidence that the role was deliberated, not invented casually.

title: The Cleanest Family
canonical_topic: The Dynamic Stats Template System produces 70+ single-version pitch templates by auto-populating canonical values from one Supabase table, eliminating drift and manual version management.
definition: Out of 70+ publication-specific pitches in the Liana Banyan archive, only 2 documents have multiple versions â€” because dynamic stats placeholders ({{innovationCount}}, etc.) auto-populate from the platform_canonical table. When stats change, every pitch updates automatically.
founder_voice_quotes: This is institutional discipline as code.
Write once. Template everywhere. Update centrally. Distribute consistently.
The template replaces the drafts.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Seventy pitches, two with multiple versions â€” the template system made iteration unnecessary.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: TechCrunch, The Verge, Forbes, The Economist, 70+ publications, each with a tailored pitch. Only the master template and PITCH_LA_CAPITAL_DEL_SABOR have multiple versions. Every other pitch is single-version because the dynamic stats system updates them automatically.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: ~30 dynamic variables ({{innovationCount}}, {{platformMargin}}, etc.) pull from the platform_canonical Supabase table activated at K170. Each publication gets one customization â€” the news hook paragraph â€” while everything else comes from the template. Zero drift risk: when innovation count changes, every pitch, letter, media kit updates from one number in one table.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Dynamic Stats Template System converts a governance problem (keeping 70 documents current) into an engineering solution (one canonical table, ~30 variables, zero manual updates). The cleanest document family in the archive is clean not because someone was careful â€” because the system made carelessness impossible.

title: The Strategic Pivot
canonical_topic: Kickstarter Campaign 1 exists in two fully preserved strategic versions â€” the $12,000 Tereno Water Table and the $1,000 Slotted Top â€” representing two distinct launch theories kept for future synthesis.
definition: Campaign 1 Version 1 (Tereno, $12K goal) leads with the hero product for maximum commitment; Version 2 (Slotted Top, $1K goal) leads with a compatible upgrade for ecosystem entry. Both are preserved in the archive for a Pawn B45 hybrid synthesis.
founder_voice_quotes: The Attic preserved the question. The Attic did not force the answer.
That is why you keep two versions. Because someday you will need to synthesize them, and you will be glad the evidence is still there.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Two versions of the same campaign preserve two theories of how to launch a product line.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Theory A (Tereno, $12K): lead with the hero product, make believers commit big. Theory B (Slotted Top, $1K): lead with a compatible upgrade, get people into the ecosystem first. A Pawn B45 hybrid analysis was dispatched to synthesize four options from these two theories.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Tereno is a gaming surface creating real tides and currents â€” no batteries, no electronics, 40 years of engineering, $12,000 goal. Slotted Top is a single 60mm hex tile compatible with Catan/BattleTech/Heroscape â€” gateway to the 27-piece modular Hexel system, $1,000 goal with 5% Joule chain-link bonuses. The 14-campaign arc includes character progression and connects to Patent Bags 5 and 6.

wading_diving_in_layer: The preservation of both campaign versions converts what appeared to be a discarded draft into a strategic asset. The difference between Theory A and Theory B is the difference between asking "do you believe?" and asking "do you want to try?" â€” fundamentally different theories of how trust is built with backers.
empirical_receipts: 5% Joule chain bonus per consecutive backing

title: Load-Bearing Fables
canonical_topic: Three classic children's fables (Little Red Hen, Stone Soup, Grasshopper and the Ants) serve as the narrative foundations for three production systems, not as marketing decoration.
definition: The Liana Banyan Fable Arc maps Stone Soup to the Recipe Pot (Innovation #2143), Grasshopper and the Ants to cooperative labor economics ('Us. The Ants.'), and Little Red Hen to the 83.3% creator share. The narratives are the platform's foundation, not its wallpaper.
founder_voice_quotes: The platform is serious infrastructure told as a bedtime story.
The narrative layer is not decoration on the outside of the platform. It is the foundation underneath.
The cooperative is the colony. The membership is the ants.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three bedtime stories power three production systems â€” the fables are not marketing; they are the foundation.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Stone Soup -> Recipe Pot (Innovation #2143): a project declares its recipe of skills needed, members bring their spice, the cooperative produces. Little Red Hen -> 83.3% to the creator: those who plant, harvest, grind, and bake get to eat. Grasshopper and the Ants -> 'Us. The Ants.': the colony survives because everyone carries something.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Fable Arc is documented in 07_REFERENCE_MATERIALS/Fable_Arc/ with drawing briefs specifying warm hand-painted children's book style: golden amber, soft yellows, burnt orange. Anti-corporate: avoid harsh neons, cold grays, black outlines. Three fables. Three production systems. The brand is a bedtime story that runs a cooperative economy. The Founder's son is illustrating.

wading_diving_in_layer: Load-bearing fables function as semantic anchors â€” they make the platform's technical mechanics emotionally legible across generations. The Recipe Pot is not a metaphor for collaboration; it IS the collaboration system. The fable and the function share the same architecture.

title: The Seven Duplicates
canonical_topic: Seven filesystem duplicate artifacts ("_1 copy.md" files) across four directories reveal how unclear document taxonomy produces copy proliferation as organizational fossil record.
definition: The B075 Attic sweep identified 7 files ending in "_1 copy.md" across 4 directories â€” ACADEMIC_PAPER_1_MARKET_VALUATION (3 locations), CONVERTIBLE_NOTE_PITCH_001 (2 locations), INVESTOR_CONVERSATION_QUICKREF (2 locations) â€” representing taxonomy ambiguity rather than versioning.
founder_voice_quotes: Seven duplicates. Three unique documents. Four directories. The duplicates are the fossil record of an organization figuring out its own taxonomy.
The lesson is that file system hierarchies are a THEORY of how your documents relate to each other.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Seven files ending in "_1 copy.md" are not versions â€” they are the fossil record of an organization figuring out its own taxonomy.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The ACADEMIC_PAPER_1_MARKET_VALUATION file appears in three directories: Crown Letters, Academic Papers, and Written root. Each copy spawned its own "_1 copy" artifact. The pattern reveals taxonomy confusion: when a file belongs in multiple categories simultaneously, it gets copied to all of them instead of tagged.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The fix is architectural: tags, cross-references, or single canonical locations â€” not three copies. The B075 sweep pattern "*_1 copy.md" caught all seven. These are not edits, not versions â€” just drag-and-drop fingerprints that accumulate when taxonomy is unclear. Recommendation: archive all "_1 copy.md" files, move ambiguous-location files to single canonical directories with cross-references.

wading_diving_in_layer: Filesystem duplicates are not a hygiene problem â€” they are a legibility problem. The seven copies reveal three documents whose category membership was genuinely ambiguous at the time of filing. Cleaning up duplicates without resolving the taxonomy ambiguity will produce more duplicates.

title: All The Things She Said
canonical_topic: The 0_AllTheThingsSheSaid inbox (242 files) is a transparent first-in-hierarchy record of content that arrived faster than synthesis could keep up, named after a t.A.T.u. song as an act of radical honesty.
definition: The Inbox is placed FIRST in the vault hierarchy ("0" prefix) with 242 files including 24 ZIP archives, a HUMANIZED content series, numbered structured outputs, and confirmed canonical duplicates. It is treated as a staging queue, not clutter.
founder_voice_quotes: Here is everything I haven't yet processed. It exists. It's first in the list because it's the truth.
Not clutter. Not shame. The honest record of content that arrived faster than the synthesis could keep up.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: 242 files named after a t.A.T.u. song, placed first in the archive, because the Founder chose honesty over appearance.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: 60 numbered batch drafts, 10 HUMANIZED content files (college-freshman and teen reading levels), 24+ ZIP archives (black boxes), and 3+ confirmed canonical duplicates. The "0" prefix ensures it appears first in any directory listing.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The ZIP archives are the most urgent concern: "Academic Paper for Currency Differential.zip" and "Academic Paper for Currency Differential UPDATED.zip" sit next to each other â€” same paper, two ZIPs, one marked UPDATED. Which is canonical? Indeterminate without extraction. The Inbox is a multi-session cleanup project. The naming is already done: honesty about the backlog is the first step toward resolving it.

wading_diving_in_layer: The decision to name the inbox after a pop song and place it first in the hierarchy is itself a canonical act â€” it converts organizational embarrassment into organizational transparency. Most founders hide their backlog; this Founder made it the first thing any agent sees when entering the vault.

title: There Is No Spoon
canonical_topic: Innovations #2145-#2148 define Temporal Content Architecture, distributing scheduling as a platform primitive across staff, creators, and members via a single shared UI component.
definition: The "All the Pudding" TV Guide and Scheduled Viewing Beacon give members the same scheduling tool as platform operators â€” a tooltip-based appointment system that turns feed consumption into appointment-based learning, with a shared primitive across three user roles.
founder_voice_quotes: The Matrix said "There is no spoon" and meant: the constraint is in your mind. The cooperative says "There is no spoon" and means: there is no object between you and the cooperative's content except the one YOU bend.
Your time is yours.
The content is ready. The schedule is yours.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Members get the same scheduling tool as operators â€” your time is yours, not the algorithm's.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Four innovations: #2145 Scheduled Viewing Beacon, #2146 Shared Scheduling Primitive, #2147 All the Pudding TV Guide, #2148 Temporal Content Architecture. The TV Guide shows 122+ Puddings, 536 BST episodes, and 628+ Spoonfuls in programming blocks with a schedule button on every item.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The same scheduling code powers three surfaces: Staff Maven schedules the Distribution Grid, Creators schedule Cue Card Battery Dispatches, Members schedule Viewing Beacons. One UI component. Three targets. The operator monopoly on time â€” how algorithmic feeds colonize attention â€” is structurally broken. Knight prompts K274-K278 were dispatched for implementation.

wading_diving_in_layer: The operator monopoly on scheduling is one of the most undertheorized forms of platform power â€” who controls "when" controls attention, habit formation, and ultimately behavioral outcomes. Distributing the scheduling primitive is not a UX feature; it is a cooperative principle applied to time.

title: Seven Versions for a CFO
canonical_topic: The Tom Simon CFO recruitment letter holds the highest iteration count in the Crown Letter archive (7 drafts + 2 LOCKED) because recruiting a CFO into a cooperative with constitutionally-locked margins requires rewriting the financial executive pitch from scratch.
definition: Seven numbered drafts plus LOCKED01 and LOCKED04 snapshots make the CFO letter the most-iterated in the archive â€” more than MacKenzie Scott (5), Craig Newmark (5), or Michael Seibel (5). The gap between conventional executive recruiting and cooperative executive recruiting is approximately seven drafts wide.
founder_voice_quotes: Seven drafts is approximately how far those two worlds are apart.
The iteration count is a measure of the gap between conventional executive recruiting and cooperative executive recruiting.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Seven drafts to invite a CFO into a cooperative â€” because conventional executive pitches don't apply.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: 122 Crown Letter files across ~45 recipients. Tom Simon CFO: 7 numbered drafts + LOCKED01 + LOCKED04. The CFO ask is the hardest: no equity dilution, no stock options, constitutionally-locked margins, a three-currency system. What you offer is the chance to design cooperative capital structures that do not exist at scale.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Version 1: case for the platform. Version 2: realized CFOs understand numbers â€” the blocker is "why no standard compensation?" Version 3: role definition. Version 004: reporting relationships. Version 005: refined opening hook. Version 006: tightened language. Version 007: canonical. 22 LOCKED files exist across all Crown recipients; CFO accounts for 2. B046 coordinated refresh of 11 letters simultaneously.

wading_diving_in_layer: The CFO letter is not just a recruitment document â€” it is a proof-of-concept for cooperative governance. Each revision probes a different objection a traditional finance executive might have. Seven revisions means seven distinct objections addressed. The final letter is structurally sound against the hardest counterarguments.

title: Three Ways to Be Canonical
canonical_topic: Three distinct canonical patterns coexist across the 45-paper academic vault: sequential versions (highest number wins), depth variants (reading commitment levels), and publication targets (venue-tuned versions).
definition: A file is canonical RELATIVE TO the purpose it serves â€” sequential versions (V1/V2/V3), depth variants (AT_A_GLANCE/IN_DEPTH/MORE_DETAILS), and publication targets (base/MEDIUM/SSIR) are all canonical simultaneously. Mechanical "highest version wins" rules destroy depth and venue variants.
founder_voice_quotes: Canonical is context-dependent. A file is canonical RELATIVE TO the purpose it serves.
Three ways to be canonical. One way to confuse an AI.
Name files so the mode is visible in the filename.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three files about executive pay are not three drafts â€” they are three reading commitments.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: AT_A_GLANCE is for skimmers, IN_DEPTH for readers, MORE_DETAILS for researchers. MEDIUM and SSIR are venue-tuned variants. A mechanical AI applying "highest version wins" would consolidate both â€” losing the skimmer version and the academic submission simultaneously.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: 45 academic papers in the vault. PAPER_EXECUTIVE_PAY: AT_A_GLANCE + IN_DEPTH + MORE_DETAILS (depth variants â€” all three canonical). PAPER_UNLIMITED_THROWS: base + MEDIUM + SSIR (publication targets â€” all three canonical). PAPER_ACCOUNTS_PAYABLE_ELIGIBLE_MARKS: V1/V2/V3 (sequential â€” V3 canonical). File naming conventions that embed the canonical type prevent compilation errors.

wading_diving_in_layer: The three-canonical-types framework is a meta-documentation primitive: it teaches any agent how to navigate archives without destroying content that looks like redundancy but is actually intentional multiplicity. This pudding is itself the documentation of a convention that was previously undocumented.

title: Two Forks and a Synthesis
canonical_topic: The Fork-1/Fork-2/Synthesis methodology produces five documents per architectural decision â€” two full alternatives, an evaluation log, a canonical synthesis, and a CANON governance file.
definition: Liana Banyan's default architectural decision method: write two complete alternative designs at matched detail (Fork-1 Additive, Fork-2 Transformative), log the evaluation, produce a synthesis, commit via CANON file. Five documents. No undocumented choices.
founder_voice_quotes: Most projects document what they decided. Liana Banyan documents what it rejected.
The methodology transfers.
Surface alternatives, document them fully, choose deliberately, preserve the rejected options so future reviewers can audit the choice.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Most projects document what they decided. Liana Banyan documents what it rejected.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Five documents per decision: FORK1 (Additive), FORK2 (Transformative), INTEGRATION_LOG (evaluation), SYNTHESIS_REPORT_033 (canonical outcome), StarChamberAdvisoryCouncilCANON.txt (governance lock). The rejected forks are preserved at the same detail level as the chosen path.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Fork methodology protects against two architectural failure modes: committing too fast (missing a better alternative) and drifting too slow (accumulating unexamined assumptions). By requiring a full write-up of the rejected alternative, it forces genuine evaluation. The CANON file (.txt format) locks the governance commitment at creation. The same methodology was applied in Pawn B45 Campaign 1 hybrid analysis: four options laid out, three rejected with explicit reasoning.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Fork methodology is not documentation overhead â€” it is an epistemic commitment. By requiring the rejected path to be written at the same level of detail as the chosen path, it prevents post-hoc rationalization. The organization can answer 'why didn't you do it the other way?' with a document, not a memory.
ratification_chronology: B075 (April 4, 2026); Star Chamber synthesis at version 033
empirical_receipts: Pawn B45 applied same methodology to Campaign 1

title: Why 033 Shows Up Everywhere
canonical_topic: Version number 033 appears across three document families (Blueprint, Execution Protocols, Star Chamber Synthesis) as a cooperative cross-document commitment marker for synchronized architectural events.
definition: MASTER-BLUEPRINT-033.json, EXECUTION_PROTOCOLS_033.json, and STAR_CHAMBER_SYNTHESIS_REPORT_033.md share version 033 by convention â€” major synthesis events produce cross-document version stamps that bind multiple document families to a single architectural moment.
founder_voice_quotes: One number. Three documents. One moment of architectural coherence, preserved across three different content domains.
The 033 convention is a cooperative response to drift.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: When version 033 happened, it touched three document families simultaneously â€” the shared number says they all agree with each other.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: At version 033: the master blueprint was updated, the execution protocols were revised, and the Star Chamber synthesis was committed. All three got the 033 stamp. This allows any agent to grep "033" across the archive and find every document part of that architectural event.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Most archives version each document independently with no way to reconstruct platform state at any single moment. The 033 convention solves this with shared version numbers across document families â€” a commitment device meaning "at this point, these documents agree." Research question: what were the 034 and 035 events? This Pudding is itself the documentation of an undocumented convention.

wading_diving_in_layer: The 033 convention is a temporal alignment primitive â€” it makes the platform's architectural history reconstructible from the outside. Any future agent, auditor, or historian can ask 'what was the state of the platform at version 033?' and get a complete answer by querying the archive for all 033-stamped documents.

title: Two Pudding Systems
canonical_topic: Two parallel Pudding storage systems evolved independently â€” System A (128 sequential numbered) and System B (21 topic-based vault files) â€” with 12 feature explainers ready for integration and one gap at #25.
definition: System A is the current canonical (numbered Puddings in cephas_puddings DB with Depth Layers and Spice Tags); System B is a predecessor of 21 topic-based feature explainers (Backer Election, Captain System, Ghost World, etc.) in the vault, ready to be integrated into the main numbering.
founder_voice_quotes: Systems drift even when individual documents are disciplined.
Periodically audit for parallel systems. Not just audit within systems. Audit ACROSS systems.
Are we solving the same problem in two places without noticing?

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Two Pudding systems did the same job without noticing each other â€” 12 feature explainers are ready to be integrated.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: System A: 128 sequential numbered Puddings in cephas_puddings, with structured format, Spice Tags, and Depth Layers. System B: 21 vault files including 12 feature explainers. PUDDING_025 is confirmed missing from System A.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: System B predates the numbering architecture â€” feature explainers were written before System A was formalized, then left in place when sequential Puddings began at #1. Both systems are well-maintained; the problem is parallel evolution without awareness. Integration: each System B file needs a number, spice tag, and SQL insert. Until integration, 12 platform features are invisible in the All the Pudding TV Guide. Pawn B53 dispatched for reconciliation.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Two Pudding Systems finding is a case study in organizational drift: neither system failed, neither was undisciplined, but they evolved in parallel without awareness until an audit surfaced both. The lesson is not about tidiness but about the need for cross-system audits distinct from within-system audits.

title: Why Prov 12 Stays Open
canonical_topic: Provisional Patent 12 is deliberately held open to absorb 18 innovations (#2131-#2148) before filing, prioritizing completeness over cadence at zero additional filing cost.
definition: The Founder chose to keep Prov 12 open while A&A formals for innovations #2131-#2148 are written. One $65 micro-entity fee covers all 18 innovations in a single coherent filing â€” completeness is cheaper than cadence when fees are fixed.
founder_voice_quotes: When will the next patent be filed? When the documentation catches up to the innovation, not when the calendar says so.
Completeness is cheaper than cadence when paperwork fees are fixed.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Prov 12 is open on purpose â€” 18 innovations wait for their formal documentation before the filing closes.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: 11 provisionals already filed (Prov 1-11), covering innovations up to #2130. Sessions B071-B075 produced 18 new innovations (#2131-#2148) including the Spice Rack (#2142), Recipe Pot (#2143), Scheduled Viewing Beacons (#2145), Shared Scheduling Primitive (#2146), All the Pudding TV Guide (#2147), and Temporal Content Architecture (#2148).
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The choice: file Prov 12 now with 2,130 innovations, then Prov 13 next month with 18 more. Or hold Prov 12 open until all 18 have A&A formals, then file once comprehensively. Cost difference: same $65 micro-entity fee either way. 97 A&A formal documents cover #1912-#2130 â€” the gap is real and documentable. Filing before the formals are written means filing without structured claims that make innovations defensible.

wading_diving_in_layer: The completeness-over-cadence principle reveals the structural advantage of not having investors: startups file quickly because speed signals momentum to investors. Liana Banyan has no investors to signal. The Founder can optimize for filing quality rather than filing frequency â€” a competitive advantage invisible to anyone measuring patent count over time.

title: The Backer Election
canonical_topic: The Backer Election presents three irrevocable contribution options (Gift, Credits, Community Fund) that create legal clarity through permanent member-chosen purpose assignment on every funding flow.
definition: When backing a project, members choose Option A (Gift Receipt), Option B (Credits Election at $1=1 Credit), or Option C (Community Fund). The election is irrevocable by design, protecting both member and cooperative while satisfying regulatory classification requirements.
founder_voice_quotes: Your election is irrevocable. Once you choose, it cannot be changed. This is not a limitation â€” it is a protection.
No fine print. No hidden terms. One choice, clearly explained, permanently recorded.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three choices. One pick. Permanent. Your money is what you say it is.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Option A: gift, no return. Option B: Credits at $1=1 Credit, spendable on platform services (not investments, not appreciating). Option C: community fund, not charitable deduction. All three are clearly defined to satisfy regulatory classification: Credits are not securities, gifts are not investments.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The irrevocability serves three interests simultaneously: for the member (terms locked in), for the cooperative (every dollar has documented purpose), for regulators (clear classification demonstrated). The five-step process (find project -> choose amount -> select option -> confirm with digital signature -> election permanently recorded) is the anti-fine-print funding flow.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Backer Election is a regulatory architecture primitive: it resolves at the moment of contribution the legal classification of every dollar that enters the cooperative. This eliminates the classification ambiguity that creates liability for most crowdfunding platforms.
ratification_chronology: B075 integration (April 4, 2026); original vault source predates B075

title: There Is No Spoon (B076 academic version)
canonical_topic: The academic cooperative case that temporal scheduling is a platform primitive that should be distributed to all user roles, with a research agenda to test appointment-based vs. feed-based consumption.
definition: Paper 6 (Temporal Content Architecture) argues that any cooperative platform must distribute the scheduling primitive to avoid replicating operator time-monopolies under new ownership. The Shared Scheduling Primitive exposes operators, creators, and consumers to the same UI component.
founder_voice_quotes: Time is a primitive like any other.
Cooperative platforms exist to distribute what extractive platforms concentrate.
Cooperatively-owned platforms that still run algorithmic feeds their members cannot schedule replicate the monopoly under new ownership.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Time is a platform primitive â€” cooperative architecture must distribute it to avoid replicating operator monopolies under new ownership.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three research hypotheses: H1 (members using Viewing Beacons show higher content completion), H2 (beacon-scheduled content produces deeper engagement), H3 (members with active beacons show lower 90-day churn). Based on Gollwitzer implementation intentions research and Ericsson deliberate practice.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Scheduling is one of the most powerful primitives a platform owns â€” when a platform monopolizes it, members lose control not just of "what" they see but "when," and "when" determines whether information becomes practice. Literature on cooperative platforms has focused on ownership, revenue, governance; it has ignored time. TCA is a distributed scheduling primitive as cooperative principle. The paper is published so other cooperatives can replicate the pattern.

wading_diving_in_layer: The B076 academic version provides the theoretical framework that the B075 design version (Pudding #123) and the shipped version (Pudding #133) implement. It also introduces a replicability commitment: the architecture is published so other cooperatives can steal it, making the pattern a commons rather than a moat.
ratification_chronology: B076 (April 4, 2026); companion to Paper 6 full draft
empirical_receipts: Research agenda: 12 months of data required

title: Battery Dispatch
canonical_topic: The Universal Remote / Battery Dispatch system enables cooperative members to dispatch content to 12 social platforms in one tap for $0.42/month vs. competitors charging $15-$739/month.
definition: Battery Dispatch is a three-system workflow (Plugs for OAuth connections, Circle in a Square Hole for per-platform adaptation, Dispatch button for staged multi-platform publishing) with Stamp-to-Send approval and Mark rewards, priced at $5/year through cooperative economics.
composing_primitives: Circle in a Square Hole adapter
founder_voice_quotes: The most expensive social media management tool in the world costs $739 a month. Ours costs forty-two cents.
We optimize for trust.
Where they have shareholders, we have members.
Connect your Plugs. Hit Dispatch. Change the math.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: One button dispatches your content to 12 platforms â€” for $0.42/month, because the savings go to you, not shareholders.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three systems: 1) Plugs â€” connect each social account once via OAuth (12 platforms: TikTok, Instagram, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube, Bluesky, Threads, Mastodon, Discord, Substack, Imgur). 2) Circle in a Square Hole â€” auto-adapts content for each platform. 3) Dispatch â€” staged send with per-platform preview, Stamp approval, 30-second undo window.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Stamp-to-Send model is the trust differentiator: every dispatch is staged (compose -> review per-platform previews -> stamp each platform -> send). Every stamp is logged with timestamp, platform, and content snapshot. Dispatch respects platform ToS with built-in delays. Marks rewards: +2 per platform dispatched (up to +10 for 5 platforms). Buffer at $100/month has investors to pay; Hootsuite at $739/month has investors + sales team + advertising + bonuses. The $5/year is the literal infrastructure cost divided by members.

wading_diving_in_layer: Battery Dispatch is the cooperative economics argument made visible in a comparison table: Buffer $100/month, Hootsuite $739/month, Liana Banyan $0.42/month. The 1,700x price difference is not a discount â€” it is what the platform costs when no one is extracting margin beyond Cost+20%.
ratification_chronology: B075 integration (April 4, 2026); vault source predates B075

title: How Liana Banyan Works (B076 canonical)
canonical_topic: The fundamental cooperative mechanism: Cost+20% margin locked by operating agreement ensuring 83.3% creator share, structurally channeled into the Sweet Sixteen initiatives through three commercial portals.
definition: On every transaction, the platform takes Cost+20% and the worker keeps 83.3% ($416.67 on a $500 transaction). This is locked in the operating agreement â€” no board vote or investor can raise it. Medallions record contribution without tradability; HexIsle enables distributed desktop manufacturing.
founder_voice_quotes: The economics come first, and everything else is consequence.
What cannot be traded cannot be extracted.
Good judgment comes from bad judgment, and most people cannot afford real-world bad judgment.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Cost + 20%. Locked. $500 -> $416.67 to the creator, 83.3%, always, by bylaw.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three commercial portals (.com, .biz, .net) channel the 16.7% platform margin into Sweet Sixteen initiatives â€” food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, civic response, education, HR, retail, business incubation, arts. Medallions are blockchain-anchored contribution receipts: not tradable, not redeemable, not speculatable.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: HexIsle is the manufacturing unlock: a modular water-powered physical computing game (hydraulics, pneumatics, magnetic coupling, no batteries) designed for desktop 3D printing. One person with a desktop printer can produce components at a fraction of traditional cost. The $5 membership exists mostly for legal age verification. Members start ventures in a live economy, lose small amounts instead of their house, and learn business fundamentals through operation.

wading_diving_in_layer: The constitutional lock on Cost+20% is the most important and undertheorized platform feature: it converts the success-vs-extraction tension that destroys most platforms from a governance problem into an architecture problem. Once locked, success cannot be expressed as higher margins â€” only as more people benefiting from the same fixed split.

title: The Captain System
canonical_topic: Captains are community-grown sales ambassadors who onboard local businesses through the Moses Model progression (Walking Billboard -> Apprentice -> Captain -> Senior Captain) with geographic corridors and Calling Cards.
definition: The Captain System grows local cooperative ambassadors from the community itself â€” not hired salespeople but neighbors talking to neighbors. Captains use Pitch Packets with real numbers (83.3% to businesses), Tiered Commitment Charts (C+20 through C+90), and QR Calling Cards via Durin's Door.
founder_voice_quotes: That trust cannot be hired. It can only be earned.
A Captain is a neighbor talking to a neighbor.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Captains are not hired â€” they are grown from the community, neighbor by neighbor.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Four stages: Walking Billboard (visible, scan-earning), Apprentice (shadowing, learning the pitch), Captain (own territory, dashboard, Pedestal), Senior Captain (training new Apprentices). Captains carry a Pitch Packet with local member stats and the Cost+20% value proposition â€” showing restaurant owners a better deal than DoorDash/UberEats/Yelp.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Calling Card is a QR-coded personalized access card via Durin's Door â€” hand it to a restaurant owner and they scan straight to their onboarding flow; hand it to a neighbor and they scan to membership signup. The Tiered Commitment Chart (C+20 through C+90) is the negotiation framework for restaurant margin negotiations. The Pedestal is the Captain's public leadership profile with community support signals. Innovations #1963, #1966, #1975, #1978, #1985, #1988 are referenced.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Moses Model (leaders emerge from the community they serve) is the Captain System founding principle â€” trust is built-in rather than bolted-on. A Captain who eats at the restaurants they onboard and lives in the corridor they serve has credibility no hired salesperson can replicate.
ratification_chronology: B075 integration (April 4, 2026); vault source predates B075

title: There Is No Spoon, Shipped
canonical_topic: The Temporal Content Architecture went from academic paper to production in under 12 hours, shipping the All the Pudding TV Guide, Scheduling Entry Box component, and all_cephas_content database view.
definition: Paper 6 (TCA) was drafted at 10am, Pudding at noon, Knight prompt by 3pm, production at 6pm. Innovation #2148 (Temporal Content Architecture) became live code the same day it was theorized â€” demonstrating the cooperative AI build loop (Bishop + Knight + Pawn + Founder).
founder_voice_quotes: Paper at 10am. Pudding at noon. Knight prompt by 3pm. Production at 6pm.
There is no spoon.
Time is yours, not ours.
cross_references: K275 (All the Pudding TV Guide implementation)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: A paper in the morning became a live production feature by evening â€” the cooperative AI build loop in action.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three shipped artifacts: All the Pudding TV Guide at /cephas/all-the-pudding (Listings / Schedule / Calendar views), Scheduling Entry Box (reusable dialog for member-controlled appointment scheduling), and all_cephas_content database view (unified feed across Puddings, BST episodes, Spoonfuls, Skipping Stones, and news).
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Three-surface TCA (Innovation #2148, Crown Jewel candidate) is now live code. Surfaces 1 (Staff Broadcast Schedule) and 2 (Cue Card Battery Dispatch) existed previously and need migration to the shared SchedulingEntryBox. Surface 3 (Scheduled Viewing Beacon, #2146) is the new contribution. The empirical research agenda (H1-H3 from Paper 6) runs against live member data over the next 12 months.

wading_diving_in_layer: The paper-to-production loop (Bishop drafts -> Knight builds -> Pawn verifies -> Founder holds vision) is itself an Innovation: a cooperative AI team executing a design pattern into infrastructure in a single business day, with each agent doing what they do best and no agent waiting for permission from another.

title: Cold Start Hub
canonical_topic: The Cold Start Hub offers six colored entry paths (Food, Manufacturing, Service, Local Business, Guild, Tribe) tailored to distinct member motivations, each leading into the full cooperative economy.
definition: Six doors into the cooperative: Orange (Food), Slate (Manufacturing), Blue (Service), Emerald (Local Business), Purple (Guild/professional), Gold (Tribe/personal). Each path answers a different "why should I join?" â€” because not everyone joins a cooperative for the same reason.
founder_voice_quotes: One door in. The whole cooperative opens up.
Six paths means six different "aha" moments.
Not everyone joins a cooperative for the same reason.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Six doors in â€” food, making, service, business, profession, community â€” one cooperative behind all of them.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Orange: meal plans, local restaurants, Breakfast Runner nodes. Slate: Canister System, HexIsle terrain, 3D printing. Blue: skill marketplace, Cost+20%. Emerald: Captain shows business owner the math vs. DoorDash/UberEats. Purple: Guild with treasury, volume discounts. Gold: Tribe with Family Table, treasury.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Cold Start Hub solves the chicken-and-egg problem by offering six distinct first-value-delivery mechanisms. A single mom who joins for cheaper groceries (Orange) discovers the service marketplace. A maker who joins for injection molding (Slate) discovers the Guild system. A restaurant owner who joins for lower delivery costs (Emerald) discovers the full cooperative network. Three questions narrow the path match; the platform tracks progress automatically.

wading_diving_in_layer: The six-path architecture solves onboarding's fundamental problem: different people have different activation energies for different value propositions. By offering six distinct entry points, each optimized for a specific motivation, the Cold Start Hub maximizes the probability that any given person finds their door.
ratification_chronology: B075 integration (April 4, 2026); vault source predates B075

title: Ghost World
canonical_topic: Ghost World is the hex-tile visual discovery layer of the cooperative economy where every member storefront is a map tile, browsable as a fully populated community market.
definition: Ghost World displays the cooperative as a hex grid of tiles â€” each tile a real member, business, or service. Members get one free storefront, can deploy Pop-Up Kiosks in other neighborhoods, and discover offerings via the Cross-Island Discovery Feed and Deck Cards connected to the Loteria reward system.
founder_voice_quotes: Ghost World is the visual layer of the cooperative economy.
It is not a game. It is a visual way to discover what your community has to offer.
cross_references: PUDDING_133 (Cold Start Hub â€” entry paths lead here)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Ghost World is a hex map of the cooperative economy â€” your neighbors' stores visible as tiles, discoverable without an account.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Every member gets one free storefront tile (name, offerings, prices in Credits, photos, live-synced with real-world presence). Pop-Up Kiosks let businesses test new markets without commitment. The Cross-Island Discovery Feed shows trending storefronts, new arrivals, and personalized matches.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The hex grid is not a game mechanic â€” it is a geographic metaphor made visual. Closer tiles = closer in real life. Busier tiles glow brighter. Ghost World is the unauthenticated browse experience showing what the platform looks like when populated â€” critical for cold-start conversion. Prospective members see the cooperative as if it already exists in their neighborhood. WildFire Tour mode (demonstration) shows a populated map; Real Data mode starts empty for new members.

wading_diving_in_layer: Ghost World converts the cold-start problem into a discovery experience: by showing prospective members a hex map representing what the cooperative could look like in their area, it makes the value proposition concrete rather than abstract. The map is the promise made visual.
ratification_chronology: B075 integration (April 4, 2026); vault source predates B075

title: Anyone Can Be a Harper
canonical_topic: Harper Guild replaces journalism's credential gatekeeping with five transparent operational disciplines anyone can earn through verified bounty work.
definition: Harper Guild grants journalism status through five operational disciplines â€” verifiability, correction discipline, independence declaration, peer review, and subject-matter transparency â€” not institutional credentials. Anyone can climb four tiers (Cub Harper, Stamped Harper, Harper, Master Harper) by completing verified bounties while keeping 83.3% of earnings.
founder_voice_quotes: The truth-telling function does not belong to the people who can pay to credential it. It belongs to the people who do the work well enough to have it verified.
Because a cooperative platform that says 'member ownership' but leaves journalism to external institutions is cooperative-adjacent, not cooperative.
The bus driver is coming.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Journalism qualified by verified work, not institutional credentials.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Five criteria replace degree requirements. Four tiers â€” Cub, Stamped, Harper, Master â€” each earned on-ledger from verified bounties and losable if discipline fails. Economic fix: 83.3% creator bounty keep, zero advertising revenue.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Traditional journalism collapsed on two axes: advertising revenue and closed credentialing pipelines. Harper Guild rebuilds both from scratch. Peer attestation weights by rater reputation so false stamps damage the stamper. The cooperative runs its own fact-checking infrastructure â€” embedded in cooperative bylaws rather than outsourced to collapsing media institutions. A bus driver with 200 verified checks can reach Master Harper status through operational excellence alone, not institutional affiliation.

wading_diving_in_layer: Journalism as cooperative infrastructure â€” the information a cooperative runs on (ethics audits, fact-checks, accountability stories) must live inside the cooperative's own bylaws, not depend on external institutions that are themselves collapsing.

title: The Guest Marks Wallet
canonical_topic: Prospective members earn real platform Marks in a 90-day temporary wallet before committing to membership, solving legal no-purchase-necessary compliance and conversion trust simultaneously.
definition: The Guest Marks Wallet holds Marks earned by non-members for 90 days, allowing participation in contests and challenges without a membership fee. Upon joining at $5/year, the full Guest Wallet balance transfers automatically to the member account with no value lost.
founder_voice_quotes: Try Before You Join.
Saw a challenge that looks interesting? Jump in. You do not need to be a member yet.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Earn real Marks before joining; wallet converts automatically on signup.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Solves two problems at once: legal compliance (genuine no-purchase-necessary path for prize contests) and conversion trust (let people earn real value before committing five dollars). One wallet per email. Marks held 90 days, expire if unused.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Guest Wallet is an architectural bridge between non-member participation and member conversion. By making earned value transferable at signup, it removes the psychological barrier of committing to a platform unseen. It also satisfies prize-promotion legal requirements by creating a genuine alternative entry path â€” the platform gains legal standing for contests while generating a pre-committed member cohort who already understand the value proposition through lived experience rather than marketing claims.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Guest Wallet transforms the five-dollar membership decision from a leap of faith into a ratification of already-experienced value â€” the most powerful conversion mechanism because it replaces persuasion with proof.

title: We Need What You're Good At
canonical_topic: What-If Commissions are pre-drafted cooperative authority grants the Founder deploys spontaneously mid-conversation when unexpected talent is recognized, collapsing recognition and deployment into a single act.
definition: What-If Commissions are flexible, pre-authorized role contracts the Founder carries and activates on-the-spot, eliminating the gap between recognizing talent and deploying it. The commission is multi-dimensional (time, scope, project, and decision-fractional) and triggers automatic platform routing updates upon signature.
founder_voice_quotes: We Need What You're Good At.
What if we meet someone of unusual ability who would meet higher expectations? That person is you.
The recognition and the deployment are the same act.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Commission given on-the-spot the moment talent is recognized in conversation.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Military battlefield commission model applied to cooperative platform. Pre-drafted templates exist for unanticipated people. Four-dimensional scope (time, scope, project, decision) lets the recipient set commitments without abandoning other roles. Platform fires automatically: profile updates, routing shifts, personalized Red Carpet activates, Crowns notified.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Traditional organizations hemorrhage extraordinary people in the paperwork gap between recognition and formal deployment. What-If Commissions compress this gap to zero. Pre-authorization means authority exists before the conversation â€” the document waits for the person, not the reverse. Netherlands fractional framework extended to four dimensions (time, scope, project, decision) makes LB commissions more flexible than any employment structure. The window of 'I just realized this person is right' is also the window of authority transfer â€” recognition and deployment are the same act.

wading_diving_in_layer: The What-If Commission is the cooperative's structural answer to talent evaporation â€” extraordinary people are never lost to bureaucratic lag because the infrastructure for recognizing them was built before they arrived.

title: The LB Card
canonical_topic: The LB Card is a Stripe-powered prepaid cooperative payment card where Credits spent route 83.3% to receiving businesses, support scheduled and community-funded loading, and automate charitable contributions on every transaction.
definition: The LB Card is a Stripe-powered prepaid card loading Credits (one dollar equals one Credit, one-way conversion) for spending at participating cooperative businesses. It supports scheduled auto-funding, community-sponsored loading by guilds and tribes, and automatic charitable donation linking on every transaction.
founder_voice_quotes: Everyone wins except the extractive middleman.
Guaranteed demand is the most powerful negotiating tool in commerce.
This is not charity. It is cooperative economics.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Prepaid cooperative card where spending keeps 83.3% with the local provider.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Stripe-powered. One dollar equals one Credit, one-way (no cashout). 83.3% to the receiving merchant, Cost+20% to platform. Scheduled funding from 500-plus members creates predictable demand that Captains leverage for volume discounts. Guilds and Tribes can fund each other's cards cooperatively. Every swipe can route a percentage to charity automatically.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The LB Card mechanizes cooperative purchasing power at scale. Predictable weekly loading across member cohorts creates guaranteed demand â€” the strongest negotiating lever in commerce â€” letting Captains secure better terms with local businesses. Community-funded cards (Guild-to-member, Tribe-to-member) replace charity logic with cooperative investment: money circulates locally rather than extracting to payment processor headquarters. Charity linking requires zero behavioral change from the member. The card is precisely defined: not a credit card (no debt), not a bank account (no deposits), not an investment (no appreciation) â€” cooperative service currency.

wading_diving_in_layer: Scheduled, predictable cooperative purchasing turns individual spending acts into collective bargaining power â€” the LB Card is simultaneously a payment instrument and a negotiation tool for every Captain dealing with local businesses.

title: Marks Payback
canonical_topic: Active members who earn 100 or more Marks in their membership year have their five-dollar renewal automatically drawn from their own earned Credits, making consistent participation self-funding.
definition: Marks Payback is an automatic weekly renewal check: members with 100 or more Marks earned in their membership year and five or more Credits have their membership renewed automatically from their Credit balance. No application or approval required â€” participation earns re-entry.
founder_voice_quotes: Participate, earn Marks, earn Credits, membership renews automatically, keep participating.
The cooperative says: You have earned this. Your membership is on us.
Just keep contributing. When your renewal comes up, Marks Payback handles the rest.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Earn 100 Marks and your membership pays for itself automatically.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: System runs weekly checking members whose membership expires within 7 days. If 100-plus Marks and 5-plus Credits: automatic renewal, member notified, Credits deducted. Cooperative still earns operating revenue but those Credits originated from the member's own participation â€” work pays membership.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Marks Payback closes the cooperative flywheel loop at the membership layer. The five-dollar fee is never a barrier for active members â€” it becomes a natural output of engagement. The 100-Mark threshold represents roughly a few months of moderate activity, achievable without gaming. Credits are deducted (maintaining operating revenue integrity), but they originated from the member's work, so the effective cost is zero for contributors. This elegantly solves the retention problem: the most engaged members (highest value) face the lowest renewal friction, creating a self-reinforcing participation loop.

wading_diving_in_layer: Marks Payback makes active membership economically self-perpetuating â€” the cooperative's best contributors retain membership without conscious financial decision, aligning member retention precisely with cooperative health.

title: MoneyPenny the Receptionist
canonical_topic: MoneyPenny is the platform's AI gatekeeper that routes inbound contacts through a four-tier trust hierarchy, protecting member attention while maintaining professional responsiveness across all interaction types.
definition: MoneyPenny handles first contact screening through four tiers: Known Contacts (immediate pass-through), Platform Members (fast response with context), Verified Externals (queued acknowledgment), and Unknown contacts (professional auto-response plus screening queue). Members control contacts lists, templates, filtering preferences, and availability windows.
founder_voice_quotes: MoneyPenny handles all of this. Quietly. Professionally. And you decide how much of it you ever see.
Your people are your people.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: AI receptionist routes contacts by trust tier so members stay reachable without being overwhelmed.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Four tiers: Known Contacts pass through immediately, Platform Members receive context-rich fast response, Verified Externals are queued for review, Unknown contacts receive professional auto-response and enter screening queue. Members control all variables: contacts list, templates, filter aggressiveness, availability windows.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: MoneyPenny solves the attention-economy problem for cooperative platform members who have business, creative, and personal inbound streams simultaneously. Captains onboarding businesses need to screen serious inquiries from spam and competitor intelligence gathering. Creators need FAQ coverage without manual response burden. Members living their lives need dinner without platform interruptions. MoneyPenny's four-tier architecture handles all three use cases with member-controlled granularity â€” the platform respects member attention as a finite cooperative resource.

wading_diving_in_layer: MoneyPenny makes professional responsiveness scalable for individual members â€” converting the normally zero-sum trade-off between availability and attention into a managed, member-controlled trust funnel.

title: The Pathfinder Journal
canonical_topic: PathFinder is a journal-based platform tool that discovers member vocational strengths by watching what they choose and complete, then reflects patterns back as opportunity connections rather than prescriptions.
definition: PathFinder is a growing journal that tracks member activity across four steps: free writing, challenge completion, pattern emergence, and opportunity threading. Rather than asking what members want, it watches what they finish and reflects those patterns as Guild openings, bounties, and Treasure Map career paths.
founder_voice_quotes: It is not an algorithm deciding for you. It is a mirror showing you what you have already decided.
PathFinder is available in your Helm. Start your first journal entry anytime.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Platform journal that surfaces vocational patterns from what you do, not what you say you want.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Four steps: start writing freely (prompts provided), complete discipline-specific challenges (design, logistics, communication, technical, creative), see patterns emerge over time, follow threads to real platform opportunities. The system watches choices and completions, not stated preferences.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Traditional job platforms require self-knowledge most people lack â€” especially career changers, re-entrants, and young people. PathFinder inverts the requirement: it generates self-knowledge from behavioral evidence. Challenge completion data aggregates into a pattern profile that the member cannot fake because it reflects actual choices. The mirror metaphor is architecturally precise â€” PathFinder reflects without prescribing, enabling member agency to remain sovereign while reducing the cognitive load of vocational self-discovery.

wading_diving_in_layer: PathFinder solves vocational uncertainty not by asking people what they want but by watching what they keep choosing â€” behavior as more reliable data than stated preference, because behavior is honest in ways that self-reporting rarely is.

title: Roommate Accountability
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's cooperative housing accountability system uses member-chosen commitment tiers, Marks escrow, photo-evidenced Stamp complaints, and reciprocally weighted reputation scoring to make shared living transparent and self-reinforcing rather than surveillance-based.
definition: The Roommate Accountability System lets members choose their own commitment level across five household duties and four frequency tiers. A 10-Marks-per-week escrow backs those commitments; valid complaints forfeit Marks to the cooperative fund (not the complainer); Steward mediation handles contested Stamps; and a four-factor Roommate Score drives placement priority.
founder_voice_quotes: Whatever you commit to, you do â€” and the benefits follow.
This isn't surveillance â€” it's a system where everyone knows the rules up front.
It's not a punishment system. It's a promise system.
cross_references: Scholz and Schneider 2016 Ours to Hack and to Own

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Choose your own household commitments; Marks escrow enforces them without surveillance.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Five duties (dishwashing, garbage, kitchen, bathroom, common areas) each with four frequency tiers (Daily, Every-Other-Day, 3x/Week, Weekly). 10 Marks/week escrowed. Valid complaint forfeits to cooperative fund â€” not the complainer (eliminates perverse incentive). 30 Marks/month cap. Reciprocal grading: rater credibility weighted by rater's own score.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The system's core insight is Ostrom-compatible: commons governance requires commitment clarity, transparent rules, and graduated sanctions. By routing forfeited Marks to the cooperative fund rather than the complainer, LB eliminates the economic incentive for malicious complaints. Reciprocal grading (rater credibility weighted by rater reputation) self-corrects for bad actors without administrator intervention. The voluntary commitment tier selection means the system rewards ambition but never punishes humility â€” a weekly commitment done consistently builds a great Roommate Score over time. High-score applicants attract high-score housing, naturally sorting the cooperative toward reliability.

wading_diving_in_layer: The most profound architectural choice is that forfeited Marks go to the cooperative fund rather than the complainant â€” this single design decision eliminates the perverse incentive that destroys every other accountability system and makes the entire structure self-correcting.

title: You're in Charge of You
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's foundational value proposition is that existing economic activity can be rerouted through a cooperative structure where 83.3% stays with workers and zero percent extracts to shareholders or advertisers.
definition: The platform proposes an economic revolution through structural change, not behavioral change: the same transactions that already happen route through Cost+20% cooperative infrastructure where creators keep 83.3%, zero goes to shareholders or advertising, and members govern their own work via participation-weighted democracy. The five-dollar membership is the entry cost to cooperative ownership.
founder_voice_quotes: I propose an economic revolution, simply by doing what we already do, with a slightly different structure. Namely, YOU being in charge of YOU.
Where does the money come from? Where it always has â€” we're just not greedy about it.
Help each other help ourselves.
The laborer is worthy of his hire.
We're not inventing something. We're remembering something. And building it with better tools.
I'm a Patriotic Interdependentalist.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The revolution is structural, not behavioral: same money, different ownership of the margin.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Cost+20% vs. traditional 15-45% platform take. 83.3% to workers vs. 30-60% on traditional platforms. Zero to shareholders or advertising. Five dollars per year, free with participation through Marks Payback. Zero demographic tracking by default. Participation-weighted governance (300 System) not dollar-weighted. LB Card converts cooperative earnings to real spending power.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Patriotic Interdependentalism is the Founder's philosophical synthesis: not independence (go it alone), not dependence (someone above you), but interdependence (your success is my success). The cooperative tradition â€” credit unions (1909), farmer co-ops, mutual aid societies, barn raisings â€” is the most American economic tradition, predating the corporate extraction model. LB rebuilds it with better tools. The radical claim is that no behavioral change is required: the same groceries, same meals, same services â€” just routed through a structure where the worker keeps what they earn.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight is that cooperativism is not radical invention but radical remembering â€” the economic pattern of mutual aid is older than corporate capitalism, and LB's architecture is its digital implementation with 37 years of design precision behind it.

title: The Closet Belongs to the Worker
canonical_topic: In response to AI labor displacement, Liana Banyan inverts the traditional employment ownership structure so that the workspace (Helm), the tools, the output, and the platform itself all belong to the worker rather than the employer.
definition: Molly Kinder's 2026 warning that closet-computer workers face AI displacement frames LB's counter-architecture: the Helm (worker's permanent workspace), cooperative tools (Canister System, Beacon network, Cephas), 83.3% output retention, and member-owned corporate structure eliminate every layer of employer ownership that AI disruption is now exploiting.
founder_voice_quotes: The closet belongs to the worker. The computer belongs to the worker. The output belongs to the worker. And the platform that hosts the closet â€” that belongs to the worker too.
The closet has a door. The door only locks from the outside.
Permanent changes need permanent architecture.
The worker's participation history IS the asset.
Not a safety net. Not wage insurance. Not UBI. A member-owned economic operating system.
cross_references: Molly Kinder Brookings Institution NYT April 3 2026

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The closet, the computer, the output, and the platform itself all belong to the worker on LB.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three inversion layers: (1) Helm is worker-owned, non-revocable, no landlord; (2) Cooperative tools (Canister, Beacon, Cephas) are co-owned utilities not licensed from a vendor; (3) 83.3% output retention written into Structural Bylaws, not marketing copy. Fourth layer: the platform itself is member-owned Wyoming C-Corp â€” no venture exit, no secondary market for control.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Kinder's quote illuminates that the traditional employment closet has four things belonging to the employer: the space, the tools, the output, and the distribution. AI disruption is not destroying work â€” it is destroying the leverage workers had when only humans could do closet work. LB's response inverts all four layers simultaneously. Marks and Joules are the permanent-shock currencies: Marks track effort differentials that persist across platform absence, Joules stamp cooperative surplus as non-expiring assets. The member's participation history IS the asset â€” it does not depreciate with the next layoff cycle and does not require employer permission to exist.

wading_diving_in_layer: The platform's deepest claim against AI displacement: permanent changes require permanent architecture, and permanent architecture means the participation record belongs to the worker rather than to any employer who can terminate the lease.

title: Permanent Shock, Permanent Architecture
canonical_topic: If AI labor displacement is a permanent rather than transitory shock, every traditional safety net built on transitory-shock assumptions fails, and the required response is member-owned contribution history that cannot depreciate when employers disappear.
definition: Anton Korinek's 2026 declaration that the safety net was built for shocks that pass frames LB's three-currency architecture as the only adequate response: Joules (forever stamps on surplus), Marks (permanent effort records), and Credits (stable service currency) form a contribution floor the worker owns independently of any employer.
founder_voice_quotes: A permanent shock needs permanent architecture. Joules don't expire. Marks compound. Contribution history is the floor.
You cannot paper a permanent shock with transitory tools.
The worker's leverage is their own accumulated contribution, held in a cooperative structure the worker partly owns.
The record of your contribution is the asset, and no employer ever owned it in the first place.
cross_references: Anton Korinek UVA to Anthropic NYT April 3 2026
Circle 2 letter to Anton Korinek pre Yale Symposium
Pudding #152 The Closet Belongs to the Worker (paired)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Permanent labor displacement requires permanent currency architecture â€” Joules, Marks, and contribution history that outlast any employer.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Korinek's framework: unemployment insurance assumes the next job is coming; retraining assumes new skills transfer; Social Security assumes a full working life; wage insurance assumes employer stability. All assume shocks pass. Joules (non-expiring surplus stamps), Marks (effort differentials that compound across platform absence), and Credits (stable plumbing) address the permanent case.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The critical distinction from UBI and wage insurance: LB's architecture is not a government transfer or employer guarantee â€” both of which remain vulnerable to political pressure and employer insolvency. It is a member-owned asset that cannot be politically defunded or employer-terminated. Korinek's move from UVA to Anthropic signals that the math is moving in the permanent-shock direction. LB built the architecture before the mainstream economics profession named the problem, emerging from reading what workers said durable means: not disappearing when the employer disappears, compounding rather than resetting, name on the door.

wading_diving_in_layer: The three-currency system (Joules, Marks, Credits) is not a safety net but a permanent-shock operating system â€” the fundamental distinction being that a safety net catches you when the floor disappears, while contribution history IS the floor.

title: The Two Shells
canonical_topic: Liana Banyan's dual-topology architecture classifies every platform surface at build time as FocusShell (conversion-optimized front porch) or AppShell (capability-dense workshop), enforcing ten design rules through component constraints rather than documentation.
definition: FocusShell is the pre-member surface (hero pattern: eyebrow, headline, body, CTA, proof strip) and AppShell is the post-member workspace (sidebar navigation, operational density, Helm and Bridges).
