Classification happens at route level at build time; ten design rules are enforced through component constraints, not guidelines. Classified as Innovation #2151 (FocusShell/AppShell) and #2152 (Design Doctrine), both Crown Jewels.
founder_voice_quotes: Most platforms have one shell. One frame that somehow has to serve the person who just arrived and the person who's been there for three years.
83.3% and Cost+20% appear as trust anchors, not promotional flourishes.
The rules are in the code, not in a wiki nobody reads.
Two shells. One porch, one workshop. Every visitor gets clarity. Every member gets capability.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every platform surface classified at build time as FocusShell (arrival) or AppShell (work).
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: FocusShell: hero pattern with mandatory proof strip (83.3%, Cost+20%) on every conversion page â€” architectural, not marketing. AppShell: full sidebar, Helm and Bridges, operational density. Ten codified rules enforced via component constraints: InformativeLock rejects punitive copy; red color token does not exist; amber-only for negative states.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The innovation is not the separation of marketing from app layouts (many frameworks do this) but the enforcement mechanism: route-level classification made at build time, component-level constraint propagation that makes alternative behavior impossible. A developer cannot accidentally place a sidebar on a conversion page because the architecture prevents it. A designer cannot use red for decline because the design token does not exist. The proof strip (83.3% creator keep, Cost+20% pricing) appears as architecture â€” like an exit sign required by building code â€” on every FocusShell page. The ten rules are not documented preferences; they compile into components.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest architectural claim: economic transparency is load-bearing infrastructure, not marketing copy â€” the same way a building code mandates exit signs, LB's design doctrine mandates economic disclosure on every conversion surface, making it impossible to hide the cooperative's terms from a visitor.

title: The X-Ray
canonical_topic: X-Ray is a member-facing toggle that activates inline annotation overlays on every platform surface, exposing economic mechanics, data flows, governance pathways, and structural connections in real time rather than in external help documentation.
definition: X-Ray is a platform-wide DOM annotation system providing member-facing transparency overlays across five dimensions â€” structure, data flow, economics, governance, and connections â€” activated by a single toggle and accompanied by a structured glossary. Classified as Innovation #2154, a Crown Jewel.
founder_voice_quotes: We built a button that turns the walls transparent.
Architecture that can't be inspected by the people who live in it isn't cooperative â€” it's a landlord who won't show you the lease.
Transparency isn't a feature. It's architecture.
The economics exist. The data flows exist. The margins exist. They just don't have a front door. X-Ray gives them one.
cross_references: No prior art in member-facing platform transparency systems

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Toggle turns walls transparent â€” inline annotations expose economics, data flow, and governance on every page.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Five annotation dimensions on every surface: structure (what this component is), data flow (where data goes), economics (who pays and earns), governance (who can change this), connections (what surfaces this links to). Every term links to glossary. Mascot changes state when X-Ray activates. Not a help page â€” contextual, inline, on the actual surface.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The technology is not novel (developer tools expose DOM, accessibility inspectors expose structure) but the audience is: X-Ray is the first member-facing toggle that exposes economic mechanics and governance pathways on production cooperative surfaces. Facebook's content removal decisions, YouTube's algorithm signals, Twitter's appeals â€” all invisible to users. X-Ray puts the server room behind glass. The barista has the right to see the margin on the coffee; the photographer has the right to see the economics of the bounty. Transparency as architecture means it cannot be removed by a PR decision.

wading_diving_in_layer: X-Ray operationalizes the claim that a cooperative architecture must be inspectable by its members â€” transparency is not a feature that can be offered or withdrawn but a structural property that defines whether a platform is genuinely cooperative or merely cooperative-adjacent.

title: The Intent Field
canonical_topic: Battery Dispatch requires members to declare their intended change before composing any message, then generates a single canonical message with per-channel edge variations rather than eleven separate drafts, confirmed by the As You Wish solemn stamp.
definition: The Intent Field is a required pre-composition gate in Battery Dispatch: members must articulate the change they want to make before accessing the composer. One canonical message is written; eleven channel-specific deltas are shown (not eleven rewrites); the As You Wish full-screen stamp provides solemn multi-surface confirmation. Classified as Innovation #2158, Crown Jewel.
founder_voice_quotes: Before you can type a single word, the system asks: What change are you trying to make?
Every broadcasting tool starts with the assumption you know what to say. We start with why you're saying it.
One intent. One truth. Eleven surfaces. Zero checkboxes.
As You Wish means: I understand what I am doing, and I choose to do it.
cross_references: No prior art in commercial multi-channel messaging tools
Hootsuite Buffer Sprout Social Mailchimp critique

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Must declare intended change before typing a word; one canonical message, eleven edge variations, solemn confirmation.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Intent Field is friction by design â€” gating the entire composition surface behind a required purpose declaration. One canonical message is written. Eleven channel tiles show only the delta (character truncation, header, subject line). As You Wish takes over the screen: shows canonical message, channels, declared intent, requests deliberate stamp. MoneyPenny reads intent and offers memory suggestions without auto-applying.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Every commercial broadcasting tool optimizes for output quantity: more posts, more channels, more reach. None measures quality of purpose. The Intent Field measures it architecturally â€” you cannot send a message whose purpose you have not articulated. The canonical-message-plus-deltas model also solves message drift across channels: one truth, eleven projections, not eleven variations that gradually diverge from a forgotten original. The As You Wish pattern is the cooperative's answer to checkbox fatigue â€” a solemn full-screen affordance that makes consequential multi-surface actions feel consequential rather than routine.

wading_diving_in_layer: Intent-first architecture changes the measure of communication quality from reach and engagement (output metrics) to clarity of purpose (input metric) â€” the only measure that predicts whether a message actually accomplishes what it was sent to accomplish.

title: The Warm Room
canonical_topic: The Family Table Hub replaces bank-aesthetic family coordination with three architecturally deliberate decisions: XP-only child display enforced at data-fetch level, human-language activity narration, and savings-jar fund visualization.
definition: Family Warm Workspace (Innovation #2161, Crown Jewel) makes three architectural choices: children see XP counters not ADAPT reputation scores (enforced at data-fetch, not CSS); activity feed entries are human sentences (Name, action verb, object) not system log codes; shared funds display as fill-level savings jars not ledger columns.
founder_voice_quotes: Every family app thinks your household is a bank. We built a warm room instead.
Children see XP. Never ADAPT scores. Not hidden â€” unrendered. The query doesn't run.
When Maya finishes a task, the feed says 'Maya completed her reading goal.' Not 'user_id 42 updated task number 1847.'
The savings jar looks like a jar. Not a ledger. Not a dashboard.
Most family tools inherited their activity feed from project management software. We wrote ours for parents.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three deliberate design choices make the family coordination space warm rather than transactional.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: XP-only for minors: the data-fetch query does not run for under-18 members â€” ADAPT score never exists in their interface. Activity feed: every entry is a sentence with first name, action verb, object. Savings jar: shared funds display as visual fill-level containers, not financial columns. All three are architecturally enforced, not style preferences.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Most gamified family apps import adult performance metrics into households â€” star ratings, leaderboards, badges that rank children against each other. The platform says it is motivating; what it is actually doing is inflicting the performance logic of the adult economy on the one space where children should be measured by effort, not output. LB refuses this at the data layer: ADAPT score queries simply do not run for minors. The activity feed was redesigned from scratch for parents rather than inherited from project management software. The savings jar aesthetic signals that this family is building toward something together â€” a relational act, not an accounting exercise.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight is enforcing child-appropriate metrics at the data-fetch layer rather than the display layer â€” ADAPT scores do not exist in the child's interface rather than being hidden, which makes the protection architecturally durable rather than circumventable.

title: The Four Judges
canonical_topic: Star Chamber runs four AI judges with distinct reasoning profiles simultaneously, displaying their full reasoning matrix to disputing parties, with Founder override implemented as a preserved annotation layer rather than a replacement verdict.
definition: Star Chamber dispute resolution deploys four AI judges simultaneously: Oracle (Claude, principled/precedent), Morpheus (Claude, contextual/empathy-weighted), Red Queen (Perplexity, adversarial/stress-testing), and Dredd (Perplexity, rule-literal). Both parties see the full reasoning matrix; convergence and divergence are visually indicated; Founder override is an annotation, not a command.
founder_voice_quotes: When a dispute happens on most platforms, you get a verdict. You don't get the reasoning. We built a courtroom with glass walls.
Dispute resolution where the reasoning is the product, not just the verdict.
Founder override is an annotation layer. It sits alongside judge reasoning. It doesn't replace it.
The escalation ladder is visible. Not buried in Terms of Service.
cross_references: Star Chamber production system (one of 36 live)
No prior art in member-facing multi-agent parallel reasoning display

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Four AI judges with different reasoning profiles display full rationale; override is annotation, not command.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Oracle asks what rule applies; Morpheus asks what fairness looks like given the people involved; Red Queen finds the weakest claim; Dredd checks rule-literal compliance. Reasoning matrix shows four columns with convergence/divergence visual indicators. Founder override adds a timestamped, reasoned annotation alongside â€” not replacing â€” judge responses. Escalation ladder from Standard Review to Star Chamber to Areopagus is visible in the interface.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Automated moderation's fundamental problem is invisible reasoning: you get the verdict but not the logic. Star Chamber puts the server room behind glass â€” both parties see exactly how four distinct reasoning profiles evaluated their case. The annotation-layer override is the most important governance design: it makes human intervention transparent and accountable rather than invisible and authoritarian. Founder reasoning sits alongside judge reasoning; members can see where they agree and where they diverge. Prior art (developer tools, accessibility inspectors) served technicians. Star Chamber serves disputing community members.

wading_diving_in_layer: The annotation-layer override design is the most consequential governance innovation â€” it makes human authority transparent and contextual rather than opaque and absolute, which is the difference between legitimate authority and arbitrary power.

title: The Seven Threads
canonical_topic: The Liana Banyan calendar organizes cooperative life into seven semantic threads displayed as a braid, with exactly one weekly suggestion framed as a question rather than a prescription, and language constraints that enforce non-judgmental framing.
definition: The Braided Thread Calendar (Innovation #2159, Crown Jewel) shows seven color-and-icon coded threads (Personal, Family, Business, Coalition, Route, Defense, Education) woven into a week view. A Week Reflection Panel auto-summarizes time distribution. The PatternHintCard surfaces exactly one adjustment suggestion per week, always framed as a question. Language constraints: amber not red, Room to Adjust not Declining.
founder_voice_quotes: Your calendar lies to you. It shows you boxes on a grid. It never says: here is your life.
Do you want to keep saying yes to this pattern?
Every productivity tool assumes you want to be optimized. We refused.
A calendar that tells the truth about your time â€” and then asks, gently, once, whether that's what you meant.
The platform doesn't know why you spent six hours on coalition. Your job is to decide what the pattern means.
cross_references: Prior art: Google Calendar, Notion Calendar, RescueTime, Toggl (all grid-based or post-hoc)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Seven semantic threads braided into a week view; exactly one question-framed suggestion per week.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Seven threads each with color AND icon (color alone fails accessibility). Week Reflection Panel shows raw thread distribution: six hours coalition, zero education, two housing â€” no judgment, no red flags. PatternHintCard fires exactly once per week with a question, never a prescription. Each thread wires to existing platform data (Family Table, Storefront, Rideshare Routes, etc.) â€” not manual entry.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The productivity tool industry has a shared disease: it assumes users want to be optimized toward factory-management efficiency metrics. Every tool shows how you are falling short. LB refuses this design philosophy at the architectural level: the calendar is a mirror not a coach, the suggestion is one question not a ranked list, the language tokens for decline states are amber-only and room-to-grow-only. The PatternHintCard's core question â€” Do you want to keep saying yes to this pattern â€” is the entire design philosophy in a sentence: the platform reflects what you are doing and asks once whether that is what you intended, then stops.

wading_diving_in_layer: Limiting suggestions to exactly one per week, framed as a question rather than a recommendation, is the most important design constraint â€” it preserves member agency while providing the minimal useful reflection signal, refusing the optimization trap that makes every other productivity tool feel like surveillance.

title: The Ratchet
canonical_topic: The Ratchet is the architectural principle that Credits, Marks, and Joules can only accumulate and never depreciate, enforced by the one-way Credit conversion, Cost+20% pricing floor, and cooperative ownership structure that removes any external incentive to dilute earned value.
definition: The Ratchet holds that completed work is a permanent fact and therefore the currencies representing that work cannot depreciate: Credits are locked at earn-time value (one-way valve, Cost+20% floor, zero inflation mechanisms), Marks are permanent effort records that survive platform absence, and Joules are non-expiring surplus stamps.
founder_voice_quotes: Completed work does not un-happen. Credits represent completed work. Therefore Credits do not lose value.
The Ratchet is not a promise â€” it is a mechanical constraint. One direction. Forward. Always.
Three currencies. One direction. Forward.
The system literally cannot do the thing that would reduce your Credits' value.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Three currencies only accumulate â€” architectural constraints make depreciation mechanically impossible.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Credits: locked at earn-time value by one-way conversion (no cash-out path eliminates speculative pressure) plus Cost+20% floor (prices cannot collapse) plus member-owned governance (no external group with dilution incentive). Marks: permanent â€” walk away for five years, return, Marks still exist. Joules: non-expiring surplus stamps. Together: predictability for members who earn slowly â€” a retiree earning 30 Credits/month fixing appliances cannot absorb 15% devaluation.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Every other stored-value system includes at least one depreciation mechanism: dollar inflation (Fed targets 2%), airline miles redemption chart changes, loyalty point expiry, gift card inactivity fees. LB eliminates all three mechanisms simultaneously: no external pressure from cash-out speculation (one-way valve), no price collapse (Cost+20% floor), no ownership group with dilution incentive (cooperative structure). The Ratchet is not a promise â€” it is a mechanical impossibility of the alternative. This protects most critically the members who earn slowly and cannot absorb value loss.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Ratchet's deepest claim: currencies that represent completed work should behave like completed work â€” permanent, non-revisable facts in the world â€” which requires all three mechanisms (one-way valve, pricing floor, cooperative ownership) operating simultaneously to remove every depreciation vector.

title: Your Castle, Ready on Day One
canonical_topic: New Liana Banyan members receive a fully furnished Helm with twelve functional entry points to major platform systems on day one, eliminating configuration friction so any new member can begin meaningful cooperative activity within 15 minutes of joining.
definition: The Helm is every member's permanent personal command center, pre-built with twelve functional doors to major platform systems (Cue Card, Bridges, Content Library, Guild memberships, Tribe connections, Marketplace, Subscriptions, Treasure Map Builder, ADAPT Score dashboard, Housing, Lemon Lot, Settings) from the moment they join.
founder_voice_quotes: You do not arrive at Liana Banyan and build from scratch. You arrive and your castle is already standing.
Friction kills participation.
A new member who joins at 9:00 AM can be browsing the Marketplace, joining a Guild, and building their first Treasure Map by 9:15.
All they had to do was choose which door to open first.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Twelve functional Helm doors ready on day one; no configuration required before first useful action.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Door 1: Cue Card (auto-updating portable reputation). Door 2: Bridges (project control panels). Door 3: Content Library (owned content, 83.3% earnings). Door 4: Guild memberships (professional communities). Door 5: Tribe connections (personal communities). Door 6: Marketplace. Door 7: Subscriptions. Door 8: Treasure Map Builder. Door 9: ADAPT Score dashboard. Door 10: Housing module. Door 11: Lemon Lot (cooperative vehicle marketplace). Door 12: Settings. Six Cold Start Pathways handle different member types.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Friction kills participation is the architectural mandate behind Helm. Traditional platform onboarding requires the new user to construct their environment before they can do anything useful â€” most leave before the construction is done. Helm inverts the model: construction is completed before the member arrives. The twelve doors are not aspirational placeholders but functional entry points to live systems. The castle metaphor is architecturally precise: the member is a feudal lord arriving to an estate that has been prepared, not a homesteader arriving to an empty plot.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Helm's deepest architectural insight is that platform value must be accessible immediately â€” the window between joining and first value experience is measured in minutes, not hours, because cooperative participation requires experiencing the cooperative as already alive rather than as something the new member must build from nothing.

title: The Board Game Lobby
canonical_topic: The Board Game Lobby is a threshold-based cooperative team formation system where members post project seeds, the platform matches Cue Card skills across Guilds and Tribes, and a Bridge auto-creates when the team threshold is reached, eliminating the coordination tax.
definition: Members post project seeds describing what to build, skills needed, team size, and launch threshold. The platform matches seeds against Cue Card skills and Guild/Tribe memberships by consent â€” matched members see seeds in their feeds and join voluntarily. When threshold is met, a Bridge with a Treasure Map scaffold auto-creates.
founder_voice_quotes: Pin your card and wait for the ding.
The lobby eliminates the coordination tax.
No cold calls. No awkward emails. No social media posts begging for volunteers.
She pinned a card to the lobby board. The lobby did the rest.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Post a project seed, set threshold; platform assembles team from Cue Card skills; Bridge auto-builds when full.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Project seed specifies skills needed, team size, launch threshold. Platform actively matches against Cue Cards in relevant Guilds and Tribes. Matched members see seed in feed â€” they decide whether to join (consent, not assignment). Threshold met: Bridge creates, Treasure Map scaffolded. No cold messaging, no coordination overhead. Lobby gets smarter: completed projects feed back into Cue Card, ADAPT Timeliness surfaces more in time-sensitive seeds.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The coordination tax is the invisible cost that kills most cooperative projects before they start: organizing a neighborhood garden requires three social media posts, phone collection, a meeting, role assignments, and follow-up with the maybes. By the time organizing is done, the energy is gone. The Board Game Lobby reduces the coordination tax to posting one seed and waiting. Threshold mechanics prevent phantom participation (people who say yes and disappear) by requiring explicit join actions. Consent flows all the way down â€” no one is assigned, no one is pressured. The lobby's neutrality (no penalty for browsing) makes it safe for introverts and deliberate decision-makers.

wading_diving_in_layer: The threshold system's deepest insight: cooperative projects fail not from lack of interest but from lack of coordination infrastructure â€” a credible, visible threshold converts diffuse interest into simultaneous commitment without anyone having to cold-message strangers.

title: The Red Queen
canonical_topic: The Red Queen is each member's personal AI manager inside their Helm, distinct from Star Chamber governance AI and MoneyPenny operational AI, providing context-aware management advice from full dashboard access while requiring As You Wish confirmation for every action.
definition: The Red Queen is a per-member AI manager with access to the member's Cue Card, active Bridges, Guild/Tribe memberships, ADAPT Score history, and full platform knowledge base. She advises, the member confirms via As You Wish. She shares no context across members, is not a retraining model, and cannot spend Credits, join projects, or send messages without explicit confirmation.
composing_primitives: Three AI Tier Architecture (Star Chamber, MoneyPenny, Red Queen)
founder_voice_quotes: The Star Chamber governs. MoneyPenny coordinates. The Red Queen is yours.
She advises. You confirm. Nothing moves without 'As You Wish.'
The most powerful piece on the board â€” and she works for you.
She is yours the way a good executive assistant is yours â€” loyal to your agenda, familiar with your patterns, silent about your business.
cross_references: Chess queen metaphor (most mobile piece, serves the king)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Personal AI manager per member with full dashboard access; every action requires As You Wish confirmation.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three AI tiers: Star Chamber (constitutional governance, members almost never interact directly), MoneyPenny (operational coordination between AI agents, members never interact), Red Queen (personal manager, member's primary AI interface). Red Queen sees entire dashboard: Bridges, milestones, teammate uploads, ADAPT score trends, matching seeds. Advises on priority. Translates platform policy changes to plain language. Over time uses accumulated context (not ML retraining) for better-timed suggestions. Cannot act autonomously.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The three-tier AI architecture solves a governance clarity problem: what should an AI know, what should it be able to do, and who should it serve? Star Chamber knows everything but acts only on constitutional questions. MoneyPenny coordinates platform machinery but is invisible to members. Red Queen knows everything about one member and serves only that member's agenda. Per-member context isolation (no shared context between Red Queens) means no member's data trains another's experience. The hard constraint â€” no autonomous action without As You Wish â€” is the cooperative principle applied to AI: the technology advises, the member decides.

wading_diving_in_layer: The per-member context isolation and the As You Wish confirmation requirement together constitute the cooperative principle applied to AI â€” technology that advises without commanding is the only AI design compatible with cooperative member sovereignty.

title: Portal Doors
canonical_topic: Portal Doors are the Cue Card-checked connection mechanism between Liana Banyan communities that enables portable reputation to carry across Guilds, Tribes, and all nine portal surfaces without reputation resets, with community-defined requirements shown as pathways rather than walls.
definition: Portal Doors check a member's Cue Card against community-defined requirements (ADAPT dimensions, completed project counts, certifications) to enable or explain access. When requirements are not met, the door shows what is needed, not just that access is denied. Reputation is bidirectional â€” work completed through any door updates the same Cue Card.
founder_voice_quotes: Liana Banyan is not islands. It is a continent with roads between every city. And those roads have doors.
Her Cue Card already did.
Not 'access denied' â€” 'here is what you need.'
One identity. Every door. No starting over.
cross_references: Nine portal surfaces (lianabanyan.com .biz .org .net the2ndsecond.com hexisle.com hexislo.com dss upekrithen)

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: One Cue Card works as a key across all communities; each door checks different dimensions; no reputation resets.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Nine portal surfaces share one identity. Community-defined requirements (ADAPT thresholds, completed project counts, certifications) set by democratic member vote, not administrator fiat. Requirements shown as pathways (two more completed projects, join intro Guild first) not binary walls. Lateral movement enabled: different communities check different Cue Card facets. Selective disclosure lets members choose which pages to show through each door.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The internet's default is reputation silos: LinkedIn professional history is invisible on Discord, Etsy shop reputation is worthless on Nextdoor. LB's Portal Doors architecture makes reputation a portable asset that compounds across every community the member touches. The requirement system is not arbitrary gatekeeping but community governance â€” standards are set by the members of each community through Star Chamber democratic processes. The multidimensional checking model (different doors check different ADAPT facets) enables lateral career movement: a chef's supply chain knowledge opens Supply Guild doors that have nothing to do with cooking credentials.

wading_diving_in_layer: The deepest insight is that portable, multidimensional reputation is not just convenient â€” it is the structural prerequisite for cooperative economics at scale, because trust cannot be rebuilt from scratch in every new context if members are to collaborate across the full breadth of their cooperative lives.

title: The Flywheel
canonical_topic: The cooperative flywheel is the self-reinforcing growth mechanism where every member action deposits energy into five or more platform systems simultaneously, creating compounding momentum that enriches participants rather than external owners.
definition: Every member action on LB stores energy in multiple systems at once: completed projects update Cue Cards, earn Credits, improve ADAPT Scores, increase Marketplace liquidity, and raise governance weight simultaneously. Content creates content creates content. The Cost+20% margin funds cooperative infrastructure, making the platform more useful, attracting more members, generating more transactions.
founder_voice_quotes: Every action stores energy for the next action. Projects create reputation. Reputation unlocks projects. Credits circulate. Cue Cards thicken.
The flywheel does not require coordination â€” it requires participation.
In extractive flywheels, momentum enriches owners. In cooperative flywheels, momentum enriches participants.
Nobody coordinated this growth. Nobody ran a growth-hacking campaign. The flywheel spun because each push stored energy for the next push.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every member action simultaneously deposits energy into five or more systems; momentum compounds and enriches participants, not owners.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Push one (joining a project): Cue Card shows project in progress, Guild sees activity, Tribe visibility increases, project credibility demonstrated, platform activity metrics rise â€” five energy deposits. Push two (project completes): Credits earned, Marketplace liquidity grows, governance weight increases, ADAPT Score deepens, Cue Card credibility thickens â€” five more deposits. Credits circulate to hire another member, who spends on food, who buys supplies â€” circular currency. Content flywheel: articles become Puddings, Puddings generate Spoonfuls, Spoonfuls link back to articles.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Amazon's flywheel and Uber's flywheel are the same mechanism: more customers, better prices, more supply, more customers. The structural difference is ownership destination â€” extractive flywheels channel momentum to shareholders and venture capitalists; cooperative flywheels channel 83.3% of every turn to the members doing the spinning. The five-dollar membership is not the revenue model â€” it is the first push. The flywheel generates operating revenue through Cost+20% on transactions, and that revenue funds infrastructure, which makes the platform more useful, which attracts more members, which generates more transactions.

wading_diving_in_layer: The cooperative flywheel is distinguished from extractive flywheels not by its mechanics (identical) but by its ownership structure â€” the same compounding momentum that made Amazon and Uber dominant, rerouted so that every turn enriches the members who spin the wheel rather than the investors who financed it.

title: The Labyrinth
canonical_topic: Gamified IDE overlay turning bug hunting into a dungeon crawl with Ariadne's Thread knowledge-transfer and Theseus Mode proximity detection.
definition: The Labyrinth is an optional gamified overlay for the platform code editor that visualizes codebases as explorable dungeon maps, recording the developer debugging path (Ariadne's Thread) and signaling proximity to the bug root cause (Theseus Mode). Every completed bug hunt produces a reusable knowledge artifact for future developers and integrates with the Cue Card reputation system, daily challenge Credits rewards, and Developer Guild leaderboards.
founder_voice_quotes: Bugs are monsters. The codebase is a labyrinth. Ariadne's Thread records your path so you cannot get lost and the next developer does not have to start over. Theseus Mode tells you when you are close. Find the Minotaur. Kill it. Document the hunt. The thread survives for whoever comes next.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The Labyrinth turns bug hunting into a dungeon crawl -- your debugging path becomes a reusable map.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Developers activate a visual overlay mapping files as rooms, functions as corridors. Ariadne's Thread records every investigative step; Theseus Mode activates within two files of the root cause. Completed threads become searchable artifacts for future developers.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Daily challenges earn Credits for specific debugging feats. Scoring weights difficulty by cyclomatic complexity and affected systems. Guild leaderboards are opt-in. Completed thread archives compound cooperative knowledge -- future developers inherit the full investigation, not just the fix. Overlay is always optional.

wading_diving_in_layer: Ariadne's Thread transforms individual debugging into cooperative institutional memory, making every bug hunt a research artifact that reduces the cognitive cost of all future similar hunts across the platform indefinitely.
empirical_receipts: 83.3% of Credits from bug fixes stay with developer

title: The Project Seed
canonical_topic: Six-stage project maturity system (Seed, Rock, Iron, Gold, Silver, Diamond) governing the cooperative project lifecycle from idea to replicable institutional infrastructure.
definition: The Project Seed system provides six named maturity stages: Seed (idea posted), Rock (team threshold met, Treasure Map drafted), Iron (active milestones, Credits flowing), Gold (deliverables accepted, compensation distributed), Silver (sustained operations), and Diamond (proven replicable infrastructure other communities can clone). Every stage is a valid resting place; ideas never expire; Diamond projects become clonable templates.
founder_voice_quotes: Seed: the idea. Rock: the team. Iron: the work. Gold: the delivery. Silver: the upkeep. Diamond: the institution. Six stages from shower thought to community infrastructure. Every stage is a valid resting place. Every Diamond started as a Seed someone was brave enough to post.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Six stages (Seed to Diamond) turn any cooperative idea into potentially replicable community infrastructure.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Seed is posted with skill requirements and team threshold. Rock means real people committed and a plan exists. Iron is active milestone work with Credits flowing. Gold distributes final compensation. Silver is sustained operations; Diamond is proven reproducible infrastructure.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Three simultaneous functions: shared vocabulary, matching-algorithm context (members filter lobby by stage), and cooperative data. Diamond gets founding member badges and template status. Gold/Silver distinction honors sustainability as a separate achievement from completion.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Gold/Silver/Diamond progression encodes a cooperative philosophy: Diamond template-cloning makes proven cooperative models the platform's highest-value IP, multiplying success across communities without repetition of effort.
empirical_receipts: Meal-prep Seed to Diamond in one year: 200 families served, cloned by two Tribes
Ideas never expire in the Board Game Lobby

title: Build Your Kingdom
canonical_topic: Business membership tier (Kingdom) providing companies branded storefronts, team management, volume pricing, and multi-portal presence in exchange for a 20% cooperative activity dedication.
definition: When a company joins Liana Banyan, it receives a Kingdom: branded Storefront, team management aggregating employee Cue Cards and ADAPT Scores into a collective profile, volume pricing through cooperative bulk purchasing, multi-surface presence across up to nine portal domains, and up to thirty active Bridges. The company dedicates 20% of platform activity to cooperative projects, earning full Credits and Marks, while operating under identical Cost+20%, 83.3% creator-keeps, and one-way valve rules as all individual members.
founder_voice_quotes: What companies do NOT get is extraction rights. A company on Liana Banyan cannot take more than it contributes. The Kingdom is a guest in the cooperative, not a lord over it.
Individuals are first-class citizens and companies are participants.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Companies join as Kingdoms: real tools, real presence, same cooperative rules as individuals -- plus a 20% dedication requirement.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Kingdom adds: branded Storefront, team management with collective ADAPT scoring, cooperative bulk purchasing discounts, presence across up to nine portal surfaces, and a dashboard rolling up thirty Bridges. The 20% dedication earns full Credits and Marks.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The Kingdom design inverts the usual marketplace hierarchy where companies are first-class. Volume pricing aggregates purchasing power to negotiate wholesale input costs. No company can use size to bypass Cost+20%, undercut individual members, or claim extraction rights. Star Chamber governance applies equally.

wading_diving_in_layer: Subjecting companies to identical cooperative rules is the mechanism preventing platform capture: a company's scale cannot translate into governance advantage or extraction rights, making the cooperative structurally resistant to the enclosure that eventually destroys most platform ecosystems.
empirical_receipts: Machine shop proof: 3 new client businesses, 12% steel purchasing discount, 30 active Bridges in first month

title: SCaaS
canonical_topic: Star Chamber as a Service -- an external API providing three-tier AI output validation against client-defined canonical data, replacing disclaimers with structural deterministic fact-checking.
definition: SCaaS is Liana Banyan's externalized AI validation product offering three service tiers: static validation against uploaded canonical datasets, dynamic validation connected to live data sources, and structural validation checking logical consistency and rule compliance. Each customer's canonical data is fully isolated and never trained on. SCaaS acts as a non-generative deterministic comparison layer between any AI and its audience, priced at Cost+20%.
founder_voice_quotes: SCaaS is not an AI. It is a validation layer that sits between an AI and its audience. It does not generate content. It does not predict. It does not hallucinate. It compares.
Liana Banyan built the brakes.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: SCaaS fact-checks AI outputs against your canonical data before they reach users -- structural brakes, not disclaimers.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three tiers: static canonical datasets, live dynamic sources, and structural rule/logic compliance. Privacy is structural -- data isolated per customer, never trained on.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: SCaaS externalizes the internal Star Chamber governance for any developer building AI applications. A deterministic comparison layer avoids the category error of using probabilistic systems to verify probabilistic outputs. Cost+20% pricing funds both the external API and internal Star Chamber development.

wading_diving_in_layer: Physically separating generation (AI) from validation (deterministic canonical comparison) eliminates the category error of using probabilistic systems to verify probabilistic outputs -- creating a structural guarantee at any scale, integrable in an afternoon.
empirical_receipts: Health startup: 50,000 daily queries, 3% pre-SCaaS inaccuracy drops to near-zero for canonical violations
100% canonical data isolation -- privacy structural not promissory

title: The Compensation Slider
canonical_topic: Per-contract mechanism allowing members to choose any cash-to-Credits ratio as compensation, from 0% to 100% at either end, set before work begins and irrevocable afterward.
definition: The Compensation Slider is a UI element presented at contract acceptance letting members drag a handle from 100% cash to 100% platform Credits, with any ratio in between. The ratio is locked before work begins, per-contract rather than per-member. Higher Credit ratios earn Marks faster (increasing governance weight), while 100% cash remains available without penalty for members with immediate dollar obligations.
founder_voice_quotes: Every contract comes with a slider. Drag left for cash. Drag right for Credits. Stop anywhere in between. Your financial reality is nuanced -- your compensation should be too.
Three contracts, three ratios, one week. The slider does not judge. It matches.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every contract has a slider: 100% cash to 100% Credits, or any mix -- your choice, per project.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Credits go further because Cost+20% eliminates middleman markup. A higher Credit ratio earns Marks faster. Cash remains at 100% for members with immediate dollar obligations -- no judgment. Ratio locks at contract acceptance to prevent post-work gaming.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The slider mediates between payer and worker currency preferences. Members typically migrate toward Credits over time as platform services become more valuable -- not due to system pressure but because the math makes sense. Effects compound across economic, governance, and surplus dimensions simultaneously.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Compensation Slider preserves individual economic sovereignty as a design principle: per-contract sliding choice matches the natural heterogeneity of human financial realities rather than forcing a single cooperative compensation model.
empirical_receipts: Three contracts in one week at 100% cash, 80% Credits, 100% Credits -- matching three distinct financial realities

title: The Chronicle Keeper: Game Masters Become Business Owners
canonical_topic: Platform system monetizing tabletop RPG game masters by logging sessions as cooperative service events and enabling a publication pipeline from campaign notes to commercial IP through the IP Ledger and Storefront.
definition: The Chronicle Keeper integrates the Calendar, Commerce Engine, IP Ledger, Storefront, and Design Pipeline into a unified workflow for tabletop game masters. Sessions are scheduled and logged as cooperative service events with tracked attribution. Campaign narratives (structured session summaries of scene beats, character actions, world changes, and unresolved threads) are compiled into publishable arcs through Guild-based role specialization with all contributors compensated under Cost+20% cooperative economics.
founder_voice_quotes: Nobody told Marcus he was building a business. He just kept showing up on Thursdays. The Chronicle Keeper did the rest.
A campaign that cost nothing to play has produced a commercial product. A hobby became a business became a published work.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The Chronicle Keeper turns Thursday-night game masters into published authors and business owners -- the platform logs sessions and pipelines them to publication.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Sessions are logged as cooperative service events. The Commerce Engine prices sessions at Cost+20%. Campaign notes flow into structured format that compilation tools organize into publishable arcs. All contributors tracked by IP Ledger.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Game masters produce narrative, intellectual property, and a loyal audience but previously without commercial infrastructure. The Chronicle Keeper creates a dual revenue stream: session fees and published works sold through the Storefront. All formats (novel, serial, lore guide, audio drama) follow the same capture-structure-compile-edit-publish-distribute sequence.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Chronicle Keeper makes visible and compensable a category of creative labor previously entirely outside the formal economy: by instrumenting sessions as ledgered service events and campaign notes as structured IP, it transforms ephemeral social experience into permanent, fairly attributed commercial IP.
empirical_receipts: 12-arc campaign archive across 3 years yields published serial with active Guild and revenue-generating Storefront
Session logging takes 10-15 minutes per session

title: Leave the Corners: Boaz Contribution Types
canonical_topic: Four-category contribution model (Campaign 10%, Product 5-15% sliding, Service 1:10, Knowledge always free) with three voluntary generosity tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold), operationalizing the Boaz principle from the book of Ruth.
definition: The Boaz Contribution Model defines four contribution types with distinct floor rates: Campaign contributions at 10% to the cooperative general fund, Product contributions at 5-15% sliding by platform involvement level, Service contributions at a 1:10 ratio of equivalent value hours, and Knowledge contributions always free. Three voluntary generosity tiers (Bronze default, Silver with reputation bonus, Gold with priority Recipe Pot matching) layer on top of these floor rates, all within the Cost+20% economic floor.
founder_voice_quotes: Not charity. Not philanthropy. Architecture. A system where generosity is visible, measurable, reputationally meaningful, and entirely voluntary -- but where the floor ensures that even the least generous participant still leaves enough for the system to sustain itself.
Boaz didn't leave the corners because he was wealthy. He left them because he understood that a field that feeds only the farmer eventually runs out of workers.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Four contribution types with defined floor rates and three voluntary generosity tiers -- leave the corners of your field so the system feeds everyone.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Campaign: 10% to cooperative fund. Product: 5-15% by platform involvement. Service: 1-in-10 hours. Knowledge: always free. Bronze is automatic; Silver earns reputation visibility; Gold earns priority project matching. Generosity tiers affect only the member's voluntary surplus above the floor.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Knowledge is free by deliberate design: gating knowledge creates information asymmetry that undermines cooperative governance -- every member needs to understand the system to vote on how it should change. The 1:10 service ratio ensures the cooperative infrastructure is continuously replenished. Gold's Recipe Pot priority rewards demonstrated generosity with opportunity, not extraction.

wading_diving_in_layer: Making Knowledge always free explicitly subordinates short-term platform revenue from educational content to the long-term democratic health of cooperative governance, treating informed participation as a prerequisite rather than a premium product.
empirical_receipts: Woodworker proof: Silver bump immediately increases Recipe Pot visibility; Gold bump attracts restaurant chain and wedding planner contracts

title: From Campaign to Novel
canonical_topic: Pipeline converting tabletop RPG session logs into publishable fiction through structured session capture, compilation tool arc identification, Guild-based editorial specialization, and Cost+20% Storefront publication.
definition: The campaign-to-publication pipeline integrates Calendar (session scheduling), structured session capture templates (scene beats, character actions, world changes, unresolved threads), compilation tools (arc identification and chapter assembly), the IP Ledger (multi-contributor attribution), the Design Pipeline (illustration), and the Storefront (Cost+20% publication). Format is flexible: novel, serial, illustrated lore guide, or audio drama -- each following the same capture-structure-compile-edit-publish-distribute sequence.
founder_voice_quotes: The binder in the closet was always a book. It just needed a system that could see it.
You didn't write a novel. You played one. The Chronicle Keeper helped you realize it.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Your campaign binder is already a novel -- the platform structures it, identifies the arcs, and takes it to publication with all contributors credited.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Each session takes 10-15 minutes to log. Across 40-100 sessions character actions accumulate into arcs. The platform's compilation tools identify story arcs and assemble chapters. A Chronicle Keeper Guild specializes roles -- all receiving attributed shares tracked by the IP Ledger.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Session summaries are scene directives containing world-building, character motivations, and plot resolutions -- reducing editorial distance to the prose conversion layer. A Skipping Stone (single chapter summary) can hook readers who have never played tabletop games, creating new audience pathways through story discovery.

wading_diving_in_layer: The campaign-to-novel pipeline transforms ephemeral social experience into permanent, fairly attributed commercial IP: by instrumenting the act of play, it converts collective memory into structured artifacts without changing the nature of the play itself.
empirical_receipts: 104-session campaign compiled into 4 arcs, first arc published as novella with 40%/20%/20%/20% attribution split

title: The Montana Principle: Would You Accept Your Own Deal?
canonical_topic: Two-part design constraint requiring every platform feature to pass a reversal test (designer accepts the deal from the other side) and a newspaper test (deal survives public scrutiny unqualified), enforced by the Founder's participation as a standard member.
definition: The Montana Principle is a dual ethical filter -- derived from the Founder's experience as a 13-year-old newspaper carrier absorbing customer payment risk -- applied to every platform feature, fee structure, contribution rate, and governance rule. Reversal test: would the person offering the deal accept it from the other side? Newspaper test: would the deal survive public scrutiny without qualification? Both tests are enforced by the Founder participating as a standard member subject to all platform rules.
founder_voice_quotes: No deal on this platform can exist if the person offering it wouldn't accept it from the other side.
This is not altruism. It is engineering.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Every platform deal must pass two tests: would the designer accept it from the other side, and would it look good on a newspaper front page?
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The Montana Principle eliminates asymmetric deals by making reversal a design constraint. The Founder participates as a standard member -- same Cost+20% rules, same contribution rates, same currency restrictions, same governance thresholds.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: At the transaction level: every fee disclosed before commitment. At governance: every rule applies to the Founder. At contribution: Boaz rates designed by asking what rate the Founder would accept if someone else set them. At currency: Credits never cash out -- including for the Founder.

wading_diving_in_layer: Making ethical asymmetry structurally impossible rather than merely discouraged: by requiring the platform's designer to personally live under every rule he designs, it eliminates information asymmetry that enables extraction before it can be designed in.
empirical_receipts: Founder's Storefront listings follow identical Cost+20% rules
Content Shield moderation applies to Founder's content
Marks accumulate under same one-year redemption window for Founder

title: The Birthright: Your Marks Become Benefits
canonical_topic: One-year maturation threshold for accumulated Marks converting effort-differential currency into platform service access, cooperative surplus allocation weight, governance participation eligibility, and tier progression benefits.
definition: The Birthright is the twelve-month maturation threshold after which accumulated Marks become redeemable for four categories of platform benefit: premium service access (extended Storefront features, priority Recipe Pot matching, advanced analytics), cooperative surplus allocation weighting, governance weight in Star Chamber votes, and tier progression. Marks never convert to fiat currency (one-way valve). The one-year window prevents gaming, aligns with fiscal cycle, and builds culture of sustained generosity.
founder_voice_quotes: That's the Birthright. You built it. Now it builds you back.
Marks are a ledger of generosity.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: After one year, accumulated Marks unlock real platform benefits -- not cash, but the tools you need to grow.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Marks represent effort-differential above minimum required contribution. After twelve months they become redeemable for: premium service access, cooperative surplus allocation weight, governance vote weight, and tier-status progression. Marks do not expire after maturation.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Three purposes of the one-year window: prevents gaming (single generous transaction produces negligible Marks), aligns with cooperative annual surplus calculation cycle, builds a culture of sustained rather than transactional generosity. The benefit catalog is functional rather than symbolic.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Birthright reframes cooperative generosity from charitable giving to strategic compounding: Marks earned through above-floor participation become instruments of future capability growth, making generosity economically self-reinforcing rather than self-sacrificing.
empirical_receipts: Carpenter proof: Marks redeemed for Recipe Pot priority yield housing cooperative contract for 12 units; analytics reveal most popular product; governance weight timed to manufacturing policy vote

title: Daily Mazes: Sharpen Your Skills, Earn Your Credits
canonical_topic: Daily algorithmically-generated skill puzzles mapped to the Spice Rack's ten-domain taxonomy with a ten-level difficulty progression, feeding Credits rewards, ADAPT Score trajectory, and Guild-level competency benchmarking.
definition: Daily Mazes are shared-seed algorithmic puzzles generated each morning, advancing from Level 1 foundational to Level 10 impossibility-detection challenges where the correct answer is 'this cannot be done, and here is why.' Integration points include Credit rewards from the cooperative general fund (top 10% of solvers), ADAPT Score contribution tracking skill trajectory, Guild benchmarking for organizational competency diagnostics, and Guild Maze Runs -- collaborative multi-domain challenges mirroring the Recipe Pot skill-matching model.
founder_voice_quotes: Every morning at six o'clock, a new maze appears.
Knowing when a problem has no solution is the most advanced skill of all.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Daily puzzles across ten skill domains, six minutes a morning, compounding into real ADAPT Score trajectory and Credits.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: All members at the same difficulty level share the same seed-generated maze each day. Level 10 mazes are unsolvable by design -- detecting impossibility is the test. Credits reward the top 10% of daily solvers, funded from the cooperative general fund.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Daily Mazes bridge the gap between skill declaration (Spice Rack) and demonstrated skill (ADAPT Score) through active problem-solving. Guild Maze Runs replicate the Recipe Pot model in miniature -- the maze declares its recipe, the Guild assigns members by spice strength, and collaboration solves what no individual could.

wading_diving_in_layer: Level 10's impossibility-detection design is the system's deepest contribution: by making 'this problem has no solution' a correct and rewarded answer, it teaches that the most advanced professional skill is recognizing when problem-solving is the wrong response -- a judgment no certification can capture.
empirical_receipts: New member proof: starts Level 1 Garlic, discovers Level 1 Cinnamon requires three attempts, Guild co-solving brings her to Level 3 in 3 months enabling cross-domain Recipe Pot matching

title: Your Island, Your Rules: Four Ownership Models
canonical_topic: Four HexIsle governance models (Solo, Guild, Project-Sponsored, Rogue) with distinct decision-making structures, all constrained by Cost+20% pricing and Boaz contribution floors as non-negotiable minimums.
definition: HexIsle has four ownership models: Solo (single owner, total control within cooperative rules), Guild (collective professional governance with graduated voting thresholds scaled to decision severity: simple majority, 67% supermajority, unanimity), Project-Sponsored (temporary governance tied to a specific initiative, dissolving cleanly with permanent IP Ledger preservation), and Rogue (custom experimental governance with transparency badge, subject only to Cost+20% and Boaz floor). Successful Rogue governance patterns can be incorporated into standard models through Star Chamber vote.
founder_voice_quotes: Your island. Your rules. Four models. Choose.

Rogue islands are the platform's laboratory. The governance innovations that emerge from Rogue experimentation can be studied, documented, and -- if they prove effective -- incorporated into the platform's standard models.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Your HexIsle has four governance models -- Solo, Guild, Project-Sponsored, or Rogue -- each with distinct rules and the same cooperative floor.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Solo is single-owner total control. Guild uses graduated voting thresholds. Project-Sponsored islands exist for a project's duration then dissolve with full IP Ledger preservation. Rogue allows fully custom governance under a transparency badge.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Guild governance's scaled voting thresholds calibrate to decision severity. Project-Sponsored islands solve overhead for time-limited efforts. Rogue islands carry visible badges for member choice. The Star Chamber can vote to formally adopt successful Rogue patterns as new standard models.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Rogue governance model as a formal laboratory -- with results eligible for elevation through democratic vote -- embeds a mechanism for cooperative constitutional evolution, making governance itself a cooperative product that improves through participation.
empirical_receipts: Guild island proof: 3 woodworkers and designer form Guild, spin up Project-Sponsored island for restaurant contract, dissolve cleanly, core Guild continues unchanged

title: The 20% Rule: Why Sharing Makes You Richer
canonical_topic: Economic argument that Cost+20% cooperative margin generates more total member income than a solo 100% model through three compounding layers: volume multiplication, purchasing power aggregation, and demand routing flywheel.
definition: The Cost+20% model allocates 20% of every transaction to the cooperative's shared infrastructure (Commerce Engine, Content Shield, Star Chamber, Calendar, Recipe Pot, ADAPT Score, IP Ledger, Beacon) funded without venture capital or debt. Three economic layers compound member benefit: marketplace visibility and Guild referrals multiply volume, cooperative bulk purchasing reduces input costs and improves margins, and the demand routing algorithm favors dedicated members in a self-reinforcing flywheel of visibility, demand, and revenue.
founder_voice_quotes: Twenty percent is not a fee. It's a company.
Eighty percent of thirty is more than a hundred percent of ten. The twenty percent that felt like a cost was actually a volume multiplier.
The twenty percent is the grease.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The 20% cooperative margin buys a volume multiplier that makes 80% of more worth more than 100% of less.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Layer one: marketplace visibility and Guild referrals multiply sales volume. Layer two: cooperative bulk purchasing lowers input costs improving margins even after the 20% take. Layer three: the routing algorithm favors dedicated members creating a flywheel of visibility-demand-revenue-dedication.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The 20% funds the Commerce Engine, Content Shield, Star Chamber, Calendar, Recipe Pot, ADAPT Score, IP Ledger, and Beacon with no venture capital. Baker proof: 12 to 22 cakes/month by month 3, falling cost basis from cooperative flour purchasing, hired assistant who joins as member.

wading_diving_in_layer: The 20% Rule's deepest insight: the cooperative margin is not a tax on member revenue but an investment in collective infrastructure that creates member revenue -- making the member's contribution to shared capacity the mechanism by which shared capacity increases individual capacity in a compounding loop.
empirical_receipts: Baker proof: 12 to 22 cakes/month by month 3, falling cost basis from cooperative flour purchasing, hired assistant by month 6

title: The Drink Cookbook: How One Old Book Started All This
canonical_topic: Founder origin narrative connecting an 1800s drink cookbook copied from a library to the platform's architectural principle of organizing scattered inputs into amplifying systems -- the compilation instinct as platform DNA.
definition: The Drink Cookbook origin story traces the Founder's compilation instinct -- first expressed by copying recipes from an obscure 1800s book -- to the platform's foundational architecture: the Librarian MCP (25 tools, 15 index files), the Stitchpunk Corps (9 automated content pipeline scripts), and cross-referencing infrastructure connecting 2,267 innovations into a coherent system. The food metaphor chain (Stone Soup, Bread, Pudding, Spoonfuls, Spice Rack, Recipe Pot) traces directly to this compilation instinct. The platform was built forward from accumulated artifacts, not backward from projected business plans.
founder_voice_quotes: Liana Banyan is a cookbook. Not for drinks. For economic structures.
The cookbook came first. The kitchen was built to fit the recipes.
Innovation #47 connects to Innovation #1200 connects to Innovation #2100 the same way a base spirit connects to a sweetener connects to a garnish. None of them is the drink. All of them together are.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The platform's architecture traces to a man copying recipes from an 1800s library book -- the compilation instinct became platform DNA.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: The food metaphor chain traces directly to the Founder's first instinct: organize it, preserve it, make it findable. The Librarian MCP and Stitchpunk Corps are mechanical expressions of this instinct, ensuring nothing useful stays buried.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The compilation instinct explains why one founder without venture funding could produce 2,267 cataloged innovations: not inventing from scratch but recognizing patterns in accumulated observations and cross-referencing them into amplifying systems. Every platform system compiles: Commerce Engine (transactions), IP Ledger (contributions), Star Chamber (votes), Beacon (engagement).

wading_diving_in_layer: The Drink Cookbook origin reveals that the platform's deepest architectural principle -- compilation as a primary mode of value creation -- was not discovered through strategic planning but through decades of personal practice proving itself across thousands of observations before the platform was designed.
empirical_receipts: Home brewer compilation proof: historical recipes compiled to Storefront, home brewing Guild forms, collaborative published results, original compiler credited as lead contributor

title: Wave Pricing: The Impatience Tax That Funds Everything
canonical_topic: Multi-tier sequential pricing system that converts early-adopter urgency premiums above the Cost+20% floor into production funding, eliminating need for external capital while ensuring patient buyers receive the lowest price at Wave 3.
definition: Wave Pricing structures product launches in defined waves with decreasing premium above Cost+20%, increasing quantities, and increasing information availability from prior wave feedback. Wave 1 (highest price, smallest quantity) funds tooling and initial production; Wave 2 (moderate premium, medium quantity) expands inventory with Wave 1 feedback incorporated; Wave 3 (Cost+20% floor, full quantity) delivers the tested refined product at lowest price. Wave 1 buyers form automatic feedback cohorts earning Marks for detailed testing reports, creating a quality improvement loop between waves.
founder_voice_quotes: The impatient fund the patient. The patient get the best deal. Everyone eats.

Wave Pricing is not a Kickstarter model. Kickstarter says: give us money and we'll try to build it. Wave Pricing says: we're building it -- the question is when you want yours and how much the timing is worth to you.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: Wave Pricing turns early-adopter impatience into production capital -- Wave 1 funds the tooling, Wave 3 gets the lowest price.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Products must have verified cost bases before Wave 1 opens. Wave 1 buyers pay a premium (typically 50% above Cost+20% floor) and form a feedback cohort earning Marks for testing reports. Their feedback is incorporated before Wave 2 ships. Wave 3 buyers get the tested, refined product at the floor price.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Wave Pricing is categorically distinct from crowdfunding: products must be manufacturing-ready with known COGS before any wave opens. Wave 1 buyers become community -- their weighted reviews and Marks accumulation create reputational incentives for early participation beyond price premium. No loans, no dilution, no outside capital.

wading_diving_in_layer: Wave Pricing converts the economic friction of impatience into production capital that enables the lowest possible price for the patient majority -- aligning commercial incentives with cooperative values by making early adopters the mechanism of broad access rather than its obstacle.
empirical_receipts: Leatherworker tool belt: Wave 1 (40 units at $63, 50% premium) sells out in 3 days funding tooling; Wave 2 (100 units at $52) incorporates pocket modification; Wave 3 (300 units at $42 floor)

title: BandWagon: Trust Your Taste
canonical_topic: Taste-prediction authority system scoring member judgment accuracy via hit rate, discovery score, and diversity coefficient, converting demonstrated curatorial ability into allocation budgets for directing cooperative resources.
definition: BandWagon converts demonstrated campaign-backing judgment into allocation budgets for directing cooperative resources. Scoring combines hit rate (prediction accuracy), discovery score (early-backing multiplier), and diversity coefficient (cross-domain accuracy). Allocation budgets scale with score but are ceiling-capped to prevent taste concentration. TasteMaker integration elevates content recommendations from high-scoring members in their demonstrated domains. The ceiling cap creates mentorship incentives: spreading taste compounds allocation power more than hoarding it.
founder_voice_quotes: Trust your taste. Then teach it.
The taste you demonstrate is the compounding asset.
Good taste compounds. The better your recommendations, the more visible your future recommendations become, and the more responsible you are for what succeeds next.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: BandWagon turns your track record of picking winning campaigns into the authority to direct more cooperative resources toward future winners.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Three scoring components multiply: hit rate (did backed projects succeed?), discovery score (early backing multiplier before crowd validation), and diversity coefficient (cross-domain accuracy). Allocation budgets grow with score but are ceiling-capped so no single member controls cooperative allocation.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: The ceiling cap creates the system's most elegant incentive: a member at maximum allocation can only grow collective impact by teaching others to identify quality. TasteMaker integration makes high-scoring members' content recommendations more visible within their demonstrated domains. Feedback loop: recommended content generates Beacon engagement, which validates the recommendation, which strengthens the BandWagon score.

wading_diving_in_layer: BandWagon's ceiling-cap-plus-mentorship-incentive converts the cooperative resource-direction mechanism into a distributed network of developed curatorial intelligence rather than a concentrated prediction machine -- making collective taste the emergent property of individual judgment shared rather than hoarded.
empirical_receipts: Ray proof: 14 projects backed, 11 succeed (79% hit rate), 9 successful picks backed before 20 supporters, allocation budget grows to 3x default
Guild reaches highest average BandWagon score through taste teaching

title: The Shop That Fixed My Son's Car
canonical_topic: Founder-voice anecdote demonstrating the four-currency system (Credits, Marks, Joules, Backed Marks) through a nine-month IT/vehicle-repair exchange with D&M Service Center in Universal City, Texas, proving cooperative economics already run in ordinary human life.
definition: Written by Jonathan Jones (Founder), this essay uses a real nine-month IT infrastructure buildout for D&M Service Center (Universal City, Texas) exchanged for family vehicle maintenance to illustrate the four-currency system: Credits (cost-equivalent cash for tow and parts), Marks (labor discount and priority turnaround as effort-differential above cost), Joules (nine months of IT work banked as surplus effort), and Backed Marks (the shop fronting everything on a phone call because the Joule reservoir was visible). Cites WIR Bank (1934), Sardex (2009), Radford P.O.W. Camp economy (1945), and Amish barn-raising as historical precedents.
founder_voice_quotes: We didn't invent this. We just named the pieces and built a system where you can have this kind of relationship with people you've never met.
The only thing Liana Banyan adds is the ability to have this relationship with a stranger.

My son drove his own car home today because I wired a network nine months ago. That's not a metaphor. That's a four-currency transaction settled on a handshake, closed on the exact day this essay was written.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: A real IT-for-car-maintenance exchange with a Texas auto shop proves the four-currency system already runs in ordinary human life -- it just lacked a name.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Credits cover cost-equivalent transactions (tow, parts). Marks are the differential -- labor discount, priority turnaround, same-day delivery. Joules are the nine months of IT work banked ahead of need. Backed Marks are the shop fronting everything on a phone call because the Joule reservoir was visible. Time-patient settlement: Marks don't have to clear today.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Historical precedents demonstrate none of this is fringe: WIR Bank (92-year run, billions in annual volume), Sardex (launched during European debt crisis, still growing), Radford P.O.W. economy (spontaneous currency in weeks), Amish barn-raising. The platform's contribution is scale: making reservoir-backed cooperative relationships available between strangers.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Shop That Fixed My Son's Car is the platform's most important empirical anchor: by reducing the four-currency system to a single real transaction on a specific day with a named business, it converts abstract cooperative theory into falsifiable lived experience -- placing the innovation not in the invention of cooperative economics but in the engineering of access to them at stranger-scale.
empirical_receipts: D&M Service Center Universal City TX -- real named business
Son's fuel pump replaced same day essay was written
Nine months IT buildout: computers, network segmentation, VoIP, security cameras, diagnostic software
WIR Bank running since 1934 with billions in annual volume

title: The Triple Double and the Lottery Ticket Monkeys
canonical_topic: Founder-voice motivational framework pairing the Triple Double ($100/day doubled three times to $192,000/year) with Swing for the Fences (attempts volume over precision) and No Effort Is Wasted, contrasted with lottery-ticket thinking as a personal financial strategy.
definition: Written by Jonathan Jones (Founder), this essay presents three compounding motivational tools: the Triple Double ($100/day base doubled three times to $192,000/year capability rung, with 48 working weeks and five days built in); Swing for the Fences (Babe Ruth's 714 home runs alongside 1,330 strikeouts as the cost of willingness to swing); and No Effort Is Wasted (Calvin's-dad principle: missed throws purchase learning). Contrasted against $300/year average lottery spending at 1-in-300-million odds. Liana Banyan's Cold Start pathway is presented as the tool letting members validate ideas before committing capital.
founder_voice_quotes: Who motivates the motivator? The motivator does.
Hit the Triple Double. Swing for the Fences. No effort is wasted.
You don't need to hit every throw. You need to keep throwing. The ladder takes care of the rest.
cross_references: Pudding 182 The Shop That Fixed My Sons Car

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: $100/day doubled three times gets you to $192,000/year; swing hard enough and often enough; no effort is wasted.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Triple Double: Rung 0 $24K, Rung 1 $48K, Rung 2 $96K, Rung 3 $192K. Doubles rather than additions because each rung is tractable -- do what you already did, and then do it again. Babe Ruth struck out 1,330 times earning 714 home runs. Calvin's dad: when you don't get what you want, you get experience.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Lottery: $300/year average at 1-in-300-million odds vs. compound effort in a system built to make first attempts survivable. Cold Start pathway lets members validate ideas in Marks (effort only) before committing capital. The Helm attempts counter counts throws, not wins, labeled No Effort Is Wasted.

wading_diving_in_layer: The Triple Double reframes ambition from destination-thinking to trajectory-thinking: by making each rung a tractable extension of the previous rather than a categorical leap, it converts paralyzing goals into manageable Monday-morning problems -- and the Cold Start pathway is the structural expression of that reframing in platform architecture.
empirical_receipts: Babe Ruth: 714 home runs, 1,330 strikeouts (Baseball Reference)
Average American: $300-$412/year lottery spending, 1 in ~292M Powerball odds

title: Going First (Opt-In Rewrite, B098)
canonical_topic: Founder-First Anecdote Mapping as an opt-in-with-benefits program: Founder documents all 2,248 innovations before inviting members to contribute their own origin stories, with five concrete benefits; full membership preserved for non-participants. Final draft superseding B097 mandatory framing.
definition: Written by Jonathan Jones (Founder), Draft 2.0 corrected from mandatory to opt-in framing per Founder B097. The Founder commits to documenting the specific anecdote and photograph behind each of the 2,248 innovations before the first member opt-in flow is published. Five opt-in benefits: named attribution, co-authorship on platform derivatives, Service Allocation Authority accrual, research eligibility as named case study subjects, and durable cooperative memory preservation. Full membership without asterisk is explicitly guaranteed for non-participants. Epistemological frame: documented innovations become preliminary hypothesis documents with live empirical tests attached.
founder_voice_quotes: I will not ask a single member of this platform to document the origin of their own contribution until I have finished documenting the origin of all 2,248 of mine.
The point of going first is not to make documentation universal. The point of going first is to make documentation available.
I am going first. I will finish all 2,248 before I publish the sign-up flow for the first member to go second.

skipping_stones_layers.at_a_glance: The Founder documents all 2,248 innovation origin stories first -- photographed and published -- before any member is invited (never required) to contribute theirs.
skipping_stones_layers.more_details: Five opt-in benefits: named attribution, co-authorship on derivatives, Service Allocation Authority accrual, research eligibility, and durable cooperative memory. Non-participants receive identical full membership. The distinction between making documentation universal and making documentation available defines the enterprise as cooperative rather than compliance-oriented.
skipping_stones_layers.in_depth: Three reasons for going first: pedagogical (2,248 examples exist before any member is asked), epistemological (documented anecdotes turn papers into preliminary hypothesis documents with live empirical tests attached), and moral (Founder doesn't ask members to be vulnerable in ways he is unwilling to be). Consent is granular: opt in per contribution, withdraw from research eligibility, change mind later.

wading_diving_in_layer: The epistemological reframing from founder whitepaper to preliminary hypothesis with live empirical test positions the entire innovation corpus as empirically verifiable primary data rather than promotional narrative, making the platform a legitimate object of academic research.
empirical_receipts: 18 open physical journals visible in Founder's Office One-Take Unedited video
2,248 innovations tracked across sixteen active initiatives
Innovation 2246 Living Laboratory as research ethics anchor

title: Going First: Founder-First Anecdote Mapping (Pawn B57 Draft)
canonical_topic: Original mandatory-framing Pawn B57 draft of the Founder-First Anecdote Mapping concept with partial Research Appendix (Studs Terkel, StoryCorps, Ostrom, Garage Myth), superseded by B098 opt-in rewrite. Variant: anecdote-mapping version with incomplete appendix.
definition: Pawn B57 skeleton of Pudding #184, drafted by Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking and pollinated in B097, using mandatory framing ('must be mapped', 'requirement') later corrected by Founder. Contains ten sections: platform-becomes-museum problem, Garage Myth (Wozniak 2014), Studs Terkel's Working as oral-history-as-infrastructure, Elinor Ostrom's go-to-the-thing-itself methodology, and the photograph as commitment device. Research Appendix partially complete (A1 Studs Terkel only; A2 StoryCorps, B1 Ostrom, C1 Garage Myth truncated). Superseded by B098 opt-in rewrite. MacKenzie Scott positioning line included.
